nodes.en: The formal science of number, quantity, structure, space, and change, developed through abstraction and rigorous deductive proof. Sibling to Computer Science, Logic, Information Theory, and Systems Theory; it groups pure mathematics (theory for its own sake), applied mathematics, and computational mathematics.
nodes.cs: Formální věda o čísle, kvantitě, struktuře, prostoru a změně, budovaná abstrakcí a přísným deduktivním důkazem. Sourozenec informatiky, logiky, teorie informace a teorie systémů; sdružuje čistou matematiku (teorii samu o sobě), aplikovanou matematiku a výpočetní matematiku.
nodes.brief:
Mathematics is the formal science of number, quantity, structure, space and change, developed through abstraction and rigorous deductive proof rather than empirical observation. A mathematical result, once proved, holds independently of any physical measurement — which is what distinguishes mathematics from the natural sciences it so often serves.

Mathematics sits beside [[02.02]] within the Formal Sciences: computer science studies effective, mechanizable processes, while mathematics studies the abstract structures those processes manipulate, and the two share deep common ground in the theory of algorithms and computability. Internally mathematics splits by motive: [[02.01.01]] pursues structure and proof for their own sake, without regard to application, while [[02.01.02]] takes mathematical tools and points them at problems from physics, engineering and beyond. Both draw on [[02.03]] for the formal notion of proof itself — a valid mathematical argument is, at bottom, a chain of logical inference.

Mathematics also supplies the language in which [[01.01]] states its laws: calculus, differential equations and linear algebra are as much physics's working vocabulary as mathematics's own subject matter, and each field has repeatedly driven the other forward. For further reading, see the [MacTutor History of Mathematics archive](https://mathshistory.st-andrews.ac.uk/) and the [Encyclopaedia Britannica entry on mathematics](https://www.britannica.com/science/mathematics).

nodes.en: Mathematics pursued for its intrinsic structure and logical beauty rather than external application — algebra, analysis, geometry, topology, and number theory, developed through definition, theorem, and proof. Distinct from Applied Mathematics (real-world problems) and Computational Mathematics (algorithmic computation).
nodes.cs: Matematika pěstovaná pro svou vnitřní strukturu a logickou krásu, nikoli pro vnější použití — algebra, analýza, geometrie, topologie a teorie čísel, budovaná definicí, větou a důkazem. Odlišná od aplikované matematiky (reálné problémy) a výpočetní matematiky (algoritmické počítání).
nodes.brief:
Pure Mathematics pursues mathematical structure for its own intrinsic interest rather than for any external application: algebra studies structures built from operations and their symmetries, analysis studies limits, continuity, and change, geometry studies shape and space, topology studies properties preserved under continuous deformation, and number theory studies the integers and their patterns. Its method is definition, theorem, and proof — a result, once established, holds with a certainty no experiment can match.

Its branches are not walled off from each other: [[02.01.01.01]] Algebra supplies the structural machinery that [[02.01.01.05]] Number Theory uses to study the integers, and [[02.01.01.03]] Geometry and [[02.01.01.04]] Topology are inseparable in much of contemporary research, the one studying shape with distances and angles, the other studying which of those shapes remain equivalent under continuous deformation. Pure mathematics is distinguished from its sibling [[02.01.02]] Applied Mathematics by orientation rather than subject matter — the same calculus or algebra can serve either aim, depending on whether the question is asked for its own sake or in service of a problem outside mathematics.

For further reading, see the [Encyclopedia of Mathematics](https://encyclopediaofmath.org/) and the [Clay Mathematics Institute](https://www.claymath.org/), whose Millennium Prize Problems mark some of pure mathematics's deepest open questions.

nodes.en: The branch of pure mathematics concerned with symbols and the rules for manipulating them — equations, structures such as groups, rings, and fields, and the abstract relationships among quantities. Ranges from elementary manipulation to abstract structures; sibling to Analysis, Geometry, Topology, and Number Theory.
nodes.cs: Odvětví čisté matematiky zabývající se symboly a pravidly jejich úprav — rovnicemi, strukturami jako grupy, okruhy a tělesa a abstraktními vztahy mezi veličinami. Sahá od elementárních úprav po abstraktní struktury; sourozenec analýzy, geometrie, topologie a teorie čísel.
nodes.brief:
Algebra is the branch of pure mathematics concerned with symbols and the rules for manipulating them: solving equations, and building and classifying structures — groups, rings, fields, and the vector spaces and modules built from them — that capture abstract relationships among quantities independent of what those quantities represent. It ranges from the elementary manipulation of expressions to the fully abstract study of structure for its own sake.

Its own children trace that range: Elementary Algebra covers equation-solving and polynomial manipulation, Linear Algebra studies vector spaces and linear maps, and Abstract Algebra, Commutative Algebra and Homological Algebra progressively generalize those structures and the tools (exact sequences, derived functors) used to relate them. Within [[02.01.01]] Pure Mathematics, algebra supplies the structural vocabulary — groups, rings, fields — that [[02.01.01.05]] Number Theory uses to study the integers, and that intertwines with [[02.01.01.04]] Topology wherever algebraic invariants (homology, homotopy groups) are used to classify topological spaces.

Algebra's abstractions — first developed to solve concrete equations — turned out to be the common language of nearly every other branch of mathematics. See the [Encyclopedia of Mathematics entry on algebra](https://encyclopediaofmath.org/wiki/Algebra) and the open [Abstract Algebra: Theory and Applications](http://abstract.ups.edu/) textbook for a rigorous introduction.

nodes.en: The foundational level of algebra — solving linear and quadratic equations, manipulating polynomials, factoring, and handling systems of equations using variables and basic operations. Distinct from Abstract Algebra (general structures like groups and rings): it is the school-level algebra of symbolic arithmetic and equation-solving.
nodes.cs: Základní úroveň algebry — řešení lineárních a kvadratických rovnic, úpravy polynomů, rozklad na součin a řešení soustav rovnic pomocí proměnných a základních operací. Odlišná od abstraktní algebry (obecné struktury jako grupy a okruhy): jde o školní algebru symbolické aritmetiky a řešení rovnic.

nodes.en: Equations in which each variable appears only to the first power, describing straight-line relationships; solving them isolates the unknown or finds where lines meet. The simplest equation type in elementary algebra, distinct from quadratic equations (second-power terms) and from full systems treated jointly.
nodes.cs: Rovnice, v nichž se každá proměnná vyskytuje jen v první mocnině a jež popisují přímkové vztahy; jejich řešení osamostatní neznámou nebo najde průsečík přímek. Nejjednodušší typ rovnice v elementární algebře, odlišný od kvadratických rovnic (členy druhé mocniny) i od soustav řešených společně.

nodes.en: Equations of the second degree, containing a squared variable term, solved by factoring, completing the square, or the quadratic formula, with up to two real roots. Distinct from linear equations (first power) by the curvature they describe and the possibility of two, one, or no real solutions.
nodes.cs: Rovnice druhého stupně obsahující člen s druhou mocninou proměnné, řešené rozkladem, doplněním na čtverec nebo pomocí vzorce, s nejvýše dvěma reálnými kořeny. Od lineárních rovnic (první mocnina) se liší křivostí, kterou popisují, a možností dvou, jednoho či žádného reálného řešení.

nodes.en: Expressions built from variables and coefficients using addition, subtraction, and non-negative integer powers, such as sums of terms of varying degree. The general objects underlying linear and quadratic equations; here the focus is their form, arithmetic, and behavior rather than solving a specific equation.
nodes.cs: Výrazy sestavené z proměnných a koeficientů pomocí sčítání, odčítání a nezáporných celočíselných mocnin, jako součty členů různého stupně. Obecné objekty, na nichž stojí lineární a kvadratické rovnice; zde je důraz na jejich tvar, aritmetiku a chování, nikoli na řešení konkrétní rovnice.

nodes.en: Rewriting a polynomial or number as a product of simpler factors — extracting common terms, recognizing special forms, or splitting into irreducible components — to simplify expressions and solve equations. A technique applied to polynomials, distinct from solving equations directly: it exposes structure as a product.
nodes.cs: Přepsání polynomu nebo čísla jako součinu jednodušších činitelů — vytknutím společných členů, rozpoznáním zvláštních tvarů či rozkladem na nerozložitelné složky — k zjednodušení výrazů a řešení rovnic. Technika aplikovaná na polynomy, odlišná od přímého řešení rovnic: odhaluje strukturu jako součin.

nodes.en: Sets of two or more equations sharing the same unknowns, solved jointly by substitution, elimination, or graphing to find values satisfying all of them simultaneously. Distinct from single linear or quadratic equations: the object is the shared solution of several equations at once.
nodes.cs: Soubory dvou či více rovnic sdílejících tytéž neznámé, řešené společně dosazením, sčítací metodou nebo graficky k nalezení hodnot, jež vyhovují všem současně. Odlišné od jednotlivých lineárních či kvadratických rovnic: předmětem je společné řešení několika rovnic najednou.

nodes.en: The branch of algebra dealing with vector spaces and linear mappings between them — matrices, systems of linear equations, eigenvalues, determinants, and linear transformations. Distinct from Elementary Algebra (scalar equation-solving) and Abstract Algebra (general structures): its objects are vectors, matrices, and the maps that preserve linear structure.
nodes.cs: Odvětví algebry zabývající se vektorovými prostory a lineárními zobrazeními mezi nimi — maticemi, soustavami lineárních rovnic, vlastními čísly, determinanty a lineárními transformacemi. Odlišné od elementární algebry (řešení skalárních rovnic) i abstraktní algebry (obecné struktury): jeho objekty jsou vektory, matice a zobrazení zachovávající lineární strukturu.

nodes.en: Rectangular arrays of numbers arranged in rows and columns, with an algebra of addition and multiplication, used to represent linear transformations and systems of equations compactly. A core object of linear algebra, distinct from vector spaces (the abstract setting) and determinants (a scalar derived from a square matrix).
nodes.cs: Obdélníková pole čísel uspořádaná do řádků a sloupců s algebrou sčítání a násobení, používaná k úspornému zápisu lineárních transformací a soustav rovnic. Základní objekt lineární algebry, odlišný od vektorových prostorů (abstraktní rámec) a determinantů (skalár odvozený ze čtvercové matice).

nodes.en: Sets closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication: subspaces, span, linear independence, basis and dimension. The abstract stage of linear algebra, as opposed to the concrete calculus of Matrices, the spectral focus of Eigenvalues, or the maps between spaces under Linear transformations.
nodes.cs: Množiny uzavřené vůči sčítání vektorů a násobení skalárem: podprostory, lineární obal, lineární nezávislost, báze a dimenze. Abstraktní rámec lineární algebry — na rozdíl od konkrétního počtu s maticemi, spektrálního zaměření vlastních čísel či zobrazení mezi prostory u lineárních transformací.

nodes.en: Scalars for which a linear operator has nonzero invariant directions (eigenvectors): characteristic polynomial, spectrum, diagonalization and spectral decomposition, with applications to stability, dynamics and PCA-style factorizations. Specifically the spectral structure of operators, not general matrix calculus or transformation theory.
nodes.cs: Skaláry, pro něž má lineární operátor nenulové invariantní směry (vlastní vektory): charakteristický polynom, spektrum, diagonalizace a spektrální rozklad, s aplikacemi na stabilitu, dynamiku a faktorizace typu PCA. Jde specificky o spektrální strukturu operátorů, nikoli o obecný maticový počet či teorii transformací.

nodes.en: Structure-preserving maps between vector spaces studied as functions in their own right: kernel, image, rank–nullity, composition and invertibility. Matrices are their coordinate representations (the sibling node), while their spectral properties belong under Eigenvalues.
nodes.cs: Zobrazení mezi vektorovými prostory zachovávající strukturu, studovaná jako funkce samy o sobě: jádro, obraz, věta o dimenzi jádra a obrazu, skládání a invertibilita. Matice jsou jejich souřadnicovou reprezentací (sourozenecký uzel), spektrální vlastnosti patří pod vlastní čísla.

nodes.en: The scalar invariant of a square matrix measuring the volume scaling and orientation of its transformation: cofactor expansion, behaviour under row operations, Cramer's rule and the invertibility criterion det ≠ 0. Narrower than general matrix theory; it feeds the characteristic polynomial but spectra themselves sit under Eigenvalues.
nodes.cs: Skalární invariant čtvercové matice měřící změnu objemu a orientaci příslušné transformace: rozvoj podle kofaktorů, chování při řádkových úpravách, Cramerovo pravidlo a kritérium invertibility det ≠ 0. Užší než obecná teorie matic; vede k charakteristickému polynomu, samotná spektra však patří pod vlastní čísla.

nodes.en: Axiomatic algebraic structures — groups, rings, fields, modules — with their homomorphisms, quotients and classification results such as Galois theory. More general than the equation-solving of Elementary Algebra and the vector-space focus of Linear Algebra; the ring-theoretic and functorial specializations are split off into Commutative and Homological Algebra.
nodes.cs: Axiomatické algebraické struktury — grupy, okruhy, tělesa, moduly — s jejich homomorfismy, faktorstrukturami a klasifikačními výsledky, jako je Galoisova teorie. Obecnější než řešení rovnic v elementární algebře i než vektorové prostory lineární algebry; okruhové a funktoriální specializace jsou vyčleněny do komutativní a homologické algebry.

nodes.en: The theory of commutative rings, their ideals and modules: Noetherian conditions, localization, prime spectra and integral extensions, developed largely as the algebraic foundation of algebraic geometry and number theory. Restricted to commutative rings, unlike general Abstract Algebra, and ring-theoretic rather than functorial, unlike Homological Algebra.
nodes.cs: Teorie komutativních okruhů, jejich ideálů a modulů: noetherovské podmínky, lokalizace, prvoideálová spektra a celistvá rozšíření, rozvíjená především jako algebraický základ algebraické geometrie a teorie čísel. Na rozdíl od obecné abstraktní algebry se omezuje na komutativní okruhy a na rozdíl od homologické algebry pracuje okruhově, nikoli funktoriálně.

nodes.en: Chain complexes, exact sequences and derived functors such as Ext and Tor — machinery measuring how far modules and functors fail to be exact. It abstracts tools shared by topology, commutative algebra and category theory, whereas its sibling nodes study the algebraic structures themselves.
nodes.cs: Řetězcové komplexy, exaktní posloupnosti a derivované funktory jako Ext a Tor — aparát měřící, nakolik moduly a funktory nejsou exaktní. Abstrahuje nástroje sdílené topologií, komutativní algebrou a teorií kategorií, zatímco sourozenecké uzly studují samotné algebraické struktury.

nodes.en: The branch of pure mathematics built on limits, continuity and convergence: differentiation, integration, sequences and series, and the function spaces where they live. Split into Real, Complex, Functional and Harmonic Analysis plus Measure Theory; distinguished from Algebra's discrete structures and from Geometry and Topology's spatial focus by its quantitative, limit-based methods.
nodes.cs: Odvětví čisté matematiky založené na limitách, spojitosti a konvergenci: derivování, integrování, posloupnosti a řady a prostory funkcí, v nichž žijí. Dělí se na reálnou, komplexní, funkcionální a harmonickou analýzu a teorii míry; od diskrétních struktur algebry i od prostorového zaměření geometrie a topologie ji odlišují kvantitativní, limitní metody.

nodes.en: Rigorous calculus on the real line and R^n: completeness, sequences and series, continuity, differentiation, Riemann integration and uniform convergence. The foundational sibling — complex-variable methods, operator and function-space theory, Fourier methods and abstract integration are split into the other Analysis children.
nodes.cs: Rigorózní kalkulus na reálné ose a v R^n: úplnost, posloupnosti a řady, spojitost, derivování, Riemannův integrál a stejnoměrná konvergence. Základní sourozenec — metody komplexní proměnné, teorie operátorů a prostorů funkcí, Fourierovy metody a abstraktní integrace patří do ostatních dětí analýzy.

nodes.en: Functions of a complex variable: holomorphy, Cauchy's theorem, power and Laurent series, residues, conformal maps and analytic continuation. Its rigidity — a complex-differentiable function is automatically analytic — contrasts with the pathologies possible in Real Analysis.
nodes.cs: Funkce komplexní proměnné: holomorfnost, Cauchyova věta, mocninné a Laurentovy řady, rezidua, konformní zobrazení a analytické prodloužení. Její rigidita — komplexně diferencovatelná funkce je automaticky analytická — kontrastuje s patologiemi možnými v reálné analýze.

nodes.en: Infinite-dimensional spaces of functions and the operators on them: Banach and Hilbert spaces, bounded operators, spectral theory, duality and the pillar theorems (Hahn–Banach, open mapping, uniform boundedness). Where Real and Complex Analysis study individual functions, this node studies whole spaces of them.
nodes.cs: Nekonečněrozměrné prostory funkcí a operátory na nich: Banachovy a Hilbertovy prostory, omezené operátory, spektrální teorie, dualita a pilířové věty (Hahnova–Banachova, o otevřeném zobrazení, o stejnoměrné omezenosti). Zatímco reálná a komplexní analýza studují jednotlivé funkce, tento uzel studuje celé jejich prostory.

nodes.en: Decomposing functions and signals into frequency components: Fourier series and transforms and their generalizations to groups, plus wavelets, singular integrals and maximal functions. Distinguished from Functional Analysis by its focus on Fourier-type expansions rather than general operator theory.
nodes.cs: Rozklad funkcí a signálů na frekvenční složky: Fourierovy řady a transformace a jejich zobecnění na grupy, dále wavelety, singulární integrály a maximální funkce. Od funkcionální analýzy ji odlišuje zaměření na rozvoje fourierovského typu místo obecné teorie operátorů.

nodes.en: Abstract theories of size and integration: sigma-algebras, measures, Lebesgue integration, convergence theorems, Radon–Nikodym and product measures. It underpins modern probability and Real Analysis; unlike its siblings it studies the integration framework itself rather than particular classes of functions.
nodes.cs: Abstraktní teorie velikosti a integrace: sigma-algebry, míry, Lebesgueův integrál, věty o konvergenci, Radonova–Nikodymova věta a součinové míry. Je základem moderní pravděpodobnosti a reálné analýzy; na rozdíl od sourozenců studuje samotný rámec integrace, nikoli konkrétní třídy funkcí.

nodes.en: Shapes, sizes and spatial configurations, from axiomatic plane figures to smooth manifolds and algebraic varieties; its children split by method into Euclidean, differential, algebraic, Riemannian and symplectic approaches. Distinguished from Topology by caring about rigid quantities — distance, angle, curvature — not just continuity.
nodes.cs: Tvary, velikosti a prostorové konfigurace, od axiomatických útvarů v rovině po hladké variety a algebraické variety; děti se dělí podle metody na eukleidovský, diferenciální, algebraický, riemannovský a symplektický přístup. Od topologie se liší tím, že jí záleží na rigidních veličinách — vzdálenosti, úhlu, křivosti — nikoli jen na spojitosti.

nodes.en: Classical axiomatic geometry of points, lines, circles and polygons in the plane and space: congruence, similarity, ruler-and-compass constructions and the parallel postulate. The synthetic, metrically flat baseline against which the curved and structure-rich geometries of its siblings are defined.
nodes.cs: Klasická axiomatická geometrie bodů, přímek, kružnic a mnohoúhelníků v rovině a prostoru: shodnost, podobnost, konstrukce pravítkem a kružítkem a postulát o rovnoběžkách. Syntetická, metricky plochá základna, vůči níž se vymezují zakřivené a strukturně bohatší geometrie jejích sourozenců.

nodes.en: Smooth manifolds, curves and surfaces studied with calculus: tangent spaces, vector fields, connections, curvature and differential forms. The general smooth framework — adding a metric tensor gives the sibling Riemannian Geometry, adding a closed nondegenerate 2-form gives Symplectic Geometry.
nodes.cs: Hladké variety, křivky a plochy studované prostředky kalkulu: tečné prostory, vektorová pole, konexe, křivost a diferenciální formy. Obecný hladký rámec — přidáním metrického tenzoru vzniká sourozenecká riemannovská geometrie, přidáním uzavřené nedegenerované 2-formy geometrie symplektická.

nodes.en: Geometric objects defined as zero sets of polynomials — varieties and schemes — studied through commutative algebra and sheaf theory. Its polynomial, algebraic methods separate it from the calculus-based approaches of its Differential and Riemannian siblings.
nodes.cs: Geometrické objekty definované jako nulové množiny polynomů — variety a schémata — studované prostřednictvím komutativní algebry a teorie svazků. Polynomiální, algebraické metody ji oddělují od kalkulových přístupů diferenciálních a riemannovských sourozenců.

nodes.en: Smooth manifolds equipped with a metric tensor, yielding lengths, angles, geodesics and curvature (sectional, Ricci, scalar); the language of general relativity and of comparison theorems. A metric specialization of Differential Geometry, contrasting with Symplectic Geometry, which measures area rather than length.
nodes.cs: Hladké variety vybavené metrickým tenzorem, který dává délky, úhly, geodetiky a křivost (sekční, Ricciho, skalární); jazyk obecné relativity a srovnávacích vět. Metrická specializace diferenciální geometrie, v kontrastu se symplektickou geometrií, jež měří plochu, nikoli délku.

nodes.en: Even-dimensional manifolds carrying a closed nondegenerate 2-form — the natural setting of Hamiltonian mechanics: symplectomorphisms, Lagrangian submanifolds, moment maps and Darboux's theorem. Unlike its Riemannian sibling it has no local invariants such as curvature; its rigidity phenomena are global.
nodes.cs: Sudorozměrné variety nesoucí uzavřenou nedegenerovanou 2-formu — přirozené prostředí hamiltonovské mechaniky: symplektomorfismy, lagrangeovské podvariety, momentová zobrazení a Darbouxova věta. Na rozdíl od riemannovského sourozence nemá lokální invarianty typu křivosti; její rigidita je globální.

nodes.en: Properties of spaces preserved under continuous deformation: open sets, compactness and connectedness, algebraic invariants such as fundamental groups and homology, and the classification of manifolds and knots. Unlike its Geometry sibling it ignores distance, angle and curvature entirely.
nodes.cs: Vlastnosti prostorů zachovávané spojitou deformací: otevřené množiny, kompaktnost a souvislost, algebraické invarianty jako fundamentální grupy a homologie a klasifikace variet a uzlů. Na rozdíl od sourozenecké geometrie zcela ignoruje vzdálenost, úhel i křivost.

nodes.en: Properties of the integers and their generalizations: primes, divisibility, congruences and Diophantine equations, together with the analytic and algebraic machinery (L-functions, algebraic number fields) built to study them. Centered on arithmetic structure, unlike sibling branches organized around equations, limits or space.
nodes.cs: Vlastnosti celých čísel a jejich zobecnění: prvočísla, dělitelnost, kongruence a diofantické rovnice spolu s analytickým a algebraickým aparátem (L-funkce, algebraická číselná tělesa) vybudovaným k jejich studiu. Soustředí se na aritmetickou strukturu, na rozdíl od sourozeneckých větví organizovaných kolem rovnic, limit či prostoru.

nodes.en: Mathematical methods developed for and driven by problems outside mathematics: statistics, probability, numerical computation, optimization and game theory. Distinguished from Pure Mathematics by its problem-first orientation and from Computational Mathematics by covering modelling and inference broadly, not just computation itself.
nodes.cs: Matematické metody vyvíjené pro problémy mimo matematiku a jimi motivované: statistika, pravděpodobnost, numerické výpočty, optimalizace a teorie her. Od čisté matematiky ji odlišuje orientace na problém, od výpočetní matematiky to, že pokrývá modelování a inferenci obecně, nejen samotný výpočet.
nodes.brief:
Applied Mathematics develops and adapts mathematical methods for problems that originate outside mathematics: statistics draws conclusions from data, probability models uncertainty and randomness, numerical analysis constructs algorithms for computing approximate solutions, optimization finds the best choice among alternatives subject to constraints, and game theory analyzes strategic interaction among rational decision-makers. What unites the branch is a problem-first orientation — the mathematics is chosen because it fits a question from physics, engineering, economics, or elsewhere, not the reverse.

[[02.01.02.01]] Statistics and [[02.01.02.02]] Probability share a common formal foundation but differ in direction: probability reasons forward from a known model to the likelihood of outcomes, while statistics reasons backward from observed data to infer the model that produced them. Numerical analysis and optimization are similarly paired around the theme of computing an answer rather than deriving one in closed form — many optimization problems are solved only because numerical methods make an otherwise intractable calculation feasible.

Applied mathematics is distinguished from its sibling [[02.01.01]] Pure Mathematics by that problem-first orientation, and from [[02.01.03]] Computational Mathematics by covering modelling and inference broadly rather than centering on the computation itself. For further reading, see the [Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics](https://www.siam.org/) and the Britannica entry on [applied mathematics](https://www.britannica.com/science/applied-mathematics).

nodes.en: Collecting, summarizing and drawing conclusions from data: study design, estimation, hypothesis testing and modelling in both frequentist and Bayesian flavours. Its children split by task — description versus inference versus Bayesian methodology, plus time-ordered and many-variable data. Distinct from the sibling Probability, which supplies the underlying mathematics of random models.
nodes.cs: Sběr, sumarizace a vyvozování závěrů z dat: návrh studií, odhadování, testování hypotéz a modelování ve frekventistickém i bayesovském pojetí. Děti se dělí podle úlohy — popis versus inference versus bayesovská metodologie, plus časově uspořádaná a mnohorozměrná data. Liší se od sourozenecké pravděpodobnosti, která dodává matematiku náhodných modelů.

nodes.en: Summarizing observed data with no inference to a larger population: measures of location and spread, quantiles, correlation, tabulation and summary visualization. The boundary with the sibling Inferential Statistics is exactly the absence of generalization claims.
nodes.cs: Sumarizace pozorovaných dat bez zobecňování na širší populaci: míry polohy a rozptýlenosti, kvantily, korelace, tabelace a přehledové vizualizace. Hranici vůči sourozenecké inferenční statistice tvoří právě absence zobecňujících tvrzení.

nodes.en: Generalizing from samples to populations: estimators and their properties, confidence intervals, hypothesis tests, p-values and regression inference, largely in the frequentist framework. Contrast Descriptive Statistics (no generalization) and Bayesian Statistics (posterior-based inference).
nodes.cs: Zobecňování z výběrů na populace: odhady a jejich vlastnosti, intervaly spolehlivosti, testy hypotéz, p-hodnoty a inference v regresi, převážně ve frekventistickém rámci. Srovnej popisnou statistiku (bez zobecňování) a bayesovskou statistiku (inference přes posteriorní rozdělení).

nodes.en: Inference that treats parameters as random and updates prior distributions with data via Bayes' theorem: priors, posteriors, credible intervals, hierarchical models and MCMC computation. The methodological alternative to its frequentist sibling, Inferential Statistics.
nodes.cs: Inference, která pokládá parametry za náhodné a aktualizuje apriorní rozdělení daty podle Bayesovy věty: priory, posteriory, kredibilní intervaly, hierarchické modely a výpočty MCMC. Metodologická alternativa k frekventistickému sourozenci, inferenční statistice.

nodes.en: Statistical modelling of data ordered in time and its dependence structure: autocorrelation, stationarity, ARIMA and state-space models, spectral analysis and forecasting. What separates it from the other Statistics children is that the temporal ordering of observations is the central object.
nodes.cs: Statistické modelování časově uspořádaných dat a jejich závislostní struktury: autokorelace, stacionarita, modely ARIMA a stavové modely, spektrální analýza a předpovídání. Od ostatních dětí statistiky ji odlišuje to, že ústředním objektem je časové uspořádání pozorování.

nodes.en: Statistical methods for many variables observed jointly: covariance structure, principal components and factor analysis, clustering, discriminant analysis and MANOVA. Its focus on cross-variable structure separates it from the temporal ordering of Time Series Analysis and the single-response focus of basic inference.
nodes.cs: Statistické metody pro mnoho společně pozorovaných proměnných: kovarianční struktura, hlavní komponenty a faktorová analýza, shlukování, diskriminační analýza a MANOVA. Zaměření na strukturu mezi proměnnými ji odděluje od časového uspořádání v analýze časových řad i od jedné odezvy v základní inferenci.

nodes.en: The mathematical theory of randomness: probability spaces, random variables, distributions, expectation, limit theorems and stochastic processes. It supplies the axioms and models on which the sibling Statistics builds its data-driven inference.
nodes.cs: Matematická teorie náhody: pravděpodobnostní prostory, náhodné veličiny, rozdělení, střední hodnota, limitní věty a stochastické procesy. Dodává axiomy a modely, na nichž sourozenecká statistika staví svou inferenci z dat.

nodes.en: Design and error analysis of algorithms that approximate continuous mathematics: floating-point arithmetic, solvers for linear systems and eigenvalues, interpolation, quadrature and numerical ODE/PDE methods. Its concern is accuracy, stability and convergence — unlike the sibling Optimization, defined by its objective-extremizing problem class.
nodes.cs: Návrh a analýza chyb algoritmů aproximujících spojitou matematiku: aritmetika v plovoucí čárce, řešiče lineárních soustav a vlastních čísel, interpolace, kvadratura a numerické metody pro ODR/PDR. Jde jí o přesnost, stabilitu a konvergenci — na rozdíl od sourozenecké optimalizace, vymezené úlohou extremalizace účelové funkce.

nodes.en: Finding the best solution under constraints: linear, convex, nonlinear, integer and stochastic programming, optimality conditions and duality, and algorithms from gradient descent to interior-point methods. The extremize-an-objective problem class distinguishes it from the general approximation focus of Numerical Analysis.
nodes.cs: Hledání nejlepšího řešení za omezujících podmínek: lineární, konvexní, nelineární, celočíselné a stochastické programování, podmínky optimality a dualita, algoritmy od gradientního sestupu po metody vnitřního bodu. Třída úloh extremalizace účelové funkce ji odlišuje od obecného aproximačního zaměření numerické analýzy.

nodes.en: Mathematical models of strategic interaction among rational agents: normal- and extensive-form games, Nash equilibria, cooperative and evolutionary games, mechanism design and auctions. Unlike its siblings it studies multi-agent incentives rather than single-objective computation or inference.
nodes.cs: Matematické modely strategické interakce racionálních aktérů: hry v normální a extenzivní formě, Nashova ekvilibria, kooperativní a evoluční hry, návrh mechanismů a aukce. Na rozdíl od sourozenců studuje pobídky více aktérů, nikoli výpočet či inferenci s jediným cílem.

nodes.en: Mathematics done with and about computation: symbolic computation and computer algebra, algorithmic treatment of mathematical problems, experimental mathematics and computer-assisted proof. It sits between its siblings — less abstract than Pure Mathematics, and centered on the computation itself rather than the broad modelling and inference of Applied Mathematics.
nodes.cs: Matematika prováděná výpočtem a o výpočtu: symbolické výpočty a počítačová algebra, algoritmické zpracování matematických problémů, experimentální matematika a počítačem asistované důkazy. Stojí mezi svými sourozenci — méně abstraktní než čistá matematika a soustředěná na samotný výpočet, nikoli na široké modelování a inferenci aplikované matematiky.
nodes.brief:
Computational Mathematics is mathematics done with and about computation: symbolic computation and computer algebra, the algorithmic treatment of mathematical problems, and experimental mathematics and computer-assisted proof, where a computer's calculation becomes part of the mathematical argument itself rather than merely a tool for checking one. It sits between its siblings in abstraction — less concerned with proof for its own sake than [[02.01.01]] Pure Mathematics, and more centered on the computation itself than the broad modelling and inference of [[02.01.02]] Applied Mathematics.

Where applied mathematics might use numerical analysis as one tool among many to model a physical system, computational mathematics takes the act of computing — exact symbolic manipulation, algorithm design for mathematical objects, verification of a proof by machine — as its central subject. Landmark results such as the computer-assisted proof of the four-color theorem exemplify the field: a claim about mathematics established not by human derivation alone but by a computation whose correctness is itself part of what had to be proven.

Computational mathematics also borders [[02.02]] Computer Science, since the algorithms it designs and the software that implements them are engineering artifacts as much as mathematical ones. For further reading, see the [Encyclopedia of Mathematics entry on computational mathematics](https://encyclopediaofmath.org/wiki/Computational_mathematics) and the [Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics](https://www.siam.org/).

nodes.name: Mathematical Disciplines (MSC2020)
nodes.en: The mathematical sciences organized by the Mathematics Subject Classification 2020 — 52 core-mathematics disciplines from number theory and algebraic geometry through functional analysis, topology, probability and numerical analysis. Physics-applied MSC classes live in the Physics cluster; this branch is pure and applied mathematics proper.
nodes.cs: Matematické vědy uspořádané podle Mathematics Subject Classification 2020 — 52 disciplín jádra matematiky od teorie čísel a algebraické geometrie přes funkcionální analýzu, topologii, pravděpodobnost a numerickou analýzu. Třídy MSC aplikované ve fyzice patří do fyzikálního shluku; toto odvětví je vlastní čistá a aplikovaná matematika.

nodes.name: General and overarching topics; collections
nodes.en: The MSC2020 top class (00-XX) for material that falls outside standard disciplinary classification, split between general and recreational mathematics, education and philosophy of mathematics on one side, and purely editorial collections — proceedings, festschriften, collected works — on the other. A catch-all and bibliographic-format class rather than a subject-matter one.
nodes.cs: Nejvyšší třída MSC2020 (00-XX) pro látku mimo standardní oborové členění, rozdělená na obecnou a rekreační matematiku, vzdělávání a filozofii matematiky na jedné straně a čistě ediční sbírky — sborníky, pamětní svazky, souborná díla — na straně druhé. Jde spíše o zastřešující a bibliograficko-formátovou třídu než o třídu vymezenou předmětem.
nodes.brief:
The opening class of the MSC scheme is deliberately not a discipline: it holds what the subject-matter classes cannot — recreational and popular mathematics, philosophy and methodology, general reference works, and the editorial formats (proceedings, festschriften, collected works) by which mathematical literature is packaged. Its two children split along exactly that line: cross-cutting topics on one side, bibliographic formats on the other.

Its subject-flavored corners are shared with neighbors: pedagogy and popularization border [[02.01.04.52]] mathematics education, methodological and historical reflection borders [[02.01.04.02]] history and biography, and the catch-all interfaces of mathematics with physics, music, art and society are the informal counterpart of the systematic mathematics-physics boundary that runs through [[01.01]].

nodes.name: General and Miscellaneous Mathematical Topics
nodes.en: Covers material that sits outside the standard disciplinary classification: general and recreational mathematics, mathematics education and popularization, philosophy and methodology of mathematics, mathematical modeling and simulation, dictionaries and reference works, and the interfaces of mathematics with physics, music, art, architecture, sport, and society. This subclass functions as the MSC scheme's catch-all for cross-cutting, pedagogical, and interdisciplinary material that no single discipline can claim.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá materiál stojící mimo standardní oborové třídění: obecnou a rekreační matematiku, výuku a popularizaci matematiky, filozofii a metodologii matematiky, matematické modelování a simulaci, slovníky a referenční díla a rozhraní matematiky s fyzikou, hudbou, uměním, architekturou, sportem a společností. Tato podtřída slouží v klasifikaci MSC jako sběrná kategorie pro průřezový, pedagogický a mezioborový materiál, který si nemůže nárokovat žádný jednotlivý obor.
nodes.brief:
Covers material that sits outside the standard disciplinary classification: general and recreational mathematics, mathematics education and popularization, philosophy and methodology of mathematics, mathematical modeling and simulation, dictionaries and reference works, and the interfaces of mathematics with physics, music, art, architecture, sport, and society. This subclass functions as the MSC scheme's catch-all for cross-cutting, pedagogical, and interdisciplinary materia

### Concepts
- **General and miscellaneous specific topics** (00Axx) — Groups cross-disciplinary and non-technical mathematical material — general/recreational mathematics, education, popularization, philosophy and methodology, modeling and simulation, reference works, and mathematics' interfaces with physics, the arts, and society — that does not belong to any single specialized MSC area.

nodes.name: Collections, Proceedings, and Collected Works
nodes.en: Classifies published volumes by their editorial format rather than by mathematical content: conference proceedings, festschriften, collected or selected works of an individual, and multi-author collections of articles. Such classification labels are attached alongside a subject-matter code to indicate that a work is an edited compilation rather than a monograph or a single-author paper. The category is purely bibliographic and administrative, reflecting how mathematical literature is published rather than what it is about.
nodes.cs: Třídí publikované svazky podle redakčního formátu, nikoli podle matematického obsahu: sborníky z konferencí, festschrifty, sebrané či vybrané spisy jednotlivce a sbírky článků více autorů. Takové klasifikační značky se připojují vedle věcného kódu na znamení, že jde o redigovanou kompilaci, nikoli o monografii či článek jediného autora. Kategorie je čistě bibliografická a administrativní a odráží to, jak je matematická literatura vydávána, nikoli o čem pojednává.
nodes.brief:
Classifies published volumes by their editorial format rather than by mathematical content: conference proceedings, festschriften, collected or selected works of an individual, and multi-author collections of articles. Such classification labels are attached alongside a subject-matter code to indicate that a work is an edited compilation rather than a monograph or a single-author paper. The category is purely bibliographic and administrative, reflecting how mathematical liter

### Concepts
- **Conference proceedings and collections of articles** (00Bxx) — Bibliographic classification for edited volumes — conference proceedings, festschriften, and collected or selected works — used to tag a publication's editorial format independent of its mathematical subject matter.

nodes.name: History and biography
nodes.en: The MSC2020 class (01-XX) covering the history of mathematics and the biography of mathematicians: historical surveys, institutional history, and studies of individual mathematicians, texts or theorems across eras and cultures.
nodes.cs: Třída MSC2020 (01-XX) pokrývající historii matematiky a biografie matematiků: historické přehledy, dějiny institutí a studie jednotlivých matematiků, textů či vět napříč epochami a kulturami.
nodes.brief:
Where every other pillar classifies mathematics itself, this one classifies its past: the development of concepts, notation and methods across cultures and eras, together with the biographies and institutional settings of the people who made them. Its methods are those of the humanities — philology of primary sources, archival work, history of science — applied to material whose content is mathematical.

It overlaps [[02.01.04.01]] where general and philosophical writing shades into historical reflection, and [[02.01.04.52]], where the history of mathematics serves as a teaching resource. Historical studies of a single theorem or text are classified here even when their subject matter belongs to another pillar — the history of the calculus whose modern descendants live in [[02.01.04.18]] and [[02.01.04.24]] is the standard example.

nodes.name: History of Mathematics and Mathematicians
nodes.en: Studies the historical development of mathematical ideas, methods, and notation across cultures and eras, alongside the biographies, correspondence, and institutional contexts of mathematicians. Draws on primary-source philology, archival research, and the history of science to reconstruct how concepts such as number, proof, and infinity evolved. Encompasses both broad surveys of mathematical civilizations and focused studies of individual theorems, texts, or figures, informing our understanding of how the discipline came to its present shape.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá historický vývoj matematických idejí, metod a notace napříč kulturami a epochami spolu s biografiemi, korespondencí a institucionálním kontextem matematiků. Opírá se o filologii pramenů, archivní výzkum a dějiny vědy, aby rekonstruovala, jak se vyvíjely pojmy jako číslo, důkaz či nekonečno. Zahrnuje jak široké přehledy matematických civilizací, tak zaměřené studie jednotlivých vět, textů či osobností, a prohlubuje tak porozumění tomu, jak obor dospěl ke své dnešní podobě.
nodes.brief:
Studies the historical development of mathematical ideas, methods, and notation across cultures and eras, alongside the biographies, correspondence, and institutional contexts of mathematicians. Draws on primary-source philology, archival research, and the history of science to reconstruct how concepts such as number, proof, and infinity evolved. Encompasses both broad surveys of mathematical civilizations and focused studies of individual theorems, texts, or figures, informi

### Concepts
- **History of mathematics and mathematicians** (01Axx) — Studies the origins, evolution, transmission, and biographical/institutional context of mathematical ideas, texts, and practitioners across all periods and cultures.

nodes.name: Mathematical logic and foundations
nodes.en: The MSC2020 class (03-XX) for mathematical logic and the foundations of mathematics, split between the syntactic side — proof theory, formal deductive systems, and constructive foundations — and the semantic side — model theory, set theory, and computability theory.
nodes.cs: Třída MSC2020 (03-XX) pro matematickou logiku a základy matematiky, rozdělená na syntaktickou stránku — teorii důkazu, formální deduktivní systémy a konstruktivní základy — a sémantickou stránku — teorii modelů, teorii množin a teorii vypočitatelnosti.
nodes.brief:
Mathematical logic turns the machinery of mathematics on mathematics itself: formal languages and deductive systems on the syntactic side, model theory, set theory and computability on the semantic side. It is where the notions of proof, truth and algorithm receive exact definitions, and where the limits of formalization — incompleteness, undecidability, independence — are established.

Its algebraic encodings, Boolean and Heyting algebras, live next door in [[02.01.04.05]]; its study of structures-with-operations at maximal generality parallels the universal algebra of [[02.01.04.06]]; its computability theory is the common root it shares with [[02.01.04.47]]; and category-theoretic foundations connect it to [[02.01.04.14]].

nodes.name: Deductive Systems, Proof, and Foundations
nodes.en: Studies the syntactic side of logic: the formal languages, inference systems, and philosophical frameworks used to formalize mathematical reasoning, together with the algebraic structures that encode them. Encompasses propositional, predicate, modal, and intuitionistic calculi, the analysis of formal proofs as combinatorial objects (ordinal analysis, cut-elimination), constructive mathematics, and the translation of logical systems into algebras such as Boolean, Heyting, and cylindric algebras.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá syntaktickou stránku logiky: formální jazyky, odvozovací systémy a filozofické rámce užívané k formalizaci matematického usuzování, spolu s algebraickými strukturami, které je kódují. Zahrnuje výrokové, predikátové, modální a intuicionistické kalkuly, analýzu formálních důkazů jako kombinatorických objektů (ordinální analýza, eliminace řezu), konstruktivní matematiku a překlad logických systémů do algeber, jako jsou Booleovy, Heytingovy a cylindrické algebry.
nodes.brief:
Studies the syntactic side of logic: the formal languages, inference systems, and philosophical frameworks used to formalize mathematical reasoning, together with the algebraic structures that encode them. Encompasses propositional, predicate, modal, and intuitionistic calculi, the analysis of formal proofs as combinatorial objects (ordinal analysis, cut-elimination), constructive mathematics, and the translation of logical systems into algebras such as Boolean, Heyting, and 

### Concepts
- **Philosophical aspects of logic and foundations** (03Axx) — Examines the philosophical, historical, and methodological questions surrounding logical systems and the foundations of mathematics.
- **General logic** (03Bxx) — Covers classical and non-classical propositional and predicate calculi — including many-valued, modal, intuitionistic, and other logics — and their general model-theoretic and proof-theoretic properties.
- **Proof theory and constructive mathematics** (03Fxx) — Analyzes formal proofs as mathematical objects, measuring the strength of theories via ordinal analysis and cut-elimination, and develops constructive alternatives to classical mathematics.
- **Algebraic logic** (03Gxx) — Studies algebraic structures — Boolean algebras, Heyting algebras, cylindric and relation algebras — that model logical systems and recast logical questions as algebraic ones.

nodes.name: Model Theory, Set Theory, and Computability
nodes.en: Studies the semantic and structural side of logic: the interpretations, structures, and universes in which formal theories are realized, and the limits of algorithmic definability within them. Model theory classifies structures by the sentences they satisfy; set theory develops the cumulative universe of sets via cardinal and ordinal arithmetic, forcing, and large cardinals; computability theory classifies functions and sets by their algorithmic complexity; and nonstandard models of arithmetic, analysis, and set theory, built via ultraproducts, underlie nonstandard analysis.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá sémantickou a strukturální stránku logiky: interpretace, struktury a univerza, v nichž se formální teorie realizují, a hranice algoritmické definovatelnosti v nich. Teorie modelů klasifikuje struktury podle vět, které splňují; teorie množin buduje kumulativní univerzum množin pomocí kardinální a ordinální aritmetiky, forcingu a velkých kardinálů; teorie vyčíslitelnosti klasifikuje funkce a množiny podle jejich algoritmické složitosti; a nestandardní modely aritmetiky, analýzy a teorie množin, budované pomocí ultraproduktů, jsou základem nestandardní analýzy.
nodes.brief:
Studies the semantic and structural side of logic: the interpretations, structures, and universes in which formal theories are realized, and the limits of algorithmic definability within them. Model theory classifies structures by the sentences they satisfy; set theory develops the cumulative universe of sets via cardinal and ordinal arithmetic, forcing, and large cardinals; computability theory classifies functions and sets by their algorithmic complexity; nonstandard models

### Concepts
- **Model theory** (03Cxx) — Studies the correspondence between formal languages and the structures that interpret them, classifying structures by the sentences they satisfy.
- **Computability and recursion theory** (03Dxx) — Studies which functions and sets are algorithmically computable or definable, and organizes the resulting hierarchy of unsolvability degrees.
- **Set theory** (03Exx) — Develops the foundational theory of sets, including cardinal and ordinal arithmetic, the axiom of choice, forcing, and large cardinal axioms.
- **Nonstandard models** (03Hxx) — Studies nonstandard models of arithmetic, analysis, and set theory built via ultraproducts and related constructions, underlying nonstandard analysis.

nodes.name: Combinatorics
nodes.en: Combinatorics is the mathematics of discrete structures and their counting, existence, and extremal behavior: enumerative and algebraic combinatorics counts structures exactly or asymptotically via generating functions and symmetric-function methods, design theory builds and classifies balanced incidence structures such as block designs and Latin squares, and graph theory with extremal combinatorics studies graphs, hypergraphs, and how large a structure can grow before it is forced to contain a given pattern.
nodes.cs: Kombinatorika je matematika diskrétních struktur, jejich počítání, existence a extremálního chování: enumerativní a algebraická kombinatorika počítá struktury přesně nebo asymptoticky pomocí vytvořujících funkcí a metod symetrických funkcí, teorie designů konstruuje a klasifikuje vyvážené incidenční struktury, jako jsou blokové designy a latinské čtverce, a teorie grafů s extremální kombinatorikou zkoumá grafy, hypergrafy a to, jak velká může struktura být, než je nucena obsahovat daný vzor.
nodes.brief:
Combinatorics studies finite and discrete structures — counting them exactly or asymptotically, proving they exist, and finding how large they can grow before a forbidden pattern is forced. Enumerative and algebraic combinatorics, design theory, and graph theory with extremal combinatorics form its three broad strata, tied together by generating functions, symmetry, and the probabilistic method borrowed from [[02.01.04.44]].

Its boundaries are busy: discrete geometry in [[02.01.04.38]] counts configurations of points and convex sets, design theory feeds the error-correcting codes of [[02.01.04.51]], order-theoretic combinatorics shades into [[02.01.04.05]], and graph algorithms and complexity questions cross into [[02.01.04.47]].

nodes.name: Enumerative and Algebraic Combinatorics
nodes.en: Studies exact and asymptotic counting of combinatorial structures — permutations, partitions, trees, lattice paths — via generating functions, bijective proofs, and species theory, alongside the algebraic structures (symmetric functions, representation theory of the symmetric group, posets, matroids, association schemes) that organize and explain these counts. The interplay between counting identities and algebraic invariants (Schur functions, RSK correspondence, Coxeter combinatorics) is the field's defining method.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá přesné i asymptotické počítání kombinatorických struktur — permutací, rozkladů, stromů, mřížkových cest — pomocí vytvořujících funkcí, bijektivních důkazů a teorie kombinatorických druhů (species), spolu s algebraickými strukturami (symetrické funkce, teorie reprezentací symetrické grupy, uspořádané množiny, matroidy, asociační schémata), které tato počítání organizují a vysvětlují. Vzájemné působení mezi kombinatorickými identitami a algebraickými invarianty (Schurovy funkce, RSK korespondence, Coxeterova kombinatorika) je určující metodou oboru.
nodes.brief:
Studies exact and asymptotic counting of combinatorial structures — permutations, partitions, trees, lattice paths — via generating functions, bijective proofs, and species theory, alongside the algebraic structures (symmetric functions, representation theory of the symmetric group, posets, matroids, association schemes) that organize and explain these counts. The interplay between counting identities and algebraic invariants (Schur functions, RSK correspondence, Coxeter comb

### Concepts
- **Enumerative combinatorics** (05Axx) — The study of exact and asymptotic counting formulas for combinatorial structures using generating functions, recurrences, and bijective methods.
- **Algebraic combinatorics** (05Exx) — The use of algebraic tools — representation theory, symmetric functions, posets, and association schemes — to encode and solve combinatorial problems.

nodes.name: Designs and Configurations
nodes.en: Concerns the existence, construction, and classification of combinatorial designs — block designs, Latin squares, Steiner systems, and finite geometries — built from finite sets with prescribed balance and incidence properties. Design theory underpins coding theory, statistical experiment design, and cryptography, and its central existence questions, such as the completion of design spectra, were resolved only in the last decade using probabilistic and algebraic methods.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se existencí, konstrukcí a klasifikací kombinatorických designů — blokových designů, latinských čtverců, Steinerových systémů a konečných geometrií — budovaných z konečných množin s předepsanými vlastnostmi vyváženosti a incidence. Teorie designů je základem teorie kódování, statistického plánování experimentů a kryptografie a její klíčové existenční otázky, jako je dokončení spekter designů, byly vyřešeny teprve v posledním desetiletí pomocí pravděpodobnostních a algebraických metod.
nodes.brief:
Concerns the existence, construction, and classification of combinatorial designs — block designs, Latin squares, Steiner systems, and finite geometries — built from finite sets with prescribed balance and incidence properties. Design theory underpins coding theory, statistical experiment design, and cryptography, and its central existence questions (such as the completion of design spectra) were resolved only in the last decade using probabilistic and algebraic methods. The 

### Concepts
- **Designs and configurations** (05Bxx) — The theory of finite incidence structures — block designs, Latin squares, and Steiner systems — satisfying prescribed regularity and balance conditions.

nodes.name: Graph Theory and Extremal Combinatorics
nodes.en: Encompasses the structural, algorithmic, and topological study of graphs and hypergraphs alongside extremal questions asking how large or dense a structure can be before it is forced to contain a given substructure or pattern — Turán-type problems, Ramsey theory, and extremal set theory. These two areas share methodology (regularity and container methods, probabilistic and algebraic techniques) and jointly drive much of modern discrete mathematics's connection to computer science.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje strukturální, algoritmické a topologické studium grafů a hypergrafů spolu s extremálními otázkami, jak velká nebo hustá může struktura být, než je nucena obsahovat daný podgraf či vzor — Turánovské problémy, Ramseyho teorii a extremální teorii množin. Obě oblasti sdílejí metodologii (metody regularity a kontejnerů, pravděpodobnostní a algebraické techniky) a společně tvoří velkou část propojení moderní diskrétní matematiky s informatikou.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses the structural, algorithmic, and topological study of graphs and hypergraphs alongside extremal questions asking how large or dense a structure can be before it is forced to contain a given substructure or pattern — Turán-type problems, Ramsey theory, and extremal set theory. These two areas share methodology (regularity and container methods, probabilistic and algebraic techniques) and jointly drive much of modern discrete mathematics's connection to computer sci

### Concepts
- **Graph theory** (05Cxx) — The study of graphs and hypergraphs — their structure, coloring, connectivity, and algorithmic properties — as models of pairwise relations.
- **Extremal combinatorics** (05Dxx) — The study of the maximum or minimum size a combinatorial structure can have while avoiding or guaranteeing a given substructure, including Ramsey and Turán-type problems.

nodes.name: Order, lattices, ordered algebraic structures
nodes.en: Studies sets and algebraic structures equipped with a compatible order relation: ordered sets and ordered algebraic structures develop posets, chains, and order-compatible groups, rings, and fields in their own right, while lattice theory specializes to posets with well-defined joins and meets — modular, distributive, and Boolean lattices — the algebraic core linking order to universal algebra and propositional logic.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá množiny a algebraické struktury vybavené kompatibilním vztahem uspořádání: uspořádané množiny a uspořádané algebraické struktury rozvíjejí uspořádané množiny, řetězce a s uspořádáním kompatibilní grupy, okruhy a tělesa samy o sobě, zatímco teorie svazů se specializuje na uspořádané množiny s dobře definovaným spojením a průsekem — modulární, distributivní a Booleovy svazy — algebraické jádro spojující uspořádání s univerzální algebrou a výrokovou logikou.
nodes.brief:
Order is the thread here: posets, chains, and algebraic structures whose operations respect an underlying comparison — ordered groups, rings and fields — culminating in lattice theory, where joins and meets make order itself algebraic. Modular, distributive and Boolean lattices are its central objects, with Boolean algebras doubling as the algebraic semantics of the propositional logic in [[02.01.04.03]].

As a bridge discipline it faces [[02.01.04.06]], since lattices and ordered algebras are prime examples of general algebraic systems and congruence lattices organize universal algebra itself; combinatorial order theory — poset dimension, Dilworth-type theorems — faces [[02.01.04.04]]; and continuous lattices and domain theory serve the semantics of computation in [[02.01.04.47]].

nodes.name: Ordered Sets and Ordered Algebraic Structures
nodes.en: Studies partially ordered sets in their own right — chains, antichains, order-preserving maps, completions, and dimension theory — alongside algebraic structures (groups, rings, fields, semigroups) equipped with a compatible order relation. This block underlies nearly all of order theory, providing the foundational vocabulary (posets, order types, cofinality) on which lattice theory and Boolean algebra are built, and it connects order to algebra via ordered algebraic systems such as ordered groups and ordered fields.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá částečně uspořádané množiny samy o sobě — řetězce, antiřetězce, monotónní zobrazení, zúplnění a teorii dimenze — spolu s algebraickými strukturami (grupy, okruhy, tělesa, pologrupy) vybavenými kompatibilním vztahem uspořádání. Tento blok je základem téměř celé teorie uspořádání, poskytuje základní pojmový aparát (uspořádané množiny, typy uspořádání, kofinalita), na němž stojí teorie svazů a Booleova algebra, a propojuje uspořádání s algebrou prostřednictvím uspořádaných algebraických systémů, jako jsou uspořádané grupy a uspořádaná tělesa.
nodes.brief:
Studies partially ordered sets in their own right — chains, antichains, order-preserving maps, completions, and dimension theory — alongside algebraic structures (groups, rings, fields, semigroups) equipped with a compatible order relation. This block underlies nearly all of order theory, providing the foundational vocabulary (posets, order types, cofinality) on which lattice theory and Boolean algebra are built, and it connects order to algebra via ordered algebraic systems 

### Concepts
- **Ordered sets** (06Axx) — The general theory of partially ordered sets: order relations, chains, antichains, well-founded and well-ordered sets, order-preserving maps, and completions.
- **Ordered structures** (06Fxx) — Algebraic structures such as groups, rings, semigroups, and fields endowed with a compatible partial or total order, studying how algebraic operations interact with the order relation.

nodes.name: Lattice Theory
nodes.en: Examines lattices — posets in which every pair of elements has a join and meet — and their principal special classes: modular and complemented lattices (generalizing subgroup and subspace lattices), distributive lattices (with their Stone/Priestley duality to topological spaces), and Boolean algebras (the algebraic counterpart of classical propositional logic and set operations). This is the classical algebraic core of order theory, with deep ties to universal algebra, projective geometry, and combinatorics.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá svazy — uspořádané množiny, v nichž má každá dvojice prvků spojení a průsek — a jejich hlavní speciální třídy: modulární a komplementární svazy (zobecňující svazy podgrup a podprostorů), distributivní svazy (se Stoneovou/Priestleyho dualitou k topologickým prostorům) a Booleovy algebry (algebraický protějšek klasické výrokové logiky a množinových operací). Jde o klasické algebraické jádro teorie uspořádání s hlubokými vazbami na univerzální algebru, projektivní geometrii a kombinatoriku.
nodes.brief:
Examines lattices — posets in which every pair of elements has a join and meet — and their principal special classes: modular and complemented lattices (generalizing subgroup and subspace lattices), distributive lattices (with their Stone/Priestley duality to topological spaces), and Boolean algebras (the algebraic counterpart of classical propositional logic and set operations). This is the classical algebraic core of order theory, with deep ties to universal algebra, projec

### Concepts
- **Lattices** (06Bxx) — Partially ordered sets in which every pair of elements has a well-defined least upper bound (join) and greatest lower bound (meet), studied as algebraic structures obeying the lattice axioms.
- **Modular lattices, complemented lattices** (06Cxx) — Lattices satisfying the modular law — generalizing the lattice of subgroups or vector subspaces — or admitting complements for each element, including relatively complemented and orthocomplemented lattices.
- **Distributive lattices** (06Dxx) — Lattices in which meet and join mutually distribute over one another, a class encompassing Heyting algebras, frames and locales, and studied via Stone and Priestley duality with topological spaces.
- **Boolean algebras (Boolean rings)** (06Exx) — Complemented distributive lattices, equivalently rings with idempotent multiplication, that algebraically model the operations of set union, intersection, and complement and underlie classical propositional logic.

nodes.name: General algebraic systems
nodes.en: Groups the general theory of algebraic structures studied at maximal abstraction — sets with operations satisfying identities, considered independently of a specific signature — realized here through universal algebra's theory of varieties and congruence lattices.
nodes.cs: Sdružuje obecnou teorii algebraických struktur zkoumaných na nejvyšší úrovni abstrakce — množin s operacemi splňujícími identity, nezávisle na konkrétní signatuře — realizovanou zde prostřednictvím teorie variet a kongruenčních svazů univerzální algebry.
nodes.brief:
This is algebra at maximal abstraction: sets carrying operations that satisfy identities, studied independently of any particular signature. Universal algebra's varieties, free algebras and congruence lattices supply theorems that hold at once for the groups of [[02.01.04.16]], the rings of [[02.01.04.12]] and [[02.01.04.13]], and the lattices of [[02.01.04.05]]. Birkhoff's variety theorem — classes defined by identities are exactly those closed under homomorphic images, subalgebras and products — is the pillar's signature result.

Its two natural interlocutors are model theory in [[02.01.04.03]], which studies the same structures through the logic of their first-order theories, and category theory in [[02.01.04.14]], which abstracts one step further, from structures-with-operations to objects-and-morphisms.

nodes.name: Universal Algebra
nodes.en: Universal algebra studies algebraic structures (sets equipped with operations satisfying identities) in full generality, abstracting away from particular signatures to uncover laws common to groups, rings, lattices, and beyond. It develops the theory of varieties (equationally defined classes closed under subalgebras, homomorphic images, and products, per Birkhoff's theorem) and congruence lattices, and extends to non-classical algebra types such as partial algebras, quasivarieties, and other generalized non-equational classes.
nodes.cs: Univerzální algebra zkoumá algebraické struktury (množiny vybavené operacemi splňujícími identity) v plné obecnosti, odhlíží od konkrétní signatury a odhaluje zákony společné grupám, okruhům, svazům a dalším strukturám. Rozvíjí teorii variet (tříd definovaných rovnicemi, uzavřených na podalgebry, homomorfní obrazy a součiny podle Birkhoffovy věty) a kongruenčních svazů a rozšiřuje se na neklasické typy algeber, jako jsou parciální algebry, kvazivariety a další zobecněné nerovnicové třídy.
nodes.brief:
Universal algebra studies algebraic structures (sets equipped with operations satisfying identities) in full generality, abstracting away from particular signatures to uncover laws common to groups, rings, lattices, and beyond. It develops the theory of varieties (equationally defined classes closed under subalgebras, homomorphic images, and products, per Birkhoff's theorem) and congruence lattices, and extends to non-classical algebra types such as partial algebras, multi-op

### Concepts
- **Algebraic structures** (08Axx) — Studies sets endowed with one or more finitary (or infinitary) operations, developing the general theory of homomorphisms, congruences, subalgebras, and direct/subdirect decompositions common to all such structures.
- **Varieties** (08Bxx) — Investigates equationally axiomatized classes of algebras (varieties in the sense of Birkhoff's HSP theorem), including free algebras, identities, Maltsev conditions, and lattices of subvarieties.
- **Other classes of algebras** (08Cxx) — Covers algebraic structure classes that fall outside the standard variety framework, such as quasivarieties, partial algebras, and other generalized or non-equational classes of algebras.

nodes.name: Number theory
nodes.en: Number theory studies the integers and their generalizations — divisibility, primes, Diophantine equations, and arithmetic in number fields — ranging from elementary and combinatorial arithmetic through analytic, algebraic, and additive methods to the arithmetic geometry and automorphic-forms machinery of the Langlands program.
nodes.cs: Teorie čísel zkoumá celá čísla a jejich zobecnění — dělitelnost, prvočísla, diofantické rovnice a aritmetiku v číselných tělesech — od elementární a kombinatorické aritmetiky přes analytické, algebraické a aditivní metody až po aritmetickou geometrii a aparát automorfních forem Langlandsova programu.
nodes.brief:
Number theory begins with the integers — divisibility, primes, congruences, Diophantine equations — and grows outward into every part of mathematics its problems touch: analytic number theory borrows the complex analysis of [[02.01.04.20]] to count primes, algebraic number theory builds on the field and ring theory of [[02.01.04.08]] and [[02.01.04.09]], and arithmetic geometry merges with the varieties of [[02.01.04.10]]. At its modern summit the Langlands program ties Galois representations, automorphic forms and L-functions into a single web.

Combinatorial and additive questions exchange methods with [[02.01.04.04]], iteration of rational maps over number fields is shared with [[02.01.04.26]], and the practical face of primality testing and factoring underpins the cryptography classified under [[02.01.04.51]].

nodes.name: Elementary, Combinatorial and Sequence-Based Number Theory
nodes.en: Studies the arithmetic of the integers through elementary, non-analytic tools — divisibility, congruences, and arithmetic functions — together with the combinatorial structure of integer sequences and sets, and the number-theoretic behavior of polynomials and matrices. This is the classical bedrock of the subject, largely settled since the 19th and early 20th centuries, though it continues to seed elementary open problems and underlies every other branch of number theory.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá aritmetiku celých čísel elementárními, neanalytickými nástroji — dělitelností, kongruencemi a aritmetickými funkcemi — spolu s kombinatorickou strukturou celočíselných posloupností a množin a s chováním polynomů a matic z hlediska teorie čísel. Jde o klasické podloží oboru, z velké části uzavřené v 19. a na počátku 20. století, které však dál plodí elementární otevřené problémy a nese každou další větev teorie čísel.
nodes.brief:
Studies the arithmetic of the integers through elementary, non-analytic tools — divisibility, congruences, and arithmetic functions — together with the combinatorial structure of integer sequences and sets, and the number-theoretic behavior of polynomials and matrices. This is the classical bedrock of the subject, largely settled since the 19th and early 20th centuries, though it continues to seed elementary open problems and underlies every other branch of number theory.

### Concepts
- **Elementary number theory** (11Axx) — Studies divisibility, congruences, primality, and arithmetic functions using elementary, non-analytic methods.
- **Sequences and sets** (11Bxx) — Investigates integer sequences (recurrences, special number sequences) and the additive-combinatorial structure of sets of integers.
- **Polynomials and matrices** (11Cxx) — Examines number-theoretic properties of polynomials and matrices with integer or arithmetic coefficients, such as discriminants and resultants.

nodes.name: Diophantine Equations, Approximation and Arithmetic Geometry
nodes.en: Concerns integer and rational solutions of polynomial equations (Diophantine equations), their geometric incarnation as points on algebraic varieties over number fields (arithmetic/Diophantine geometry), the lattice-point and convex-body methods of Minkowski used to bound and count solutions (geometry of numbers), and the fine approximation of real and algebraic numbers by rationals, including transcendence proofs. Landmark results — Faltings' theorem and Wiles' proof of Fermat's Last Theorem — mark this block among the deepest unions of algebra, geometry, and analysis in number theory.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se celočíselnými a racionálními řešeními polynomiálních rovnic (diofantické rovnice), jejich geometrickým ztvárněním jako body na algebraických varietách nad číselnými tělesy (aritmetická/diofantická geometrie), Minkowského metodami mřížkových bodů a konvexních těles pro odhad a počítání řešení (geometrie čísel) a jemnou aproximací reálných a algebraických čísel racionálními čísly, včetně důkazů transcendence. Přelomové výsledky — Faltingsova věta a Wilesův důkaz Velké Fermatovy věty — řadí tento blok mezi nejhlubší spojení algebry, geometrie a analýzy v teorii čísel.
nodes.brief:
Concerns integer and rational solutions of polynomial equations (Diophantine equations), their geometric incarnation as points on algebraic varieties over number fields (arithmetic/Diophantine geometry), the lattice-point and convex-body methods of Minkowski used to bound and count solutions (geometry of numbers), and the fine approximation of real and algebraic numbers by rationals, including transcendence proofs. Landmark results — Faltings' theorem and Wiles' proof of Ferm

### Concepts
- **Diophantine equations** (11Dxx) — Studies integer and rational solutions of polynomial equations, including Fermat-type, Pell, and exponential Diophantine equations.
- **Arithmetic algebraic geometry (Diophantine geometry)** (11Gxx) — Studies rational and integral points on algebraic varieties over number fields, especially elliptic curves and abelian varieties.
- **Geometry of numbers** (11Hxx) — Applies lattice-point and convex-body geometry, in the spirit of Minkowski, to Diophantine and arithmetic problems.
- **Diophantine approximation, transcendental number theory** (11Jxx) — Measures how well real and complex numbers are approximated by rationals or algebraics, and proves transcendence and algebraic independence.

nodes.name: Forms, Automorphic Representations and Arithmetic Groups
nodes.en: Studies quadratic and other forms and the linear algebraic groups that act on them, together with automorphic forms and representations attached to discrete arithmetic subgroups of Lie groups. This block is the arithmetic home of the Langlands program, linking Galois representations, modular forms, and L-functions, and it is one of the most intensely researched frontiers in contemporary mathematics.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá kvadratické a jiné formy a lineární algebraické grupy, které na nich působí, spolu s automorfními formami a reprezentacemi příslušnými diskrétním aritmetickým podgrupám Lieových grup. Tento blok je aritmetickým domovem Langlandsova programu, propojuje Galoisovy reprezentace, modulární formy a L-funkce a patří k nejintenzivněji zkoumaným frontám současné matematiky.
nodes.brief:
Studies quadratic and other forms and the linear algebraic groups that act on them, together with automorphic forms and representations attached to discrete arithmetic subgroups of Lie groups. This block is the arithmetic home of the Langlands program, linking Galois representations, modular forms, and L-functions, and it is one of the most intensely researched frontiers in contemporary mathematics.

### Concepts
- **Forms and linear algebraic groups** (11Exx) — Studies quadratic and higher-degree forms and the linear algebraic groups that classify and act on them.
- **Discontinuous groups and automorphic forms** (11Fxx) — Studies modular and automorphic forms on arithmetic discrete subgroups of Lie groups, central to the Langlands program.

nodes.name: Analytic, Multiplicative and Additive Number Theory
nodes.en: Covers the analytic machinery of number theory: equidistribution and metric properties of sequences modulo 1, exponential and character sum estimates, the analytic theory of zeta and L-functions, the distribution of primes, and the additive representation of integers as sums such as in the Goldbach and Waring problems and partition theory. Central open problems — the Riemann Hypothesis, the twin prime conjecture, the Goldbach conjecture — live in this block, making it a major frontier of contemporary analytic number theory.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá analytický aparát teorie čísel: ekvidistribuci a metrické vlastnosti posloupností modulo 1, odhady exponenciálních a charakterových součtů, analytickou teorii zeta a L-funkcí, rozložení prvočísel a aditivní vyjádření celých čísel jako součtů, například v Goldbachově a Waringově problému a teorii rozkladů čísel. Centrální otevřené problémy — Riemannova hypotéza, hypotéza prvočíselných dvojčat, Goldbachova hypotéza — patří do tohoto bloku, což z něj činí jednu z hlavních front současné analytické teorie čísel.
nodes.brief:
Covers the analytic machinery of number theory: equidistribution and metric properties of sequences modulo 1, exponential and character sum estimates, the analytic theory of zeta and L-functions, the distribution of primes, and the additive representation of integers as sums such as in the Goldbach and Waring problems and partition theory. Central open problems — the Riemann Hypothesis, the twin prime conjecture, the Goldbach conjecture — live in this block, making it a major

### Concepts
- **Probabilistic theory: distribution modulo 1; metric theory of algorithms** (11Kxx) — Studies equidistribution and uniform distribution of sequences modulo 1, and the metric (almost-everywhere) theory of algorithms such as continued fractions.
- **Exponential sums and character sums** (11Lxx) — Bounds exponential and character sums, tools used throughout number theory to control error terms.
- **Zeta and L-functions: analytic theory** (11Mxx) — Studies analytic properties — zeros, growth, functional equations — of zeta and L-functions, including the Riemann Hypothesis.
- **Multiplicative number theory** (11Nxx) — Studies the distribution of primes and the behavior of multiplicative arithmetic functions.
- **Additive number theory; partitions** (11Pxx) — Studies representations of integers as sums of prescribed types, including Goldbach- and Waring-type problems, and integer partitions.

nodes.name: Algebraic Number Theory and Finite Fields
nodes.en: Develops the arithmetic of number fields and their completions: ideal class groups, unit groups, and Galois theory over global fields, the p-adic and local-field analogues, and number-theoretic properties of finite fields and related commutative rings. Class field theory and local-global principles give this area a mature, largely settled core, even as programs such as Iwasawa theory keep parts of it in active development.
nodes.cs: Rozvíjí aritmetiku číselných těles a jejich zúplnění: grupy tříd ideálů, grupy jednotek a Galoisovu teorii nad globálními tělesy, jejich p-adické a lokální analogie a teoreticko-číselné vlastnosti konečných těles a příbuzných komutativních okruhů. Teorie třídových těles a lokálně-globální principy dávají tomuto oboru zralé, převážně ustálené jádro, byť programy jako Iwasawova teorie jeho části udržují v aktivním vývoji.
