Slouží jako pojmový základ, na němž stojí sesterské oblasti teorie grup — teorie reprezentací, abelovská a kategoriální rozšíření a zobecnění na pologrupy.
nodes.brief:
Establishes the axiomatic language of group theory and develops the structure and classification theory of both finite and infinite groups. Includes the machinery of subgroup lattices, composition series, and extension theory that underlies the largely completed classification of finite simple groups, alongside special structural properties such as nilpotency, solvability, growth, and geometric group theory that remain actively studied for infinite groups. Serves as the conce

### Concepts
- **Foundations** (20Axx) — Covers the basic axioms, generators-and-relations presentations, and elementary constructions (subgroups, quotients, homomorphisms) on which all group theory is built.
- **Abstract finite groups** (20Dxx) — Studies the internal structure of finite groups, including Sylow theory, solvable and nilpotent groups, and the composition-factor structure feeding into simple-group classification.
- **Structure and classification of infinite or finite groups** (20Exx) — Develops general subgroup-structure and classification results applicable to both finite and infinite groups, including chief series, maximal subgroups, and extension problems.
- **Special aspects of infinite or finite groups** (20Fxx) — Investigates specialized group-theoretic properties such as word and conjugacy problems, subgroup growth, and geometric/combinatorial group theory for finitely generated and infinite groups.

nodes.name: Group Actions, Representations, and Linear Algebraic Groups
nodes.en: Concerns groups realized as symmetries of other structures: permutation actions on sets, linear representations on vector spaces, and algebraic groups defined by polynomial equations. Representation theory and the theory of linear algebraic groups form deep, mutually reinforcing pillars connecting group theory to combinatorics, geometry, and number theory, with reductive-group theory and character theory remaining major active research programs. Matrix groups arising outside this algebraic-group framework, such as classical matrix groups studied via direct linear-algebraic methods, are also gathered here.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se grupami realizovanými jako symetrie jiných struktur: permutačními akcemi na množinách, lineárními reprezentacemi na vektorových prostorech a algebraickými grupami definovanými polynomiálními rovnicemi. Teorie reprezentací a teorie lineárních algebraických grup tvoří hluboké, vzájemně se posilující pilíře propojující teorii grup s kombinatorikou, geometrií a teorií čísel, přičemž teorie reduktivních grup a teorie charakterů zůstávají hlavními aktivními výzkumnými programy. Patří sem i maticové grupy vznikající mimo tento algebraicko-grupový rámec, studované přímými lineárně-algebraickými metodami.
nodes.brief:
Concerns groups realized as symmetries of other structures: permutation actions on sets, linear representations on vector spaces, and algebraic groups defined by polynomial equations. Representation theory and the theory of linear algebraic groups form deep, mutually reinforcing pillars connecting group theory to combinatorics, geometry, and number theory, with reductive-group theory and character theory remaining major active research programs. Matrix groups arising outside 

### Concepts
- **Permutation groups** (20Bxx) — Studies groups acting on sets, focusing on transitivity, primitivity, orbits, and the combinatorial structure of permutation representations.
- **Representation theory of groups** (20Cxx) — Analyzes homomorphisms of groups into groups of linear transformations, encompassing ordinary and modular character theory and modules over group rings and algebras.
- **Linear algebraic groups and related topics** (20Gxx) — Studies algebraic groups defined by polynomial equations over a field, including reductive and semisimple groups, their representation theory, and arithmetic and geometric variants.
- **Other groups of matrices** (20Hxx) — Covers concrete matrix groups such as classical groups, arithmetic groups, and discrete matrix subgroups studied outside the full algebraic-group apparatus.

nodes.name: Abelian Groups, Homological Connections, and Groupoids
nodes.en: Gathers the commutative branch of group theory together with its categorical and cohomological extensions. Abelian group theory is a classical, essentially settled subject built on invariants like rank and torsion, while the study of group cohomology, extensions, and categorical structures such as groupoids remains an active bridge to homological algebra and higher category theory. Groupoids generalize groups to multi-object categories with invertible morphisms, unifying symmetry that varies from point to point within a single structure.
nodes.cs: Shromažďuje komutativní větev teorie grup spolu s jejími kategoriálními a kohomologickými rozšířeními. Teorie abelovských grup je klasický, v podstatě uzavřený obor postavený na invariantech, jako jsou hodnost a torze, zatímco studium grupové kohomologie, rozšíření a kategoriálních struktur, jako jsou grupoidy, zůstává aktivním mostem k homologické algebře a vyšší teorii kategorií. Grupoidy zobecňují grupy na vícepředmětové kategorie s invertibilními morfismy a sjednocují symetrii, která se v rámci jedné struktury mění bod od bodu.
nodes.brief:
Gathers the commutative branch of group theory together with its categorical and cohomological extensions. Abelian group theory is a classical, essentially settled subject built on invariants like rank and torsion, while the study of group cohomology, extensions, and categorical structures such as groupoids remains an active bridge to homological algebra and higher category theory. Groupoids generalize groups to multi-object categories with invertible morphisms, unifying symm

### Concepts
- **Abelian groups** (20Kxx) — Studies commutative groups, classifying them via invariants such as rank, torsion, and divisibility, for both finitely and infinitely generated cases.
- **Connections of group theory with homological algebra and category theory** (20Jxx) — Examines group cohomology and homology, crossed modules, and categorical constructions such as extensions that link group theory to homological algebra.
- **Groupoids** (20Lxx) — Studies groupoids, small categories in which every morphism is invertible, generalizing groups to structures with multiple objects and partial composition.

nodes.name: Semigroups, Generalized Structures, and Probabilistic Methods
nodes.en: Extends the group concept in two directions: algebraically, by relaxing invertibility to obtain semigroups and other group-like structures such as quasigroups and loops; and methodologically, by importing probability theory to study random walks and asymptotic behavior on groups. Semigroup theory is a mature classical subject, while probabilistic group theory is a comparatively young and fast-moving research frontier with deep ties to ergodic theory and geometric group theory, particularly through the study of random walk boundaries and growth rates.
nodes.cs: Rozšiřuje pojem grupy dvěma směry: algebraicky uvolněním invertibility, čímž vznikají pologrupy a další grupě podobné struktury, jako jsou kvazigrupy a smyčky, a metodologicky importem teorie pravděpodobnosti ke studiu náhodných procházek a asymptotického chování na grupách. Teorie pologrup je zralý klasický obor, zatímco pravděpodobnostní teorie grup je poměrně mladá a rychle se rozvíjející výzkumná oblast úzce provázaná s ergodickou teorií a geometrickou teorií grup, zejména prostřednictvím studia hranic náhodných procházek a rychlostí růstu.
nodes.brief:
Extends the group concept in two directions: algebraically, by relaxing invertibility to obtain semigroups and other group-like structures such as quasigroups and loops; and methodologically, by importing probability theory to study random walks and asymptotic behavior on groups. Semigroup theory is a mature classical subject, while probabilistic group theory is a comparatively young and fast-moving research frontier with deep ties to ergodic theory and geometric group theory

### Concepts
- **Semigroups** (20Mxx) — Studies sets equipped with an associative binary operation without a general inverse requirement, including monoids and inverse semigroups and their structure theory.
- **Other generalizations of groups** (20Nxx) — Covers algebraic structures generalizing groups beyond semigroups and groupoids, such as quasigroups, loops, and other non-associative or partial generalizations.
- **Probabilistic methods in group theory** (20Pxx) — Applies probability theory to group theory, studying random walks on groups, random subgroups, and asymptotic statistical properties of group elements and structures.

nodes.name: Topological groups, Lie groups
nodes.en: Groups equipped with a compatible topological or smooth structure, spanning locally compact and compact group theory (Pontryagin duality, the Peter-Weyl theorem) and Lie groups with their algebras, representations and noncompact transformation actions. Abstract harmonic analysis and transformation-group actions on manifolds are treated in their own MSC classes.
nodes.cs: Grupy vybavené kompatibilní topologickou nebo hladkou strukturou, zahrnující teorii lokálně kompaktních a kompaktních grup (Pontrjaginova dualita, Peterova-Weylova věta) a Lieovy grupy s jejich algebrami, reprezentacemi a nekompaktními transformačními akcemi. Abstraktní harmonická analýza a transformační akce na varietách jsou zpracovány ve vlastních MSC třídách.
nodes.brief:
Add a topology or smooth structure to a group and analysis enters: locally compact groups with Haar measure, compact groups with the Peter-Weyl theorem, Pontryagin duality for abelian ones, and above all Lie groups, whose local structure is entirely captured by the Lie algebras of [[02.01.04.13]]. Representation theory, finite- and infinite-dimensional, is the pillar's engine.

It is the structural substrate on which [[02.01.04.31]] does its Fourier analysis, and its transformation-group actions on manifolds face the geometry of [[02.01.04.39]] and the topology of [[02.01.04.42]]. For [[01.01]], Lie groups are the mathematics of continuous symmetry — gauge groups, spacetime symmetries, and conservation laws via Noether's theorem.

nodes.name: Topological and Locally Compact Group Theory
nodes.en: Studies groups (and more general algebraic systems) equipped with a compatible topology, focusing on the structural interplay between algebra and topology. Core results include the Pontryagin duality theorem for locally compact abelian groups, the Peter-Weyl theorem for compact groups, and the general structure theory of locally compact groups via their group algebras and unitary representations. This area furnishes the foundational machinery for abstract harmonic analysis, including induced representations and amenability.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá grupy (a obecnější algebraické systémy) vybavené kompatibilní topologií se zaměřením na strukturální propojení algebry a topologie. Mezi klíčové výsledky patří Pontrjaginova dualita pro lokálně kompaktní abelovské grupy, Peterova-Weylova věta pro kompaktní grupy a obecná strukturální teorie lokálně kompaktních grup prostřednictvím jejich grupových algeber a unitárních reprezentací. Tato oblast poskytuje základní aparát pro abstraktní harmonickou analýzu, včetně indukovaných reprezentací a amenability.
nodes.brief:
Studies groups (and more general algebraic systems) equipped with a compatible topology, focusing on the structural interplay between algebra and topology. Core results include the Pontryagin duality theorem for locally compact abelian groups, the Peter-Weyl theorem for compact groups, and the general structure theory of locally compact groups via their group algebras and unitary representations. This area furnishes the foundational machinery for abstract harmonic analysis an

### Concepts
- **Topological and differentiable algebraic systems** (22Axx) — Studies semigroups, quasigroups, and other algebraic systems endowed with a compatible topological or differentiable structure, excluding topological rings and fields.
- **Locally compact abelian groups (LCA groups)** (22Bxx) — Develops the structure theory of abelian topological groups that are locally compact, centered on the Pontryagin duality between an LCA group and its character group.
- **Compact groups** (22Cxx) — Studies compact topological groups via the Peter-Weyl theorem, decomposing their representations into finite-dimensional irreducibles and describing their structure as inverse limits of Lie groups.
- **Locally compact groups and their algebras** (22Dxx) — Develops the general structure and unitary representation theory of locally compact groups, including their group algebras, induced representations, and amenability properties.

nodes.name: Lie Groups and Noncompact Transformation Groups
nodes.en: Concerns Lie groups — groups that are simultaneously smooth manifolds with smooth multiplication — their associated Lie algebras, structure theory, and representations, together with the actions of noncompact groups on spaces. This area bridges algebra, differential geometry, and analysis; the representation theory of noncompact semisimple Lie groups (unitary duals, discrete series, geometric actions) remains an active research frontier with several classification problems still open.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se Lieovými grupami - grupami, které jsou zároveň hladkými varietami s hladkým násobením - jejich Lieovými algebrami, strukturální teorií a reprezentacemi, spolu s akcemi nekompaktních grup na prostorech. Tato oblast propojuje algebru, diferenciální geometrii a analýzu; reprezentační teorie nekompaktních poloprostých Lieových grup (unitární duály, diskrétní série, geometrické akce) zůstává aktivní výzkumnou frontou s několika dosud otevřenými klasifikačními problémy.
nodes.brief:
Concerns Lie groups — groups that are simultaneously smooth manifolds with smooth multiplication — their associated Lie algebras, structure theory, and representations, together with the actions of noncompact groups on spaces. This area bridges algebra, differential geometry, and analysis, and its representation theory of noncompact semisimple Lie groups (unitary duals, discrete series, geometric actions) remains an active research frontier with several classification problem

### Concepts
- **Lie groups** (22Exx) — Studies Lie groups as smooth manifolds with compatible group structure, together with their Lie algebras, structure theory, and finite- and infinite-dimensional representations.
- **Noncompact transformation groups** (22Fxx) — Examines actions of noncompact topological or Lie groups on spaces, including orbit structure, proper actions, and the geometric and dynamical consequences of noncompactness.

nodes.name: Real functions
nodes.en: The MSC branch of classical real analysis: continuity, differentiability, monotonicity and variation of real-valued functions, extended to polynomial and rational approximation, analytic inequalities, and non-classical frameworks such as nonstandard analysis and o-minimal structures. Functional-analytic treatments of function spaces and analysis on manifolds belong to their own MSC classes.
nodes.cs: MSC obor klasické reálné analýzy: spojitost, diferencovatelnost, monotonie a variace reálných funkcí, rozšířené o polynomiální a racionální aproximaci, analytické nerovnosti a neklasické rámce jako nestandardní analýza a o-minimální struktury. Funkcionálně-analytické zpracování funkčních prostorů a analýza na varietách patří do vlastních MSC tříd.
nodes.brief:
The classical heart of real analysis: how real-valued functions of real variables behave — continuity, differentiability, monotonicity, convexity, bounded variation — extended to polynomial and rational approximation, analytic inequalities, and non-classical frameworks such as nonstandard analysis and o-minimality. It is the descriptive layer beneath calculus.

Its integrals and measures are refined in [[02.01.04.19]], its approximation questions systematized in [[02.01.04.29]], and its inequalities feed the estimates of [[02.01.04.30]] and [[02.01.04.25]]; the functional-analytic study of whole spaces of functions is deliberately left to [[02.01.04.34]]. Where derivatives drive evolution rather than describe shape, the subject becomes the differential equations of [[02.01.04.24]].

nodes.name: Single- and Multivariable Real Function Theory
nodes.en: Studies the local and global behavior of real-valued functions of one or several real variables: continuity, differentiability, monotonicity, convexity, absolute continuity, bounded variation, and generalized derivatives. This is the classical descriptive backbone of real analysis, underlying calculus, measure theory, and the analytic treatment of functions on R and R^n.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá lokální a globální chování reálných funkcí jedné či několika reálných proměnných: spojitost, diferencovatelnost, monotonii, konvexitu, absolutní spojitost, omezenou variaci a zobecněné derivace. Jde o klasickou deskriptivní páteř reálné analýzy, na níž stojí infinitezimální počet, teorie míry a analytické zacházení s funkcemi na R a R^n.
nodes.brief:
Studies the local and global behavior of real-valued functions of one or several real variables: continuity, differentiability, monotonicity, convexity, absolute continuity, bounded variation, and generalized derivatives. This is the classical descriptive backbone of real analysis, underlying calculus, measure theory, and the analytic treatment of functions on R and R^n.

### Concepts
- **Functions of one variable** (26Axx) — The classical theory of real-valued functions of a single real variable, covering continuity, differentiability, monotonicity, convexity, absolute continuity, bounded variation, and generalized notions of derivative.
- **Functions of several variables** (26Bxx) — The multivariable extension of one-variable real analysis, studying partial and directional derivatives, differentiability, Jacobians, convexity, and change-of-variables results for functions on R^n.

nodes.name: Polynomials and Rational Functions in Real Analysis
nodes.en: Examines real polynomials and rational functions through an analytic lens: root location and separation, extremal and shape properties, factorization, and behavior under approximation on real intervals. It bridges classical algebra with real-variable analytic techniques such as interpolation and Chebyshev-type extremal problems.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá reálné polynomy a racionální funkce analytickou optikou: polohu a separaci kořenů, extremální a tvarové vlastnosti, rozklady a chování při aproximaci na reálných intervalech. Přemosťuje klasickou algebru s technikami analýzy reálné proměnné, jako jsou interpolace a extremální úlohy čebyševovského typu.
nodes.brief:
Examines real polynomials and rational functions through an analytic lens: root location and separation, extremal and shape properties, factorization, and behavior under approximation on real intervals. It bridges classical algebra with real-variable analytic techniques such as interpolation and Chebyshev-type extremal problems.

### Concepts
- **Polynomials, rational functions in real analysis** (26Cxx) — The analytic study of real polynomials and rational functions, including root location, extremal and sign properties, and their approximation-theoretic behavior on the real line.

nodes.name: Inequalities in Real Analysis
nodes.en: Develops and classifies analytic inequalities for real functions — convexity-based, mean-value, integral, and comparison inequalities — that quantify relationships between a function's values, derivatives, or integrals. Excludes maximal-function inequalities (42B25), functional inequalities (39B72), and probabilistic inequalities (60E15), which belong to their own disciplines despite overlapping techniques.
nodes.cs: Rozvíjí a třídí analytické nerovnosti pro reálné funkce — nerovnosti opřené o konvexitu, nerovnosti o středních hodnotách, integrální a srovnávací nerovnosti — které kvantifikují vztahy mezi hodnotami funkce, jejími derivacemi či integrály. Nezahrnuje nerovnosti pro maximální funkce (42B25), funkcionální nerovnosti (39B72) ani pravděpodobnostní nerovnosti (60E15), jež přes příbuzné techniky náležejí vlastním disciplínám.
nodes.brief:
Develops and classifies analytic inequalities for real functions — convexity-based, mean-value, integral, and comparison inequalities — that quantify relationships between a function's values, derivatives, or integrals. Excludes maximal-function inequalities (42B25), functional inequalities (39B72), and probabilistic inequalities (60E15), which belong to their own disciplines despite overlapping techniques.

### Concepts
- **Inequalities in real analysis** (26Dxx) — The systematic study of classical and modern analytic inequalities for real functions, such as convexity, mean-value, and integral inequalities, distinct from maximal-function, functional, and probabilistic inequalities treated in other classes.

nodes.name: Miscellaneous and Non-Classical Real Function Topics
nodes.en: A residual category for real-function topics outside the classical descriptive and inequality theory, including nonstandard-analysis treatments, o-minimal and tame-geometry perspectives, and other emerging analytic frameworks. It serves as the growth point of the discipline, absorbing new approaches before they mature into dedicated subclasses.
nodes.cs: Zbytková kategorie pro témata o reálných funkcích mimo klasickou deskriptivní teorii a teorii nerovností, včetně přístupů nestandardní analýzy, o-minimálních a „krotkých“ geometrických perspektiv a dalších nastupujících analytických rámců. Slouží jako růstový bod disciplíny: vstřebává nové přístupy, než dozrají ve vlastní podtřídy.
nodes.brief:
A residual category for real-function topics outside the classical descriptive and inequality theory, including nonstandard-analysis treatments, o-minimal and tame-geometry perspectives, and other emerging analytic frameworks. It serves as the growth point of the discipline, absorbing new approaches before they mature into dedicated subclasses.

### Concepts
- **Miscellaneous topics in real functions** (26Exx) — A catch-all subclass for non-classical or emerging approaches to real functions — such as nonstandard analysis and o-minimal structures — not covered by the other 26-XX subclasses.

nodes.name: Measure and integration
nodes.en: The MSC branch developing measure theory and integration from Lebesgue's Euclidean framework to abstract vector-valued set functions and measures on topological or group-structured spaces, together with the measure-preserving transformations and invariant measures studied in ergodic theory. Analysis on manifolds is treated in its own MSC class.
nodes.cs: MSC obor rozvíjející teorii míry a integrace od Lebesgueova rámce na eukleidovském prostoru po abstraktní vektorově hodnotové množinové funkce a míry na prostorech s topologickou či grupovou strukturou, spolu s míru zachovávajícími transformacemi a invariantními mírami studovanými v ergodické teorii. Analýza na varietách je zpracována ve vlastní MSC třídě.
nodes.brief:
Measure theory makes size rigorous: from Lebesgue's theory on Euclidean space to abstract measures, vector-valued set functions, and measures on topological and group-structured spaces, together with the measure-preserving transformations that lead into ergodic theory. Integration against a measure is its fundamental operation and the backbone of modern analysis.

It axiomatizes the probability of [[02.01.04.44]] — a probability space is a measure space of total mass one — supplies the invariant measures of [[02.01.04.26]], underlies the L^p spaces of [[02.01.04.34]] and the Fourier analysis of [[02.01.04.30]], and refines the descriptive function theory of [[02.01.04.18]]. Geometric measure theory keeps a channel open to [[02.01.04.36]] and its minimal surfaces.

nodes.name: Foundations of Measure and Integration
nodes.en: Covers the construction of measures and integrals starting from Lebesgue's framework on Euclidean space, their extension to abstract vector-space-valued set functions, and their specialization to spaces carrying topological, group, or infinite-dimensional structure. Also gathers auxiliary and non-standard measure-theoretic constructions that fall outside the main lines. Together these subclasses supply the analytic bedrock — sigma-algebras, integration, Radon-Nikodym-type differentiation, and convergence theorems — on which the rest of analysis and probability builds.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje konstrukci měr a integrálů počínaje Lebesgueovým rámcem na eukleidovském prostoru, jejich rozšíření na abstraktní množinové funkce s hodnotami ve vektorových prostorech a jejich specializaci na prostory s topologickou, grupovou nebo nekonečněrozměrnou strukturou. Zahrnuje také pomocné a nestandardní míra-teoretické konstrukce, které se nevejdou do hlavních linií. Společně tyto podtřídy tvoří analytický základ - sigma-algebry, integraci, diferencování typu Radon-Nikodým a věty o konvergenci - na němž stojí zbytek analýzy a teorie pravděpodobnosti.
nodes.brief:
Covers the construction of measures and integrals starting from Lebesgue's framework on Euclidean space, their extension to abstract vector-space-valued set functions, and their specialization to spaces carrying topological, group, or infinite-dimensional structure. Also gathers auxiliary and non-standard measure-theoretic constructions that fall outside the main lines. Together these subclasses supply the analytic bedrock — sigma-algebras, integration, Radon-Nikodym-type dif

### Concepts
- **Classical measure theory** (28Axx) — Develops the Lebesgue framework of sigma-algebras, measurable functions, and integration on Euclidean and abstract sets, including convergence theorems, product measures, and the Radon-Nikodym derivative.
- **Set functions, measures and integrals with values in abstract spaces** (28Bxx) — Extends measure and integration theory to set functions and integrands valued in Banach spaces or other abstract targets, via vector measures and the Bochner and Pettis integrals.
- **Set functions and measures on spaces with additional structure** (28Cxx) — Studies measures compatible with extra ambient structure, such as Radon measures on topological spaces, Haar measure on locally compact groups, and Gaussian or cylindrical measures on infinite-dimensional spaces.
- **Miscellaneous topics in measure theory** (28Exx) — Collects measure-theoretic constructions outside the standard hierarchy, including finitely additive set functions and non-standard approaches to measure and integration.

nodes.name: Measure-Theoretic Ergodic Theory
nodes.en: Studies measure-preserving transformations and flows on probability spaces, treating invariant measures, entropy, mixing, and recurrence as the central objects of analysis. It forms the measure-theoretic core of dynamical systems theory, feeding into number theory, statistical mechanics, and probability via the ergodic theorem and its generalizations. The area remains a vigorous research frontier, with deep open problems on rigidity, entropy classification, and connections to homogeneous dynamics.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá míru zachovávající transformace a toky na pravděpodobnostních prostorech, přičemž invariantní míry, entropii, míšení a rekurenci považuje za ústřední objekty zkoumání. Tvoří míra-teoretické jádro teorie dynamických systémů a prostřednictvím ergodické věty a jejích zobecnění se propojuje s teorií čísel, statistickou mechanikou a teorií pravděpodobnosti. Tato oblast zůstává živou výzkumnou frontou s hlubokými otevřenými problémy týkajícími se rigidity, klasifikace entropie a souvislostí s homogenní dynamikou.
nodes.brief:
Studies measure-preserving transformations and flows on probability spaces, treating invariant measures, entropy, mixing, and recurrence as the central objects of analysis. It forms the measure-theoretic core of dynamical systems theory, feeding into number theory, statistical mechanics, and probability via the ergodic theorem and its generalizations. The area remains a vigorous research frontier, with deep open problems on rigidity, entropy classification, and connections to

### Concepts
- **Measure-theoretic ergodic theory** (28Dxx) — Analyzes measure-preserving transformations and flows through invariant measures, entropy, mixing properties, and recurrence, forming the measure-theoretic core of dynamical systems.

nodes.name: Functions of a complex variable
nodes.en: The MSC branch of one-variable complex analysis: holomorphy, power-series representations and analytic continuation, the geometric distortion and value-distribution theory of holomorphic maps, Riemann surfaces and Banach/Hilbert spaces of analytic functions, and generalized complex-analytic frameworks on metric or non-classical spaces. Several-variable complex analysis and analysis on manifolds are treated in their own MSC classes.
nodes.cs: MSC obor komplexní analýzy jedné proměnné: holomorfie, reprezentace mocninnými řadami a analytické pokračování, geometrická teorie deformace a distribuce hodnot holomorfních zobrazení, Riemannovy plochy a Banachovy/Hilbertovy prostory analytických funkcí a zobecněné komplexně-analytické rámce na metrických či neklasických prostorech. Komplexní analýza více proměnných a analýza na varietách jsou zpracovány ve vlastních MSC třídách.
nodes.brief:
One complex derivative buys infinite regularity: holomorphic functions are analytic, rigid and geometrically expressive, from power series and analytic continuation through value distribution, geometric function theory and Riemann surfaces to Banach and Hilbert spaces of analytic functions. This rigidity makes complex analysis both a subject and a universal tool.

Its potential-theoretic side — harmonic functions are locally real parts of holomorphic ones — is [[02.01.04.21]]; several variables change the phenomena enough to warrant [[02.01.04.22]]; iteration of holomorphic maps is the complex dynamics of [[02.01.04.26]]; and its residues and contour methods serve the analytic number theory of [[02.01.04.07]] and the transforms of [[02.01.04.32]].

nodes.name: Foundations, Series, and General Theory
nodes.en: Covers the core toolkit of one-variable complex analysis: holomorphy, analytic continuation, boundary behavior, and the Taylor, Laurent, and Dirichlet series representations that encode a holomorphic function's local and global structure. Also gathers miscellaneous extremal and structural questions in the complex plane that do not fit a more specialized subclass. This is the settled bedrock on which every other 30-XX area builds.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá základní instrumentář komplexní analýzy jedné proměnné: holomorfnost, analytické pokračování, chování na hranici a reprezentace Taylorovými, Laurentovými a Dirichletovými řadami, jež kódují lokální i globální strukturu holomorfní funkce. Shromažďuje také různé extremální a strukturní otázky v komplexní rovině, které nespadají do specializovanějších podtříd. Jde o ustálené podloží, na němž staví všechny ostatní oblasti třídy 30-XX.
nodes.brief:
Covers the core toolkit of one-variable complex analysis: holomorphy, analytic continuation, boundary behavior, and the Taylor, Laurent, and Dirichlet series representations that encode a holomorphic function's local and global structure. Also gathers miscellaneous extremal and structural questions in the complex plane that do not fit a more specialized subclass. This is the settled bedrock on which every other 30-XX area builds.

### Concepts
- **General properties of functions of one complex variable** (30Axx) — Studies the basic structural properties of holomorphic and meromorphic functions of a single complex variable, including continuity, differentiability, and analytic continuation.
- **Series expansions of functions of one complex variable** (30Bxx) — Studies power, Laurent, Dirichlet, and other series representations of holomorphic functions, together with their convergence and summability properties.
- **Miscellaneous topics of analysis in the complex plane** (30Exx) — Collects extremal problems, approximation questions, and other complex-plane analysis topics not classified under a dedicated 30-XX subclass.

nodes.name: Geometric Function Theory and Value Distribution
nodes.en: Examines how holomorphic maps distort shape and measure — conformal and quasiconformal mappings, univalence, extremal length, and coefficient problems descending from the Bieberbach conjecture — alongside the global growth and value-distribution theory of entire and meromorphic functions built on Nevanlinna theory. Together these areas connect complex analysis to geometry, dynamics, and Diophantine approximation; Nevanlinna theory and its ramifications remain an active research frontier.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá, jak holomorfní zobrazení deformují tvar a míru - konformní a kvazikonformní zobrazení, univalenci, extremální délku a koeficientové problémy vycházející z Bieberbachovy domněnky - spolu s globální teorií růstu a distribuce hodnot celých a meromorfních funkcí založenou na Nevanlinnově teorii. Tyto oblasti propojují komplexní analýzu s geometrií, dynamikou a diofantickou aproximací; Nevanlinnova teorie a její rozšíření zůstávají aktivní výzkumnou frontou.
nodes.brief:
Examines how holomorphic maps distort shape and measure — conformal and quasiconformal mappings, univalence, extremal length, and coefficient problems descending from the Bieberbach conjecture — alongside the global growth and value-distribution theory of entire and meromorphic functions built on Nevanlinna theory. Together these areas connect complex analysis to geometry, dynamics, and Diophantine approximation. Nevanlinna theory and its ramifications remain an active resear

### Concepts
- **Geometric function theory** (30Cxx) — Studies conformal and quasiconformal mappings, univalent functions, and the geometric distortion properties of holomorphic maps between planar domains.
- **Entire and meromorphic functions of one complex variable, and related topics** (30Dxx) — Studies the growth, factorization, and value distribution of entire and meromorphic functions via Nevanlinna theory and related methods.

nodes.name: Riemann Surfaces, the Disc, and Analytic Function Spaces
nodes.en: Covers Riemann surfaces as the natural domains of multi-valued and uniformized holomorphic functions, together with the Banach and Hilbert spaces and algebras of holomorphic functions — Hardy, Bergman, Dirichlet, and disc-algebra theory — built on the unit disc as the model domain. These areas connect complex analysis to functional analysis, operator theory, and uniformization; disc-space theory is classical, while the moduli-theoretic aspects of Riemann surfaces remain an active research area.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje Riemannovy plochy jako přirozený obor mnohoznačných a uniformizovaných holomorfních funkcí spolu s Banachovými a Hilbertovými prostory a algebrami holomorfních funkcí - Hardyho, Bergmanovou, Dirichletovou a diskovou algebrovou teorií - vystavěnými na jednotkovém kruhu jako modelové oblasti. Tyto oblasti propojují komplexní analýzu s funkcionální analýzou, teorií operátorů a uniformizací; teorie prostorů na disku je klasická, zatímco moduli-teoretické aspekty Riemannových ploch zůstávají aktivní výzkumnou oblastí.
nodes.brief:
Covers Riemann surfaces as the natural domains of multi-valued and uniformized holomorphic functions, together with the Banach and Hilbert spaces and algebras of holomorphic functions — Hardy, Bergman, Dirichlet, and disc-algebra theory — built on the unit disc as the model domain. These areas connect complex analysis to functional analysis, operator theory, and uniformization. The disc-space theory is classical while moduli-theoretic aspects of Riemann surfaces remain active

### Concepts
- **Riemann surfaces** (30Fxx) — Studies one-dimensional complex manifolds, their uniformization, coverings, and moduli, as the natural setting for multi-valued holomorphic functions.
- **Spaces and algebras of analytic functions of one complex variable** (30Hxx) — Studies Banach and Hilbert spaces and algebras of holomorphic functions, such as Hardy, Bergman, and Dirichlet spaces, and their operator-theoretic properties.
- **Function theory on the disc** (30Jxx) — Studies holomorphic functions on the unit disc, including boundary value behavior, inner-outer factorization, and Blaschke products.

nodes.name: Generalized, Universal, and Metric-Space Complex Analysis
nodes.en: Extends classical complex analysis beyond the holomorphic setting in three directions: generalized function theory embeds hyperfunctions and distributional boundary values into complex-analytic frameworks, universal holomorphic function theory studies functions whose partial sums, translates, or dilates approximate arbitrary targets, and analysis on metric spaces transplants conformal and quasiconformal methods to metric measure spaces lacking a linear structure. These are comparatively young, actively researched branches distinct from the classical one-variable theory in the sibling nodes.
nodes.cs: Rozšiřuje klasickou komplexní analýzu za holomorfní rámec ve třech směrech: teorie zobecněných funkcí vkládá hyperfunkce a distribuční okrajové hodnoty do komplexně analytických rámců, teorie univerzálních holomorfních funkcí zkoumá funkce, jejichž částečné součty, translace nebo dilatace aproximují libovolné cíle, a analýza na metrických prostorech přenáší konformní a kvazikonformní metody do obecných metrických prostorů s mírou postrádajících lineární strukturu. Jde o poměrně mladé, aktivně zkoumané obory odlišné od klasické jednorozměrné teorie v sourozeneckých uzlech.
nodes.brief:
Extends classical complex analysis beyond the holomorphic setting: generalized function theory embeds hyperfunctions and distributional boundary values into complex analytic frameworks, universal holomorphic function theory studies functions whose translates or dilates approximate arbitrary targets, and analysis on metric spaces transplants conformal and quasiconformal ideas to general metric measure spaces lacking a linear structure. These are comparatively young, actively r

### Concepts
- **Generalized function theory** (30Gxx) — Studies extensions of complex function theory to hyperfunctions, distributions, and other generalized-function frameworks.
- **Universal holomorphic functions of one complex variable** (30Kxx) — Studies holomorphic functions whose partial sums, translates, or dilates can approximate arbitrary functions, i.e. universality and hypercyclicity phenomena.
- **Analysis on metric spaces** (30Lxx) — Studies quasiconformal and conformally invariant analysis on general metric measure spaces, generalizing planar complex-analytic structures beyond the classical linear setting.

nodes.name: Potential theory
nodes.en: Potential theory studies harmonic and subharmonic functions, Green's functions, capacities, and the boundary-value problems they generate, from the classical Newtonian theory in Euclidean space through operator-generalized and sheaf-axiomatic formulations to potential theory on fractals and general metric measure spaces. Probabilistic potential theory (the potential theory of Markov processes) is classified separately under probability theory.
nodes.cs: Teorie potenciálu zkoumá harmonické a subharmonické funkce, Greenovy funkce, kapacity a jimi generované okrajové úlohy — od klasické newtonovské teorie v euklidovském prostoru přes zobecnění na jiné operátory a axiomatické (svazkové) formulace až po teorii potenciálu na fraktálech a obecných metrických prostorech s mírou. Pravděpodobnostní teorie potenciálu (potenciálová teorie markovských procesů) je klasifikována zvlášť v rámci teorie pravděpodobnosti.
nodes.brief:
Born from the Newtonian potential of gravitation and electrostatics in [[01.01]], potential theory studies harmonic and subharmonic functions, Green's functions, capacities, and the boundary-value problems they generate. Axiomatic and sheaf-theoretic formulations carry the classical Euclidean theory to fractals and general metric measure spaces.

It is the qualitative theory of the Laplace equation, hence a standing companion of the elliptic PDE theory in [[02.01.04.25]]; in the plane it is inseparable from the complex analysis of [[02.01.04.20]] and the pluripotential theory of [[02.01.04.22]]; and its probabilistic double — the potential theory of Markov processes, where Brownian motion solves the Dirichlet problem — is classified with [[02.01.04.44]].

nodes.name: Classical Euclidean Potential Theory
nodes.en: Studies harmonic and subharmonic functions, Green's functions, capacity, and boundary-value problems generated by the Laplacian in the plane and in higher-dimensional Euclidean space. This is the classical core of potential theory, born from Newtonian gravitation and nineteenth-century complex analysis, underlying the Dirichlet problem, harmonic measure, and superharmonic-function theory used throughout PDE and complex analysis; the planar case draws heavily on conformal mapping, while the higher-dimensional case relies on Newtonian-potential and capacity methods absent in two dimensions.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá harmonické a subharmonické funkce, Greenovy funkce, kapacitu a okrajové úlohy generované Laplaceovým operátorem v rovině i ve vícerozměrném euklidovském prostoru. Jde o klasické jádro teorie potenciálu, vzešlé z newtonovské gravitace a komplexní analýzy 19. století, na němž stojí Dirichletova úloha, harmonická míra a teorie superharmonických funkcí využívaná v parciálních diferenciálních rovnicích i komplexní analýze; rovinný případ silně čerpá z konformního zobrazení, zatímco vícerozměrný případ se opírá o newtonovský potenciál a kapacitní metody, které ve dvou rozměrech chybí.
nodes.brief:
Studies harmonic and subharmonic functions, Green's functions, capacity, and boundary-value problems generated by the Laplacian in the plane and in higher-dimensional Euclidean space. This is the classical core of potential theory, born from Newtonian gravitation and 19th-century complex analysis, and it underlies the Dirichlet problem, harmonic measure, and the theory of superharmonic functions used throughout PDE and complex analysis. Two-dimensional theory draws heavily on

### Concepts
- **Two-dimensional potential theory** (31Axx) — Studies harmonic, subharmonic, and superharmonic functions of two real variables and their associated Green's and Poisson kernels, exploiting the special link to complex analysis and conformal mapping.
- **Higher-dimensional potential theory** (31Bxx) — Extends classical potential theory to functions of three or more variables, treating Newtonian potentials, capacity, harmonic measure, and the Dirichlet problem for the Laplacian in Euclidean space.

nodes.name: Generalized and Axiomatic Potential Theory
nodes.en: Develops potential theory beyond the classical Laplacian by replacing harmonic functions with solutions of more general elliptic or parabolic operators, and by axiomatizing the essential structural properties — sheaf axioms, convergence axioms, balayage — that a theory of harmonic sheaves must satisfy. The axiomatic approach, pioneered by Brelot, Bauer, and Constantinescu-Cornea, unifies classical, Riesz, and other potential theories under a common sheaf-theoretic framework.
nodes.cs: Rozvíjí teorii potenciálu za hranice klasického Laplaceova operátoru tím, že harmonické funkce nahrazuje řešeními obecnějších eliptických nebo parabolických operátorů, a axiomatizuje základní strukturální vlastnosti — svazkové axiomy, axiomy konvergence, balayage —, které musí splňovat teorie harmonických svazků. Axiomatický přístup, jehož průkopníky byli Brelot, Bauer a Constantinescu-Cornea, sjednocuje klasickou, Rieszovu a další teorie potenciálu ve společném svazkově-teoretickém rámci.
nodes.brief:
Develops potential theory beyond the classical Laplacian by replacing harmonic functions with solutions of more general elliptic or parabolic operators, and by axiomatizing the essential structural properties (sheaf axioms, convergence axioms, balayage) that a theory of harmonic sheaves must satisfy. The axiomatic approach, pioneered by Brelot, Bauer, and Constantinescu-Cornea, unifies classical, Riesz, and more exotic potential theories under a common sheaf-theoretic framewo

### Concepts
- **Generalizations of potential theory** (31Cxx) — Covers potential-theoretic frameworks attached to operators other than the classical Laplacian, such as polyharmonic, Riesz, and pluripotential theories, and their associated capacities and kernels.
- **Axiomatic potential theory** (31Dxx) — Formulates potential theory abstractly via axioms on sheaves of harmonic functions (e.g. the Brelot and Bauer axiom systems), isolating the minimal structural hypotheses needed for a Green-function and boundary theory.

nodes.name: Potential Theory on Fractals and Metric Spaces
nodes.en: Constructs Dirichlet forms, Laplacians, and capacities on spaces lacking classical smooth structure, such as self-similar fractals, graphs, and general metric measure spaces satisfying doubling and Poincaré-type conditions. It generalizes harmonic analysis to settings like the Sierpiński gasket where differential-geometric tools fail, replacing them with resistance metrics and heat-kernel estimates, and remains a very active research area bridging analysis, probability, and fractal geometry.
nodes.cs: Konstruuje Dirichletovy formy, Laplaceovy operátory a kapacity na prostorech postrádajících klasickou hladkou strukturu, jako jsou samopodobné fraktály, grafy a obecné metrické prostory s mírou splňující zdvojující a Poincarého podmínky. Zobecňuje harmonickou analýzu na prostředí typu Sierpińského trojúhelníku, kde diferenciálně-geometrické nástroje selhávají, a nahrazuje je odporovými metrikami a odhady tepelného jádra; zůstává velmi aktivní oblastí výzkumu na pomezí analýzy, teorie pravděpodobnosti a fraktální geometrie.
nodes.brief:
Constructs Dirichlet forms, Laplacians, and capacities on spaces lacking classical smooth structure, such as self-similar fractals, graphs, and general metric measure spaces satisfying doubling and Poincaré-type conditions. It generalizes harmonic analysis to settings like the Sierpiński gasket where differential-geometric tools fail, replacing them with resistance metrics, heat-kernel estimates, and analysis on metric measure spaces. The field remains a very active research 

### Concepts
- **Potential theory on fractals and metric spaces** (31Exx) — Studies Laplacians, Dirichlet forms, capacities, and harmonic functions on fractal sets and general metric measure spaces, replacing smooth differential structure with resistance forms and heat-kernel methods.

nodes.name: Several complex variables and analytic spaces
nodes.en: Several complex variables and analytic spaces studies holomorphic functions and maps of more than one complex variable and the analytic spaces they define — power-series and continuation theory, local and global analytic geometry and singularities, pseudoconvexity and pluripotential theory, CR manifolds and their differential operators, compact complex manifolds and their deformations, and automorphism groups and automorphic functions. Infinite-dimensional holomorphy is classified separately under functional analysis.
nodes.cs: Teorie více komplexních proměnných a analytických prostorů zkoumá holomorfní funkce a zobrazení více než jedné komplexní proměnné a jimi definované analytické prostory — teorii mocninných řad a analytického prodloužení, lokální i globální analytickou geometrii a singularity, pseudokonvexitu a pluripotenciálovou teorii, CR-variety a jejich diferenciální operátory, kompaktní komplexní variety a jejich deformace i grupy automorfismů a automorfní funkce. Nekonečněrozměrná holomorfie je klasifikována zvlášť v rámci funkcionální analýzy.
nodes.brief:
In several complex variables, new phenomena replace the one-variable picture: domains of holomorphy, pseudoconvexity and the Levi problem govern where functions extend, while analytic spaces, singularities and deformation theory give the subject its geometric depth. CR manifolds and the d-bar equation connect function theory to geometry and PDE.

It generalizes the one-variable theory of [[02.01.04.20]] but leans harder on the pluripotential theory shared with [[02.01.04.21]] and on elliptic PDE methods from [[02.01.04.25]]. Compact complex manifolds and their moduli place it in constant exchange with the algebraic geometry of [[02.01.04.10]], and analysis on complex manifolds shades into [[02.01.04.43]].

nodes.name: Function Theory and Analytic Continuation
nodes.en: Covers the core function theory of several complex variables: power-series and integral representations, growth and boundary behavior of holomorphic functions, and phenomena such as Hartogs extension, where a holomorphic function on part of a domain is forced to extend to the whole. Also includes non-Archimedean analytic function theory, the valued-field analogue of classical holomorphy, typically studied alongside rigid and Berkovich analytic geometry.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje základní teorii funkcí více komplexních proměnných: reprezentace pomocí mocninných řad a integrálů, růst a okrajové chování holomorfních funkcí a jevy jako Hartogsovo rozšíření, kdy je holomorfní funkce definovaná na části oblasti nucena rozšířit se na celou oblast. Zahrnuje také nearchimédovskou teorii analytických funkcí, obdobu klasické holomorfie nad oceněnými tělesy, obvykle studovanou společně s rigidní a Berkovichovou analytickou geometrií.
nodes.brief:
Covers the core function theory of several complex variables: power-series and integral representations, growth and boundary behavior of holomorphic functions, and the phenomena — such as Hartogs extension — governing when a holomorphic function forced to extend beyond its original domain. Also includes non-Archimedean analytic function theory, the valued-field analogue of classical holomorphy, which is typically studied jointly with a companion classification tag. This block

### Concepts
- **Holomorphic functions of several complex variables** (32Axx) — Studies power series, integral representations, growth, and boundary behavior of holomorphic functions of several complex variables.
- **Analytic continuation** (32Dxx) — Studies extension of holomorphic functions and analytic objects beyond their initial domain of definition, including Hartogs-type forced-extension phenomena.
- **Non-Archimedean analysis** (32Pxx) — Studies analytic function theory over non-Archimedean valued fields, the several-variable analogue of rigid and Berkovich analytic geometry.

nodes.name: Local Analytic Geometry, Analytic Spaces and Singularities
nodes.en: Studies the local and global structure of analytic sets and spaces: germs of analytic varieties near a point, the ringed-space formalism of general analytic spaces, generalized variants of that formalism such as Banach-analytic or formal spaces, and the singular points where analytic varieties fail to be manifolds. Singularity theory remains a vigorous research area linking local algebra, the topology of links, and resolution techniques to algebraic geometry.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá lokální i globální strukturu analytických množin a prostorů: zárodky analytických variet v bodě, formalismus okroužkovaných prostorů obecných analytických prostorů, jeho zobecněné varianty jako banachovsko-analytické či formální prostory a singulární body, v nichž analytické variety přestávají být varietami. Teorie singularit zůstává živou výzkumnou oblastí propojující lokální algebru, topologii linků a rozlišovací (resoluční) techniky s algebraickou geometrií.
nodes.brief:
Studies the local and global structure of analytic sets and spaces: germs of analytic varieties near a point, the ringed-space formalism of general analytic spaces, generalized (e.g. Banach or formal) variants of that formalism, and the singular points where analytic varieties fail to be manifolds. Singularity theory in particular remains a vigorous research area, linking local algebra, topology of links, and resolution techniques to algebraic geometry. Together these subclas

### Concepts
- **Local analytic geometry** (32Bxx) — Studies the local structure of analytic sets and germs of analytic spaces via Weierstrass preparation and local rings of holomorphic functions.
- **Analytic spaces** (32Cxx) — Develops the general theory of analytic spaces as ringed spaces locally modeled on analytic sets, admitting nilpotent structure sheaves.
- **Generalizations of analytic spaces** (32Kxx) — Studies generalized notions of analytic space, such as Banach-analytic or formal variants, extending the classical local-ringed-space formalism.
- **Complex singularities** (32Sxx) — Studies singular points of complex analytic varieties, their resolution, the topology of their links, and invariants such as Milnor fibers.

nodes.name: Convexity, Pseudoconvex Domains and Pluripotential Theory
nodes.en: Studies the convexity notions unique to several complex variables: holomorphic convexity and the Levi problem, geometric and metric formulations of convexity, and the pseudoconvex domains that form the natural setting for holomorphic extension. Pluripotential theory extends classical potential theory via plurisubharmonic functions and the complex Monge-Ampère operator, underpinning modern work on Kähler-Einstein metrics and singular geometry.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá pojmy konvexity specifické pro více komplexních proměnných: holomorfní konvexitu a Leviho problém, geometrické a metrické formulace konvexity a pseudokonvexní oblasti, které tvoří přirozené prostředí pro holomorfní rozšíření. Pluripotenciálová teorie rozšiřuje klasickou teorii potenciálu pomocí plurisubharmonických funkcí a komplexního Monge-Ampèrova operátoru a tvoří základ moderní práce o Kählerových-Einsteinových metrikách a singulární geometrii.
nodes.brief:
Studies the convexity notions unique to several complex variables: holomorphic convexity and the Levi problem, geometric and metric formulations of convexity, and the pseudoconvex domains that are the natural setting for holomorphic extension. Pluripotential theory extends classical potential theory via plurisubharmonic functions and the complex Monge-Ampère operator, underpinning modern work on Kähler-Einstein metrics and singular geometry. The interplay between domain geome

### Concepts
- **Holomorphic convexity** (32Exx) — Studies domains and spaces that are holomorphically convex, including domains of holomorphy and the Levi problem.
- **Geometric convexity in several complex variables** (32Fxx) — Studies geometric and metric formulations of convexity, including curvature characterizations, specific to several complex variables.
- **Pseudoconvex domains** (32Txx) — Studies domains characterized by pseudoconvexity, the analytic condition governing holomorphic extendability and boundary regularity.
- **Pluripotential theory** (32Uxx) — Studies plurisubharmonic functions, complex Monge-Ampère operators, and pluripolar sets as the several-variable analogue of potential theory.

nodes.name: CR Manifolds and Differential Operators
nodes.en: Studies Cauchy-Riemann manifolds — the real hypersurfaces arising as boundaries of complex domains — together with the intrinsic CR structures they carry, and the differential operators, chiefly the d-bar operator and the d-bar-Neumann problem, used to solve extension and regularity questions on them. CR embeddability and sharp subelliptic estimates for these operators remain open in many settings, linking the boundary geometry of pseudoconvex domains to microlocal analysis.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá Cauchyho-Riemannovy variety — reálné nadplochy vznikající jako hranice komplexních oblastí — spolu s jejich vnitřní CR-strukturou a diferenciálními operátory, především operátorem d-bar a d-bar-Neumannovým problémem, používanými k řešení otázek rozšíření a regularity na těchto varietách. CR-vnořitelnost a ostré subeliptické odhady pro tyto operátory zůstávají v mnoha případech otevřené a propojují hraniční geometrii pseudokonvexních oblastí s mikrolokální analýzou.
nodes.brief:
Studies Cauchy-Riemann manifolds — the real hypersurfaces that arise as boundaries of complex domains — together with the intrinsic CR structures they carry, and the differential operators, chiefly the ∂-bar operator and the ∂-bar-Neumann problem, used to solve extension and regularity questions on them. CR embeddability and sharp subelliptic estimates for these operators remain genuinely open in many settings. This block bridges the boundary geometry of pseudoconvex domains 

### Concepts
- **CR manifolds** (32Vxx) — Studies Cauchy-Riemann manifolds, the real hypersurfaces bounding complex domains together with their intrinsic CR structure.
- **Differential operators in several variables** (32Wxx) — Studies the ∂-bar operator, the ∂-bar-Neumann problem, and other differential operators intrinsic to several-complex-variable analysis.

nodes.name: Global Geometry: Compact Spaces, Complex Manifolds and Deformations
nodes.en: Studies complex objects in the large: compact analytic spaces and their classification, the differential-geometric and topological properties of complex manifolds including Kähler geometry, and the deformation theory governing families and moduli of complex structures via Kodaira-Spencer theory and period maps. Moduli and deformation questions remain a highly active research front connecting complex geometry to algebraic geometry and mathematical physics.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá komplexní objekty v celku: kompaktní analytické prostory a jejich klasifikaci, diferenciálně-geometrické a topologické vlastnosti komplexních variet včetně Kählerovy geometrie a deformační teorii řídící rodiny a moduli komplexních struktur prostřednictvím Kodairovy-Spencerovy teorie a periodových zobrazení. Otázky moduli a deformací zůstávají velmi aktivní výzkumnou frontou spojující komplexní geometrii s algebraickou geometrií a matematickou fyzikou.
nodes.brief:
Studies complex objects in the large: compact analytic spaces and their classification, the differential-geometric and topological properties of complex manifolds including Kähler geometry, and the deformation theory that governs families and moduli of complex structures via Kodaira-Spencer theory and period maps. Moduli and deformation questions remain a highly active research front connecting complex geometry to algebraic geometry and mathematical physics. This block captur

### Concepts
- **Deformations of analytic structures** (32Gxx) — Studies families and moduli of complex analytic structures via Kodaira-Spencer deformation theory and period maps.
- **Compact analytic spaces** (32Jxx) — Studies compact complex analytic spaces and manifolds, their classification, and cohomological invariants.
- **Complex manifolds** (32Qxx) — Studies the differential-geometric and topological properties of complex manifolds, including Kähler geometry and curvature.

nodes.name: Holomorphic Mappings, Fiber Spaces and Automorphism Groups
nodes.en: Studies holomorphic maps and correspondences between complex spaces and their iteration and value-distribution behavior, holomorphic fiber bundles and the sheaf cohomology used to classify them, complex spaces distinguished by a large automorphism group such as homogeneous and symmetric spaces, and automorphic functions invariant under discrete group actions that generalize classical automorphic forms.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá holomorfní zobrazení a korespondence mezi komplexními prostory a jejich iterační a hodnotově-distribuční chování, holomorfní vláknové svazky a svazkovou kohomologii používanou k jejich klasifikaci, komplexní prostory vyznačující se velkou grupou automorfismů, jako jsou homogenní a symetrické prostory, a automorfní funkce invariantní vůči působení diskrétních grup, které zobecňují klasické automorfní formy.
nodes.brief:
Studies holomorphic maps and correspondences between complex spaces and their iteration and value-distribution behavior, holomorphic fiber bundles and the sheaf cohomology used to classify them, complex spaces distinguished by a large automorphism group such as homogeneous and symmetric spaces, and automorphic functions invariant under discrete group actions that generalize classical automorphic forms. Fiber-bundle and mapping theory here rest on well-established classical fo

### Concepts
- **Holomorphic mappings and correspondences** (32Hxx) — Studies holomorphic maps and multivalued correspondences between complex spaces, including their fixed points, iteration, and value distribution.
- **Holomorphic fiber spaces** (32Lxx) — Studies holomorphic fiber bundles and fiber spaces, including line and vector bundles and sheaf cohomology over complex spaces.
- **Complex spaces with a group of automorphisms** (32Mxx) — Studies complex manifolds and spaces equipped with a large or transitive automorphism group, including homogeneous and symmetric spaces.
- **Automorphic functions** (32Nxx) — Studies functions invariant under discrete groups acting on complex domains, generalizing classical automorphic forms to several variables.

nodes.name: Special functions
nodes.en: Special functions studies the named classes of functions — elementary transcendental functions, hypergeometric and q-hypergeometric families, and the remaining catalogued special functions — that arise as solutions of classical differential and difference equations and serve as the standard vocabulary of applied and mathematical analysis. Orthogonal-polynomial theory, combinatorial special functions, and number-theoretic special functions are classified separately under their own disciplines.
nodes.cs: Speciální funkce zkoumají pojmenované třídy funkcí — elementární transcendentní funkce, hypergeometrické a q-hypergeometrické rodiny a ostatní katalogizované speciální funkce —, které vznikají jako řešení klasických diferenciálních a diferenčních rovnic a tvoří standardní slovník aplikované i matematické analýzy. Teorie ortogonálních polynomů, kombinatorické speciální funkce a číselně-teoretické speciální funkce jsou klasifikovány zvlášť v rámci vlastních oborů.
nodes.brief:
Special functions are the named workhorses of analysis — gamma, Bessel, hypergeometric and q-hypergeometric families and their kin — arising as solutions of the classical differential and difference equations and serving as the standard vocabulary in which applied mathematics and [[01.01]] write their answers. Identities, asymptotics and integral representations are the pillar's stock in trade.

Most of its objects are born in the ODEs of [[02.01.04.24]], including the complex-domain theory that produces the Painlevé transcendents; they appear in the transform calculus of [[02.01.04.32]] and the kernels of [[02.01.04.33]], and expand functions in the orthogonal systems of [[02.01.04.30]]. Orthogonal-polynomial, combinatorial and number-theoretic special functions are delegated to their home disciplines, including [[02.01.04.07]] and [[02.01.04.04]].

nodes.name: Elementary and Classical Transcendental Functions
nodes.en: Studies the trigonometric, exponential, logarithmic, gamma, beta, and elliptic-type elementary transcendental functions that form the bedrock vocabulary of analysis. These functions arise as solutions of the simplest differential and functional equations and underlie virtually every other class of special function; their identities, asymptotics, and functional equations were largely settled in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries but remain indispensable computational and modeling tools today.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá trigonometrické, exponenciální, logaritmické, gama, beta a eliptické elementární transcendentní funkce, které tvoří základní slovník analýzy. Tyto funkce vznikají jako řešení nejjednodušších diferenciálních a funkcionálních rovnic a stojí v základu prakticky každé další třídy speciálních funkcí; jejich identity, asymptotiky a funkcionální rovnice byly z velké části ustáleny v 18. a 19. století, ale zůstávají nepostradatelným výpočetním a modelovacím nástrojem dodnes.
nodes.brief:
Studies the trigonometric, exponential, logarithmic, gamma, beta, and elliptic-type elementary transcendental functions that form the bedrock vocabulary of analysis. These functions arise as solutions of the simplest differential and functional equations and underlie virtually every other class of special function. Their identities, asymptotics, and functional equations were largely settled in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries but remain indispensable computational and 

### Concepts
- **Elementary classical functions** (33Bxx) — The class of trigonometric, exponential, logarithmic, gamma, beta, and related elementary transcendental functions together with their identities and functional equations.

nodes.name: Hypergeometric and Basic (q-) Hypergeometric Functions
nodes.en: Covers the Gauss hypergeometric function, its generalizations (Appell, Lauricella, and generalized pFq series), and their q-deformed basic hypergeometric analogues, which encode solutions to broad families of linear differential and difference equations. These series unify orthogonal polynomials, combinatorial identities, and integral transforms under a single algebraic framework; the classical theory is mature, but q-series continue to drive active research linking them to quantum groups and modular forms.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje Gaussovu hypergeometrickou funkci, její zobecnění (Appellovy, Lauricellovy a zobecněné pFq řady) a jejich q-deformované bazické hypergeometrické analogy, které kódují řešení širokých tříd lineárních diferenciálních a diferenčních rovnic. Tyto řady sjednocují ortogonální polynomy, kombinatorické identity a integrální transformace v jednotném algebraickém rámci; klasická teorie je vyzrálá, ale q-řady nadále pohánějí aktivní výzkum propojující je s kvantovými grupami a modulárními formami.
nodes.brief:
Covers the Gauss hypergeometric function, its generalizations (Appell, Lauricella, and generalized pFq series), and their q-deformed basic hypergeometric analogues, which encode solutions to broad families of linear differential and difference equations. These series unify orthogonal polynomials, combinatorial identities, and integral transforms under a single algebraic framework, with q-series linking to quantum groups and modular forms. The classical theory is mature, but q

### Concepts
- **Hypergeometric functions** (33Cxx) — Functions defined by hypergeometric series and their generalizations (Gauss, Appell, Lauricella, generalized pFq), satisfying linear differential equations with regular singular points.
- **Basic hypergeometric functions** (33Dxx) — The q-analogue theory of hypergeometric functions, built from basic (q-)series and satisfying q-difference equations, connecting to quantum algebra and modular forms.

nodes.name: Other Special Functions and Their Computation
nodes.en: Covers special functions falling outside the elementary and hypergeometric hierarchies — Bessel-type functions, Airy functions, Painlevé transcendents, and other functions defined by specific differential, integral, or functional equations (MSC 33Exx) — together with the numerical and symbolic algorithms, error bounds, and software strategies used to evaluate them reliably across parameter regimes (MSC 33Fxx). The catalogue of named functions is fixed by convention; the open work is computational: stable evaluation near singularities and in extreme parameter ranges.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje speciální funkce mimo elementární a hypergeometrickou hierarchii — funkce besselovského typu, Airyho funkce, Painlevého transcendenty a další funkce definované konkrétními diferenciálními, integrálními nebo funkcionálními rovnicemi (MSC 33Exx) — spolu s numerickými a symbolickými algoritmy, odhady chyb a softwarovými strategiemi pro jejich spolehlivý výpočet napříč parametrickými oblastmi (MSC 33Fxx). Katalog pojmenovaných funkcí je ustálený konvencí; otevřenou úlohou zůstává výpočetní stránka: stabilní vyhodnocení v blízkosti singularit a v extrémních rozsazích parametrů.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses special functions outside the elementary and hypergeometric hierarchies, such as Bessel-type, Airy, Painleve transcendents, and other functions defined by specific differential, integral, or functional equations, together with the numerical and symbolic methods used to evaluate them reliably. Computational aspects address algorithms, error bounds, and software strategies for stable evaluation across parameter regimes. The catalogue of named functions is well estab

### Concepts
- **Other special functions** (33Exx) — Special functions not classified as elementary or hypergeometric, including Bessel-type functions, Painleve transcendents, and other functions arising from specific differential or integral equations.
- **Computational aspects of special functions** (33Fxx) — The design and analysis of numerical and symbolic algorithms for evaluating special functions accurately and efficiently across their parameter domains.

nodes.name: Ordinary differential equations
nodes.en: Studies equations relating a function of one variable to its derivatives: existence, uniqueness and long-term qualitative behavior of solutions (equilibria, limit cycles, stability, bifurcation), extensions to stochastic, infinite-dimensional, delayed, and time-scale settings, and the operator-theoretic and complex-analytic treatment of linear and singular equations. Distinguished from partial differential equations by a single independent variable.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá rovnice vztahující funkci jedné proměnné k jejím derivacím: existenci, jednoznačnost a dlouhodobé kvalitativní chování řešení (rovnovážné body, limitní cykly, stabilitu, bifurkace), rozšíření do stochastického, nekonečně-dimenzionálního, zpožděného prostředí a prostředí s časovými škálami, a operátorové a komplexně-analytické zpracování lineárních a singulárních rovnic. Od parciálních diferenciálních rovnic se liší jedinou nezávislou proměnnou.
nodes.brief:
Ordinary differential equations relate a function of one variable to its derivatives, and the pillar covers the full arc: existence and uniqueness, qualitative behavior — equilibria, limit cycles, stability, bifurcation — extensions to stochastic, delayed, infinite-dimensional and time-scale settings, and the operator-theoretic (Sturm-Liouville) and complex-domain wings.

The qualitative theory flows directly into the dynamical systems of [[02.01.04.26]]; the spectral wing into the operator theory of [[02.01.04.35]]; the complex-domain wing produces the special functions of [[02.01.04.23]]. With several independent variables the subject becomes the PDE theory of [[02.01.04.25]]. As the mathematical form of deterministic evolution laws, ODEs are the first modeling language of [[01.01]] and of the control systems in [[02.01.04.50]].

nodes.name: Foundational, Boundary-Value, and Qualitative Theory
nodes.en: The classical core of ODE analysis: existence, uniqueness, and continuation of solutions; behavior under two-point or multi-point boundary constraints; the geometric long-term structure of trajectories (phase portraits, limit cycles, bifurcations); sensitivity to perturbation via stability theory; and asymptotic approximation of solutions near singularities or extreme parameter regimes. This settled, textbook-level mathematics forms the substrate every other ODE subfield builds on.
nodes.cs: Klasické jádro teorie ODR: existence, jednoznačnost a prodloužení řešení; chování za dvou- či vícebodových okrajových podmínek; geometrická dlouhodobá struktura trajektorií (fázové portréty, limitní cykly, bifurkace); citlivost na poruchy prostřednictvím teorie stability; a asymptotická aproximace řešení v blízkosti singularit či v extrémních rozsazích parametrů. Jde o ustálenou, učebnicovou matematiku, na níž stojí každá další podoblast teorie ODR.
nodes.brief:
The classical core of ODE analysis: existence, uniqueness, and continuation of solutions; behavior under two-point or multi-point boundary constraints; the geometric long-term structure of trajectories (phase portraits, limit cycles, bifurcations); sensitivity to perturbation via stability theory; and asymptotic approximation of solutions near singularities or extreme parameter regimes. This is settled, textbook-level mathematics forming the substrate on which every other ODE

### Concepts
- **General theory for ordinary differential equations** (34Axx) — Establishes existence, uniqueness, continuation, and elementary solution techniques for scalar and vector ODEs, including Picard–Lindelöf and Peano-type theorems.
- **Boundary value problems for ordinary differential equations** (34Bxx) — Studies existence, uniqueness, and multiplicity of solutions to ODEs subject to conditions imposed at two or more points, including Sturm–Liouville and nonlinear eigenvalue problems.
- **Qualitative theory for ordinary differential equations** (34Cxx) — Investigates the geometric and long-term behavior of solutions—phase portraits, periodic orbits, limit cycles, and bifurcations—without requiring explicit integration.
- **Stability theory for ordinary differential equations** (34Dxx) — Analyzes how solutions respond to perturbations of initial data or parameters using Lyapunov functions, linearization, and related stability criteria.
- **Asymptotic theory for ordinary differential equations** (34Exx) — Develops perturbation methods, WKB approximations, and singular-perturbation techniques to describe solution behavior near singularities or in limiting parameter regimes.

nodes.name: Generalized, Stochastic, and Applied ODE Frameworks
nodes.en: Extensions of the ordinary differential equation framework to richer settings that interface with neighboring fields: equations perturbed by randomness, equations valued in infinite-dimensional abstract spaces, equations with control inputs for engineering applications, equations with memory or delay, and dynamics unified across continuous and discrete time via time scales. Each subfield imports methods from a bordering discipline — probability, functional analysis, control theory, or combinatorics — to extend the classical theory beyond its original scope.
nodes.cs: Rozšíření rámce obyčejných diferenciálních rovnic do bohatších prostředí na rozhraní se sousedními obory: rovnice narušené náhodností, rovnice s hodnotami v nekonečně-dimenzionálních abstraktních prostorech, rovnice s řídícími vstupy pro inženýrské aplikace, rovnice s pamětí či zpožděním a dynamika sjednocující spojitý a diskrétní čas prostřednictvím časových škál. Každá podoblast přejímá metody ze sousedního oboru — teorie pravděpodobnosti, funkcionální analýzy, teorie řízení nebo kombinatoriky — a tím rozšiřuje klasickou teorii nad její původní rozsah.
nodes.brief:
Extensions of the ordinary differential equation framework to richer settings that interface with neighboring fields: equations perturbed by randomness, equations valued in infinite-dimensional abstract spaces, equations with control inputs for engineering applications, equations with memory or delay, and dynamics unified across continuous and discrete time via time scales. Each subfield imports methods from a bordering discipline—probability, functional analysis, control the

### Concepts
- **Ordinary differential equations and systems with randomness** (34Fxx) — Extends deterministic ODE theory to equations whose coefficients, inputs, or driving terms are random, bridging to stochastic analysis.
- **Differential equations in abstract spaces** (34Gxx) — Generalizes ODEs to equations for functions valued in Banach or Hilbert spaces, unifying finite-dimensional ODE theory with functional-analytic and PDE methods.
- **Control problems including ordinary differential equations** (34Hxx) — Studies ODE systems driven by control inputs, addressing controllability, optimal control, and feedback stabilization.
- **Functional-differential equations** (34Kxx) — Covers equations whose evolution depends on past, future, or state-dependent history values, including delay, advanced, and neutral differential equations.
- **Dynamic equations on time scales or measure chains** (34Nxx) — Unifies continuous ODE theory and discrete difference-equation theory by formulating dynamics on arbitrary closed subsets of the real line.

nodes.name: Differential Operators and the Complex Domain
nodes.en: The operator-theoretic and complex-analytic wings of ODE theory. 34Lxx treats linear differential expressions as operators on function spaces, developing spectral theory and eigenvalue distribution results in the tradition of Sturm–Liouville theory. 34Mxx studies equations whose independent variable ranges over the complex plane, where singularity structure, monodromy, and the Painlevé property govern solution behavior and give rise to special transcendental functions.
nodes.cs: Operátorové a komplexně-analytické křídlo teorie obyčejných diferenciálních rovnic. Třída 34Lxx pojednává lineární diferenciální výrazy jako operátory na prostorech funkcí a rozvíjí spektrální teorii a výsledky o rozdělení vlastních čísel v tradici Sturmovy–Liouvilleovy teorie. Třída 34Mxx zkoumá rovnice, jejichž nezávisle proměnná probíhá komplexní rovinou, kde chování řešení řídí struktura singularit, monodromie a Painlevého vlastnost a kde vznikají speciální transcendentní funkce.
nodes.brief:
The operator-theoretic and complex-analytic wings of ODE theory. 34Lxx treats linear differential expressions as operators on function spaces, developing spectral theory and eigenvalue distribution results in the tradition of Sturm–Liouville theory. 34Mxx studies equations whose independent variable ranges over the complex plane, where singularity structure, monodromy, and the Painlevé property govern solution behavior and give rise to special transcendental functions.

### Concepts
- **Ordinary differential operators** (34Lxx) — Studies linear differential expressions as operators on function spaces, including self-adjointness, spectral theory, and eigenvalue asymptotics.
- **Ordinary differential equations in the complex domain** (34Mxx) — Studies ODEs with complex independent variable, focusing on singularity analysis, monodromy, the Painlevé property, and associated special functions.

nodes.name: Partial differential equations
nodes.en: Studies equations relating a function of several variables to its partial derivatives: general existence, uniqueness and regularity theory; classification by algebraic structure (constant-coefficient, first-order, higher-order) and by principal part (elliptic, parabolic, hyperbolic, and mixed types); spectral and overdetermined boundary-value problems; and applications spanning mathematical physics and microlocal analysis via pseudodifferential and Fourier-integral operators. Distinguished from ordinary differential equations by multiple independent variables.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá rovnice vztahující funkci více proměnných k jejím parciálním derivacím: obecnou teorii existence, jednoznačnosti a regularity; klasifikaci podle algebraické struktury (rovnice s konstantními koeficienty, obecné rovnice prvního řádu, rovnice vyššího řádu) a podle hlavní části (eliptický, parabolický, hyperbolický a smíšený typ); spektrální a přeurčené okrajové úlohy; a aplikace sahající od matematické fyziky po mikrolokální analýzu prostřednictvím pseudodiferenciálních a Fourierových integrálních operátorů. Od obyčejných diferenciálních rovnic se liší více nezávislými proměnnými.
