nodes.brief:
Partial differential equations govern functions of several variables through their partial derivatives — and thereby most of the continuum models of [[01.01]]: heat, waves, fluids, electromagnetism, quantum mechanics. The pillar spans existence, uniqueness and regularity theory, the elliptic-parabolic-hyperbolic trichotomy by principal part, spectral and boundary-value problems, and the microlocal machinery of pseudodifferential and Fourier-integral operators.

Its estimates lean on the harmonic analysis of [[02.01.04.30]]; elliptic theory converses with the potential theory of [[02.01.04.21]]; operator-theoretic formulations live beside [[02.01.04.35]]; intrinsic formulations on manifolds belong to [[02.01.04.43]]; and variational formulations tie it to [[02.01.04.36]]. With one independent variable the subject reduces to [[02.01.04.24]].

nodes.name: Foundations and General Theory
nodes.en: Covers the field's cross-cutting methodological apparatus: general existence/uniqueness frameworks, the qualitative behavior of solutions (regularity, asymptotics, comparison principles), explicit and integral representation formulas, and the various notions of generalized (weak, distributional, viscosity) solutions that make modern PDE theory rigorous. These four subclasses supply the vocabulary and toolkit every other block in 35-XX draws on, rather than studying a single equation type of their own.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá průřezový metodologický aparát oboru: obecné rámce existence a jednoznačnosti, kvalitativní chování řešení (regularitu, asymptotiku, srovnávací principy), explicitní a integrální reprezentační vzorce a různá pojetí zobecněného (slabého, distribučního, viskózního) řešení, která činí moderní teorii PDR rigorózní. Tyto čtyři podtřídy dodávají slovník a nástroje, z nichž čerpá každý další blok v 35-XX, aniž by samy studovaly jediný konkrétní typ rovnice.
nodes.brief:
Covers the field's cross-cutting methodological apparatus: general existence/uniqueness frameworks, the qualitative behavior of solutions (regularity, asymptotics, comparison principles), explicit and integral representation formulas, and the various notions of generalized (weak, distributional, viscosity) solutions that make modern PDE theory rigorous. These four subclasses supply the vocabulary and toolkit that every other block in 35-XX draws on rather than studying a sing

### Concepts
- **General topics in partial differential equations** (35Axx) — Covers foundational and cross-cutting questions in PDE theory not tied to a specific equation type, such as general existence, uniqueness, and well-posedness frameworks.
- **Qualitative properties of solutions to partial differential equations** (35Bxx) — Studies structural behavior of PDE solutions — regularity, stability, asymptotics, maximum principles, blow-up, and comparison results — independent of explicit formulas.
- **Representations of solutions to partial differential equations** (35Cxx) — Develops explicit formulas, integral representations, series expansions, and transform methods that express solutions of PDEs in closed or semi-closed form.
- **Generalized solutions to partial differential equations** (35Dxx) — Formalizes weak, distributional, viscosity, and other generalized solution concepts needed when classical differentiable solutions fail to exist.

nodes.name: Structural Classification by Order and Coefficients
nodes.en: Organizes PDEs and systems by their algebraic and differential-order structure rather than by analytic type: equations with constant coefficients (amenable to Fourier and algebraic methods), general first-order equations and systems (the domain of characteristics and Hamilton–Jacobi theory), and general higher-order equations lacking a clean elliptic/parabolic/hyperbolic classification. This block sits between the general foundational tools and the classical type-based theories that follow.
nodes.cs: Třídí parciální diferenciální rovnice a jejich systémy podle algebraické a diferenciálně-řádové struktury, nikoli podle analytického typu: rovnice s konstantními koeficienty (přístupné Fourierovým a algebraickým metodám), obecné rovnice a systémy prvního řádu (doména charakteristik a Hamiltonovy–Jacobiho teorie) a obecné rovnice vyššího řádu, jimž chybí čisté eliptické, parabolické či hyperbolické zařazení. Tento blok stojí mezi obecnými základními nástroji a následujícími klasickými teoriemi podle typu.
nodes.brief:
Organizes PDEs and systems by their algebraic and differential-order structure rather than by analytic type: equations with constant coefficients (amenable to Fourier and algebraic methods), general first-order equations and systems (the domain of characteristics and Hamilton–Jacobi theory), and general higher-order equations lacking a clean elliptic/parabolic/hyperbolic classification. This block sits between the general foundational tools and the classical type-based theori

### Concepts
- **Partial differential equations and systems of partial differential equations with constant coefficients** (35Exx) — Studies PDEs and systems whose coefficients are constant, a setting where Fourier and algebraic techniques yield sharp existence and structure results.
- **General first-order partial differential equations and systems of first-order partial differential equations** (35Fxx) — Treats general first-order PDEs and systems, including quasilinear and fully nonlinear cases solved via the method of characteristics and Hamilton–Jacobi theory.
- **General higher-order partial differential equations and systems of higher-order partial differential equations** (35Gxx) — Covers general PDEs and systems of order three or higher that do not fall neatly into the elliptic, parabolic, or hyperbolic classification.

nodes.name: Classical Equation Types by Principal Part
nodes.en: Encompasses the canonical trichotomy of PDE theory — elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic equations and systems — together with the borderline close-to-elliptic and mixed-type classes that interpolate between them. Classification by the principal symbol determines the qualitative behavior (smoothing, propagation, equilibrium) and the appropriate function-space and boundary/initial-value framework for each equation, making this the analytic backbone of the discipline.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje kanonickou trichotomii teorie parciálních diferenciálních rovnic — eliptické, parabolické a hyperbolické rovnice a systémy — spolu s hraničními třídami blízkými eliptickým a se smíšenými typy, které mezi nimi interpolují. Klasifikace podle hlavního symbolu určuje kvalitativní chování (vyhlazování, šíření, rovnováhu) i vhodný rámec funkčních prostorů a okrajových či počátečních úloh pro každou rovnici, což z tohoto bloku činí analytickou páteř disciplíny.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses the canonical trichotomy of PDE theory — elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic equations and systems — together with the borderline close-to-elliptic and mixed-type classes that interpolate between them. Classification by the principal symbol determines the qualitative behavior (smoothing, propagation, equilibrium) and the appropriate function-space and boundary/initial-value framework for each equation, making this the analytic backbone of the discipline.

### Concepts
- **Close-to-elliptic equations** (35Hxx) — Studies equations and systems that fail to be strictly elliptic but retain elliptic-like structure, such as subelliptic and Hörmander-type operators.
- **Elliptic equations and elliptic systems** (35Jxx) — Studies equations and systems characterized by a positive-definite principal symbol, governing equilibrium phenomena and boundary-value problems.
- **Parabolic equations and parabolic systems** (35Kxx) — Studies equations and systems combining an elliptic spatial part with first-order time evolution, modeling diffusion and smoothing processes.
- **Hyperbolic equations and hyperbolic systems** (35Lxx) — Studies equations and systems governing wave propagation with finite speed, characterized by real, generally simple characteristics.
- **Partial differential equations of mixed type and mixed-type systems of partial differential equations** (35Mxx) — Studies equations and systems whose type (elliptic, parabolic, or hyperbolic) changes across the domain, such as Tricomi-type equations.

nodes.name: Spectral and Overdetermined Problems
nodes.en: Addresses two special problem structures layered on top of a given PDE: overdetermined boundary-value problems, where more conditions are imposed than a generic solution can satisfy — forcing rigidity results about the domain or coefficients — and spectral/eigenvalue theory, which studies the discrete or continuous spectrum of differential operators and underlies separation-of-variables and stability analysis. Both connect PDE theory tightly to operator theory and geometric rigidity results.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se dvěma zvláštními typy úloh nadstavěnými nad danou PDR: přeurčenými okrajovými úlohami, kde je kladeno více podmínek, než obecné řešení může splňovat — což vynucuje výsledky o rigiditě oboru či koeficientů — a spektrální/vlastní teorií, jež zkoumá diskrétní či spojité spektrum diferenciálních operátorů a je základem separace proměnných a analýzy stability. Obě témata úzce propojují teorii PDR s operátorovou teorií a výsledky o geometrické rigiditě.
nodes.brief:
Addresses two special problem structures layered on top of a given PDE: overdetermined boundary-value problems, where more conditions are imposed than a generic solution can satisfy — forcing rigidity results about the domain or coefficients — and spectral/eigenvalue theory, which studies the discrete or continuous spectrum of differential operators and underlies separation-of-variables and stability analysis. Both connect PDE theory tightly to operator theory and geometric r

### Concepts
- **Overdetermined problems for partial differential equations and systems of partial differential equations** (35Nxx) — Studies PDE boundary-value problems with more prescribed conditions than a generic solution admits, typically yielding rigidity or symmetry results on the domain.
- **Spectral theory and eigenvalue problems for partial differential equations** (35Pxx) — Studies eigenvalues, eigenfunctions, and spectral decompositions of differential operators arising from PDEs, connecting analysis to operator theory.

nodes.name: Applications, Generalized Operators, and Miscellany
nodes.en: Gathers PDEs organized by their origin in mathematical physics and other applied fields, the operator-theoretic generalization of differential operators to pseudodifferential and Fourier-integral operators, and a residual class of topics that resist classification elsewhere in 35-XX. This block is where the classical PDE toolkit meets modern microlocal analysis and concrete physical modeling.
nodes.cs: Shromažďuje parciální diferenciální rovnice tříděné podle původu v matematické fyzice a dalších aplikovaných oborech, operátorové zobecnění diferenciálních operátorů na pseudodiferenciální a Fourierovy integrální operátory a zbytkovou třídu témat, jež se vzpírají zařazení jinam v rámci 35-XX. V tomto bloku se klasický aparát parciálních diferenciálních rovnic potkává s moderní mikrolokální analýzou a konkrétním fyzikálním modelováním.
nodes.brief:
Gathers PDEs organized by their origin in mathematical physics and other applied fields, the operator-theoretic generalization of differential operators to pseudodifferential and Fourier-integral operators, and a residual class of topics that resist classification elsewhere in 35-XX. This block is where the classical PDE toolkit meets modern microlocal analysis and concrete physical modeling.

### Concepts
- **Partial differential equations of mathematical physics and other areas of application** (35Qxx) — Covers named equations arising from physical and applied models — such as Navier–Stokes, Schrödinger, Maxwell, and Einstein equations — studied for their specific physical content.
- **Miscellaneous topics in partial differential equations** (35Rxx) — Covers PDE topics such as equations on infinite-dimensional spaces, stochastic and fractional PDEs, and other subjects not captured by the other 35-XX subclasses.
- **Pseudodifferential operators and other generalizations of partial differential operators** (35Sxx) — Studies pseudodifferential, Fourier-integral, and other generalized operators that extend differential operators via microlocal and symbol calculus techniques.

nodes.name: Dynamical systems and ergodic theory
nodes.en: Studies the long-term qualitative and statistical behavior of systems evolving under iteration or a flow: measure-theoretic and topological invariants (entropy, mixing, recurrence) of ergodic systems; hyperbolic and low-dimensional smooth dynamics; complex and arithmetic iteration; bifurcation and random dynamical systems; Hamiltonian and infinite-dimensional dynamics; and numerical methods for simulating and classifying these systems. Spans pure structural theory and its applications to physics, number theory, and engineering.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá dlouhodobé kvalitativní a statistické chování systémů vyvíjejících se iterací nebo tokem: měrově-teoretické a topologické invarianty (entropii, míšení, rekurenci) ergodických systémů; hyperbolickou a nízkodimenzionální hladkou dynamiku; komplexní a aritmetickou iteraci; bifurkační teorii a náhodné dynamické systémy; hamiltonovskou a nekonečně-dimenzionální dynamiku; a numerické metody pro simulaci a klasifikaci těchto systémů. Zahrnuje jak čistě strukturální teorii, tak její aplikace ve fyzice, teorii čísel a inženýrství.
nodes.brief:
Dynamical systems asks what happens in the long run: orbits of iterated maps and flows, their statistical distribution under invariant measures (ergodic theory), and their qualitative structure (topological dynamics). Hyperbolicity, entropy, bifurcation, and low-dimensional and complex dynamics organize the modern theory, with Hamiltonian and infinite-dimensional systems at the physics-facing end.

The pillar continues the qualitative ODE theory of [[02.01.04.24]] by measure-theoretic means borrowed from [[02.01.04.19]]. Complex dynamics iterates the holomorphic maps of [[02.01.04.20]]; arithmetic dynamics meets the heights of [[02.01.04.07]]; ergodic methods feed back into [[02.01.04.16]] and [[02.01.04.44]]; and Hamiltonian dynamics with KAM theory is a shared frontier with [[01.01]].

nodes.name: Ergodic and Topological Foundations
nodes.en: The measure-theoretic and topological bedrock of dynamics: how orbits distribute statistically under an invariant measure, and how they behave qualitatively on a bare topological or metric space. Entropy, mixing, recurrence, minimality, and symbolic coding are the shared invariants that classify systems up to isomorphism or conjugacy in these two complementary lenses.
nodes.cs: Měrově-teoretické a topologické podloží dynamiky: jak se orbity statisticky rozdělují vůči invariantní míře a jak se kvalitativně chovají na holém topologickém či metrickém prostoru. Entropie, mísení, rekurence, minimalita a symbolické kódování jsou společné invarianty, které v těchto dvou komplementárních optikách třídí systémy až na izomorfismus či konjugaci.
nodes.brief:
The measure-theoretic and topological bedrock of dynamics: how orbits distribute statistically under an invariant measure, and how they behave qualitatively on a bare topological or metric space. Entropy, mixing, recurrence, minimality, and symbolic coding are the shared invariants that classify systems up to isomorphism or conjugacy in these two complementary lenses.

### Concepts
- **Ergodic theory** (37Axx) — Studies measure-preserving transformations and flows through invariant measures, mixing properties, entropy, and the statistical long-term behavior of orbits.
- **Topological dynamics** (37Bxx) — Studies dynamical systems on topological or metric spaces using qualitative invariants such as minimality, transitivity, symbolic coding, and topological entropy, independent of any invariant measure.

nodes.name: Smooth, Hyperbolic, and Low-Dimensional Dynamics
nodes.en: The differentiable-geometric core of the theory: general structural results for diffeomorphisms and flows on manifolds, the stable/unstable manifold machinery of uniformly and non-uniformly hyperbolic systems, and the special, more tractable dynamics of maps on intervals, circles, and surfaces. These strata build on one another, from generic smooth theory down to the concrete low-dimensional models where conjectures are first tested.
nodes.cs: Diferencovatelně-geometrické jádro teorie: obecné strukturní výsledky pro difeomorfismy a toky na varietách, aparát stabilních a nestabilních variet u stejnoměrně i nestejnoměrně hyperbolických systémů a speciální, lépe uchopitelná dynamika zobrazení na intervalech, kružnicích a plochách. Tyto vrstvy na sebe navazují — od generické hladké teorie po konkrétní nízkodimenzionální modely, na nichž se hypotézy testují nejdříve.
nodes.brief:
The differentiable-geometric core of the theory: general structural results for diffeomorphisms and flows on manifolds, the stable/unstable manifold machinery of uniformly and non-uniformly hyperbolic systems, and the special, more tractable dynamics of maps on intervals, circles, and surfaces. These strata build on one another, from generic smooth theory down to the concrete low-dimensional models where conjectures are first tested.

### Concepts
- **Smooth dynamical systems: general theory** (37Cxx) — Develops the general qualitative theory of diffeomorphisms and flows on manifolds, including structural stability, invariant manifolds, and generic dynamical properties.
- **Dynamical systems with hyperbolic behavior** (37Dxx) — Analyzes uniformly and non-uniformly hyperbolic systems (Anosov, Axiom A, partially hyperbolic) via stable/unstable manifold theory and Lyapunov exponents.
- **Low-dimensional dynamical systems** (37Exx) — Treats maps and flows on the interval, circle, and surfaces, including interval maps, rotation numbers, and surface homeomorphisms and diffeomorphisms.

nodes.name: Complex and Arithmetic Dynamics
nodes.en: Iteration theory over number-theoretic and analytic ground fields: holomorphic and meromorphic maps on the complex plane or Riemann surfaces, and rational maps over number fields, p-adic fields, and other non-Archimedean valuations. Julia/Fatou set theory sits alongside height functions and canonical heights, forging deep links to complex analysis and arithmetic geometry respectively.
nodes.cs: Teorie iterace nad základními tělesy analytické i aritmetické povahy: holomorfní a meromorfní zobrazení na komplexní rovině či Riemannových plochách a racionální zobrazení nad číselnými tělesy, p-adickými tělesy a dalšími nearchimédovskými valuacemi. Teorie Juliových a Fatouových množin tu stojí vedle výškových funkcí a kanonických výšek a buduje hluboké vazby ke komplexní analýze, respektive k aritmetické geometrii.
nodes.brief:
Iteration theory over number-theoretic and analytic ground fields: holomorphic and meromorphic maps on the complex plane or Riemann surfaces, and rational maps over number fields, p-adic fields, and other non-Archimedean valuations. Julia/Fatou set theory sits alongside height functions and canonical heights, forging deep links to complex analysis and arithmetic geometry respectively.

### Concepts
- **Dynamical systems over complex numbers** (37Fxx) — Studies the iteration of holomorphic and meromorphic maps, Julia and Fatou sets, and complex analytic dynamics in one and several variables.
- **Arithmetic and non-Archimedean dynamical systems** (37Pxx) — Studies iteration of rational and polynomial maps over number fields, p-adic fields, and other non-Archimedean valued fields, connecting dynamics to arithmetic geometry.

nodes.name: Bifurcation Theory and Random Dynamical Systems
nodes.en: How the qualitative portrait of a system changes as it is perturbed, either by a deterministic parameter or by persistent noise. Local bifurcations (Hopf, saddle-node) and global ones (homoclinic, heteroclinic) sit beside the cocycle formalism of random dynamical systems, whose random attractors and Lyapunov spectra generalize hyperbolicity to a stochastic setting.
nodes.cs: Jak se kvalitativní portrét systému mění při jeho poruše — ať už deterministickým parametrem, nebo trvalým šumem. Lokální bifurkace (Hopfova, sedlo-uzel) i globální (homoklinické, heteroklinické) stojí vedle kocyklového formalismu náhodných dynamických systémů, jejichž náhodné atraktory a Ljapunovova spektra zobecňují hyperbolicitu na stochastické prostředí.
nodes.brief:
How the qualitative portrait of a system changes as it is perturbed, either by a deterministic parameter or by persistent noise. Local bifurcations (Hopf, saddle-node) and global ones (homoclinic, heteroclinic) sit beside the cocycle formalism of random dynamical systems, whose random attractors and Lyapunov spectra generalize hyperbolicity to a stochastic setting.

### Concepts
- **Local and nonlocal bifurcation theory for dynamical systems** (37Gxx) — Studies qualitative changes in a system's phase portrait as parameters vary, covering local bifurcations such as Hopf and saddle-node and global bifurcations such as homoclinic and heteroclinic connections.
- **Random dynamical systems** (37Hxx) — Studies dynamical systems driven by noise or random parameters, including stochastic flows, random attractors, and Lyapunov exponents for cocycles.

nodes.name: Hamiltonian, Lagrangian, and Infinite-Dimensional Dynamics
nodes.en: The dynamics of conservative and dissipative systems arising from mechanics and PDEs. Finite-dimensional Hamiltonian and Lagrangian dynamics contributes integrability, KAM theory, and symplectic invariants; its infinite-dimensional counterpart extends these tools to integrable PDEs and solitons, while dissipative infinite-dimensional systems study global attractors and inertial manifolds for dissipative PDEs and semigroups.
nodes.cs: Dynamika konzervativních a disipativních systémů vycházejících z mechaniky a parciálních diferenciálních rovnic. Konečněrozměrná hamiltonovská a lagrangeovská dynamika přináší integrabilitu, KAM teorii a symplektické invarianty; její nekonečněrozměrný protějšek rozšiřuje tyto nástroje na integrabilní parciální diferenciální rovnice a solitony, zatímco disipativní nekonečněrozměrné systémy zkoumají globální atraktory a inerciální variety pro disipativní parciální diferenciální rovnice a pologrupy.
nodes.brief:
The dynamics of conservative and dissipative systems arising from mechanics and PDEs. Finite-dimensional Hamiltonian and Lagrangian dynamics contributes integrability, KAM theory, and symplectic invariants; its infinite-dimensional counterpart extends these tools to integrable PDEs and solitons, while dissipative infinite-dimensional systems study global attractors and inertial manifolds for dissipative PDEs and semigroups.

### Concepts
- **Dynamical aspects of finite-dimensional Hamiltonian and Lagrangian systems** (37Jxx) — Studies the dynamics of classical mechanical systems with finitely many degrees of freedom, including integrability, KAM theory, and symplectic invariants.
- **Dynamical system aspects of infinite-dimensional Hamiltonian and Lagrangian systems** (37Kxx) — Extends Hamiltonian dynamical methods to infinite-dimensional systems such as integrable PDEs, solitons, and Backlund transformations.
- **Infinite-dimensional dissipative dynamical systems** (37Lxx) — Studies attractors and long-time dynamics of dissipative PDEs and infinite-dimensional semigroups, including global attractors and inertial manifolds.

nodes.name: Numerical Methods and Applications of Dynamical Systems
nodes.en: The computational and applied wing of the theory: algorithms and computer-assisted proofs for simulating and rigorously analyzing dynamical systems, and the deployment of dynamical-systems tools across biology, economics, engineering, and physics. This block converts the structural theory of the other blocks into concrete, verifiable, and applicable predictions.
nodes.cs: Výpočetní a aplikovaná větev teorie: algoritmy a počítačem asistované důkazy pro simulaci a rigorózní analýzu dynamických systémů a nasazení nástrojů teorie dynamických systémů v biologii, ekonomii, inženýrství a fyzice. Tento blok převádí strukturální teorii ostatních bloků na konkrétní, ověřitelné a aplikovatelné předpovědi.
nodes.brief:
The computational and applied wing of the theory: algorithms and computer-assisted proofs for simulating and rigorously analyzing dynamical systems, and the deployment of dynamical-systems tools across biology, economics, engineering, and physics. This block converts the structural theory of the other blocks into concrete, verifiable, and applicable predictions.

### Concepts
- **Approximation methods and numerical treatment of dynamical systems** (37Mxx) — Covers numerical schemes, computer-assisted proofs, and computational tools for simulating and analyzing dynamical systems.
- **Applications of dynamical systems** (37Nxx) — Covers applications of dynamical-systems methods to biology, economics, engineering, physics, and other sciences.

nodes.name: Difference and functional equations
nodes.en: Difference and functional equations studies relations that determine an unknown sequence or function through its values at other, discrete or otherwise unrelated, arguments rather than through derivatives. It spans recurrence relations governing discrete-variable functions and general functional equations such as the Cauchy equation, unifying the discrete analogue of differential equations with the broader theory of functional constraints.
nodes.cs: Diferenční a funkcionální rovnice zkoumají vztahy, které určují neznámou posloupnost nebo funkci pomocí jejích hodnot v jiných, diskrétních nebo jinak nesouvisejících bodech, místo pomocí derivací. Obor zahrnuje rekurentní vztahy pro funkce diskrétní proměnné i obecné funkcionální rovnice, jako je Cauchyho rovnice, a spojuje tak diskrétní protějšek diferenciálních rovnic s širší teorií funkcionálních vztahů.
nodes.brief:
Here the unknown is constrained not by derivatives but by its own values elsewhere: recurrence and difference equations for discrete-variable functions, and functional equations such as Cauchy's, which pin down a function from an algebraic identity it satisfies. Stability of functional equations (Hyers-Ulam) and q-difference and time-scale dynamic equations round out the modern theory.

Difference equations are the discrete twin of the ODEs in [[02.01.04.24]] and, iterated, the maps of [[02.01.04.26]]; their numerical treatment sits in [[02.01.04.46]]; recurrences are combinatorics' native tongue in [[02.01.04.04]]; and the summation of their solutions passes to [[02.01.04.28]].

nodes.name: Difference Equations
nodes.en: Studies equations relating the values of an unknown sequence or discrete-variable function at shifted arguments, the discrete counterpart of ordinary differential equations. Covers linear and nonlinear recurrence relations and the existence, stability, periodicity, oscillation, and asymptotic behavior of their solutions, with close ties to numerical analysis, discrete dynamical systems, and combinatorics. Excludes dynamic equations on time scales and the qualitative theory of continuous differential equations, which belong to their own disciplines.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá rovnice vztahující hodnoty neznámé posloupnosti nebo funkce diskrétní proměnné v posunutých bodech — diskrétní protějšek obyčejných diferenciálních rovnic. Zahrnuje lineární i nelineární rekurentní vztahy a otázky existence, stability, periodicity, oscilace a asymptotického chování jejich řešení, úzce spjaté s numerickou analýzou, diskrétními dynamickými systémy a kombinatorikou. Nezahrnuje dynamické rovnice na časových škálách ani kvalitativní teorii spojitých diferenciálních rovnic, které patří do vlastních oborů.
nodes.brief:
Studies equations relating the values of an unknown sequence or discrete-variable function at shifted arguments, the natural discrete counterpart of ordinary differential equations. Encompasses linear and nonlinear recurrence relations, existence, stability, periodicity, oscillation, and asymptotic behavior of solutions, with close ties to numerical analysis, discrete dynamical systems, and combinatorics. Excludes dynamic equations on time scales (34N05) and the qualitative d

### Concepts
- **Difference equations** (39Axx) — The study of equations that relate the values of an unknown sequence or discrete-variable function at different (typically consecutive) points, together with the existence, stability, periodicity, and asymptotic behavior of their solutions.

nodes.name: Functional Equations and Inequalities
nodes.en: Concerns equations and inequalities whose unknown is a function itself, constrained by relations among its values at various, possibly unrelated, arguments — the paradigm case being the Cauchy equation f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) and its many generalizations. The theory develops existence, uniqueness, regularity, and stability (Hyers–Ulam type) results for such functions, with applications spanning information theory, geometry, and characterizations of elementary and special functions.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se rovnicemi a nerovnostmi, jejichž neznámou je samotná funkce, vázaná vztahy mezi svými hodnotami v různých, případně nesouvisejících bodech — typickým příkladem je Cauchyho rovnice f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) a její mnohá zobecnění. Teorie rozvíjí výsledky o existenci, jednoznačnosti, regularitě a stabilitě (typu Hyers–Ulam) takových funkcí, s aplikacemi sahajícími od teorie informace přes geometrii až po charakterizace elementárních a speciálních funkcí.
nodes.brief:
Concerns equations and inequalities whose unknown is a function itself, constrained by relations among its values at various, possibly unrelated, arguments — the paradigm case being the Cauchy equation f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) and its many generalizations. The theory develops existence, uniqueness, regularity, and stability (Hyers–Ulam type) results for such functions, with applications spanning information theory, geometry, and characterizations of elementary and special functions. 

### Concepts
- **Functional equations and inequalities** (39Bxx) — The study of equations and inequalities in which the unknown is a function subject to a relation among its values at several, generally independent, arguments, including questions of existence, regularity, and stability of solutions.

nodes.name: Sequences, series, summability
nodes.en: Sequences, series, and summability studies the convergence, divergence, and generalized summation of infinite sequences and series, from classical convergence tests through Cesàro, Abel, and Borel summation methods to their reformulation in functional-analytic terms via sequence spaces and matrix operators. It provides the foundational apparatus underlying Fourier analysis and the theory of divergent series.
nodes.cs: Posloupnosti, řady a sumabilita zkoumají konvergenci, divergenci a zobecněné sčítání nekonečných posloupností a řad — od klasických kritérií konvergence přes Cesàrovu, Abelovu a Borelovu sumační metodu až po jejich funkcionálně-analytickou reformulaci pomocí prostorů posloupností a maticových operátorů. Tvoří základní aparát, na němž stojí Fourierova analýza a teorie divergentních řad.
nodes.brief:
What it means for an infinite process to converge — and what can still be salvaged when it does not — is this pillar's whole subject: convergence tests, Tauberian theorems, and the summability methods of Cesàro, Abel and Borel that assign values to divergent series, recast functional-analytically as sequence spaces and matrix transformations.

It is foundational plumbing for the Fourier series of [[02.01.04.30]], whose pointwise behavior forced summability theory into existence; its sequence-space formulation is early functional analysis, now housed in [[02.01.04.34]]; asymptotic expansions connect it to [[02.01.04.29]] and [[02.01.04.23]]; and recurrent sequences tie back to [[02.01.04.27]].

nodes.name: Convergence of Sequences and Series
nodes.en: Studies the classical theory of when infinite sequences, series, products, and integrals converge, diverge, or oscillate, including tests, rearrangement behavior, and the extension of these questions to sequences and series indexed by two or more indices. This is the foundational layer on which all summability theory is built, tracing back to Cauchy and Abel.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá klasickou teorii toho, kdy nekonečné posloupnosti, řady, součiny a integrály konvergují, divergují nebo oscilují, včetně kritérií konvergence, chování při přerovnání a rozšíření těchto otázek na posloupnosti a řady indexované dvěma či více indexy. Jde o základní vrstvu, na níž je vystavěna celá teorie sumability, sahající až k Cauchymu a Abelovi.
nodes.brief:
Studies the classical theory of when infinite sequences, series, products, and integrals converge, diverge, or oscillate, including tests, rearrangement behavior, and the extension of these questions to sequences and series indexed by two or more indices. This is the foundational layer on which all summability theory is built, tracing back to Cauchy and Abel.

### Concepts
- **Convergence and divergence of infinite limiting processes** (40Axx) — Studies criteria and behavior governing the convergence or divergence of infinite series, sequences, products, and continued fractions.
- **Multiple sequences and series** (40Bxx) — Extends convergence theory to double and multiple-indexed sequences and series, addressing iterated versus simultaneous limits and Pringsheim-type convergence.

nodes.name: Summability Methods and Their Theorems
nodes.en: Develops linear and nonlinear methods — such as the Cesàro, Abel, Borel, and Hölder means — for assigning generalized sums to divergent series, together with the direct (Abelian) theorems showing convergence implies summability and the Tauberian theorems recovering convergence from summability plus a side condition. Covers absolute and strong summability and underlies applications from Fourier series to the classification of divergent series.
nodes.cs: Rozvíjí lineární i nelineární metody — jako Cesàrův, Abelův, Borelův a Hölderův průměr — pro přiřazení zobecněného součtu divergentní řadě, spolu s přímými (Abelovými) větami ukazujícími, že konvergence implikuje sumabilitu, a Tauberovými větami, které za dodatečné podmínky odvozují konvergenci ze sumability. Zahrnuje absolutní a silnou sumabilitu a tvoří základ aplikací od Fourierových řad až po klasifikaci divergentních řad.
nodes.brief:
Develops linear and nonlinear methods — such as Cesàro, Abel, Borel, and Hölder means — for assigning generalized sums to divergent series, together with the direct (Abelian) theorems showing convergence implies summability, the Tauberian/inversion theorems recovering convergence from summability plus a side condition, and the sharper notion of absolute or strong summability. This is the classical core of the discipline and underlies applications from Fourier series to diverg

### Concepts
- **General summability methods** (40Cxx) — Formalizes matrix, integral, and other general transformations that assign generalized sums to divergent sequences or series.
- **Direct theorems on summability** (40Dxx) — Establishes Abelian-type theorems proving that ordinary convergence of a series implies its summability by a given method, with the same sum.
- **Inversion theorems** (40Exx) — Proves Tauberian-type theorems that recover ordinary convergence from summability together with an auxiliary regularity condition.
- **Absolute and strong summability** (40Fxx) — Studies summability methods strengthened to require absolute convergence or strong (bounded-variation-type) convergence of the transformed sequence.
- **Special methods of summability** (40Gxx) — Catalogues named summability methods — Cesàro, Abel, Borel, Euler, Nörlund, and Riesz means among them — and their comparative properties.

nodes.name: Functional-Analytic and Abstract Summability
nodes.en: Recasts summability theory in the language of functional analysis, using sequence-space duality, matrix operators on Banach and FK-spaces, and Silverman–Toeplitz-type characterizations to unify classical methods, and further extends summability notions to sequences valued in abstract structures such as topological groups, Banach algebras, and locally compact groups. Reflects the twentieth-century abstraction of the subject beyond scalar series.
nodes.cs: Přepracovává teorii sumability do jazyka funkcionální analýzy pomocí duality prostorů posloupností, maticových operátorů na Banachových a FK-prostorech a charakterizací typu Silverman–Toeplitz, čímž sjednocuje klasické metody, a dále rozšiřuje pojmy sumability na posloupnosti s hodnotami v abstraktních strukturách, jako jsou topologické grupy, Banachovy algebry a lokálně kompaktní grupy. Odráží dvacáté století abstrakce oboru za hranice skalárních řad.
nodes.brief:
Recasts summability theory in the language of functional analysis, using sequence-space duality, matrix operators on Banach and FK-spaces, and Silverman–Toeplitz-type characterizations to unify classical methods, and further extends summability notions to sequences valued in abstract structures such as topological groups, Banach algebras, and locally compact groups. Reflects the twentieth-century abstraction of the subject beyond scalar series.

### Concepts
- **Functional analytic methods in summability** (40Hxx) — Applies sequence-space and operator-theoretic techniques, including FK-space and matrix-operator theory, to characterize and unify summability methods.
- **Summability in abstract structures** (40Jxx) — Generalizes summability theory to sequences and series taking values in abstract algebraic or topological structures such as topological groups and Banach algebras.

nodes.name: Approximations and expansions
nodes.en: Approximations and expansions studies how functions are approximated by structured simpler families — polynomials, splines, and other systems — and quantifies the resulting error in various norms, covering best approximation, interpolation, and moduli of smoothness. It underlies numerical analysis and constructive function theory, distinct from approximation in the complex domain or by trigonometric systems, which sit under harmonic analysis.
nodes.cs: Aproximace a rozvoje zkoumají, jak lze funkce aproximovat strukturovanými jednoduššími třídami — polynomy, splajny a dalšími systémy — a kvantifikují výslednou chybu v různých normách; zahrnují teorii nejlepší aproximace, interpolaci a moduly hladkosti. Tvoří základ numerické analýzy a konstruktivní teorie funkcí, na rozdíl od aproximace v komplexním oboru nebo trigonometrickými systémy, které patří do harmonické analýzy.
nodes.brief:
How well can a complicated function be replaced by a simple one? Best approximation, interpolation, splines, and moduli of smoothness quantify the trade-off between the simplicity of the approximating family — polynomials, rational functions, splines — and the error measured in a chosen norm. Jackson- and Bernstein-type theorems make the correspondence between smoothness and approximation rate exact.

It is the theoretical backbone of the numerical methods in [[02.01.04.46]] and of constructive function theory over the real functions of [[02.01.04.18]]; approximation by trigonometric systems is delegated to the harmonic analysis of [[02.01.04.30]], approximation in the complex domain to [[02.01.04.20]], and abstract approximation in normed spaces to [[02.01.04.34]].

nodes.name: Approximation Theory and Expansions
nodes.en: Studies how functions can be approximated by simpler classes — polynomials, rational functions, splines, and other structured families — and quantifies the resulting error in various norms. Encompasses best approximation, interpolation, moduli of smoothness, and the asymptotic behavior of approximating sequences as degree or dimension grows. Foundational to numerical analysis, signal processing, and constructive function theory, linking real-variable approximation to its complex-domain and trigonometric counterparts.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá, jak lze funkce aproximovat jednoduššími třídami — polynomy, racionálními funkcemi, splajny a dalšími strukturovanými systémy — a kvantifikuje výslednou chybu v různých normách. Zahrnuje teorii nejlepší aproximace, interpolaci, moduly hladkosti a asymptotické chování aproximujících posloupností s rostoucím stupněm nebo dimenzí. Tvoří základ numerické analýzy, zpracování signálu a konstruktivní teorie funkcí a spojuje reálnou aproximaci s jejími protějšky v komplexním oboru a trigonometrické aproximaci.
nodes.brief:
Studies how functions can be approximated by simpler classes — polynomials, rational functions, splines, and other structured families — and quantifies the resulting error in various norms. Encompasses best approximation, interpolation, quadrature-adjacent expansion theory, moduli of smoothness, and the asymptotic behavior of approximating sequences as degree or dimension grows. Foundational to numerical analysis, signal processing, and constructive function theory, linking c

### Concepts
- **Approximations and expansions** (41Axx) — The study of approximating functions by simpler classes (polynomials, rational functions, splines, etc.) and of the associated expansions, error bounds, and convergence behavior.

nodes.name: Harmonic analysis on Euclidean spaces
nodes.en: Harmonic analysis on Euclidean spaces studies the decomposition of functions on the real line and Euclidean n-space into trigonometric or other structured systems — Fourier series, the Fourier transform, wavelets, and general orthogonal expansions — together with the singular integral and maximal-function operators controlling their convergence. It supplies the analytic toolkit behind signal processing, PDE estimates, and time-frequency analysis.
nodes.cs: Harmonická analýza na eukleidovských prostorech zkoumá rozklad funkcí na reálné ose a v eukleidovském n-rozměrném prostoru pomocí trigonometrických i jiných strukturovaných systémů — Fourierových řad, Fourierovy transformace, vlnek a obecných ortogonálních rozvojů — spolu s operátory singulárních integrálů a maximálních funkcí řídícími jejich konvergenci. Poskytuje analytický aparát pro zpracování signálu, odhady v parciálních diferenciálních rovnicích a časo-frekvenční analýzu.
nodes.brief:
Fourier's idea — decompose a function into oscillations — becomes on Euclidean space a full theory: Fourier series and transforms, wavelets and general orthogonal expansions, and the singular integrals and maximal functions of Calderón-Zygmund theory that control their convergence. Time-frequency localization and oscillatory integrals are its modern research face.

It supplies the estimates on which the PDE theory of [[02.01.04.25]] runs, absorbs the summability questions of [[02.01.04.28]], and specializes the group-theoretic Fourier analysis of [[02.01.04.31]] to Euclidean space. The transform calculus of [[02.01.04.32]] is its operational cousin, and through sampling theorems and signal processing it grounds the information theory of [[02.01.04.51]].

nodes.name: Classical Fourier Analysis on Euclidean Space
nodes.en: Studies the decomposition and reconstruction of functions on the real line and Euclidean n-space via trigonometric systems — Fourier series and the Fourier transform — together with the operators, such as conjugate functions, singular integrals, and maximal functions, that control their convergence and summability. In one variable the theory is classical and largely complete, resting on Calderón–Zygmund and Littlewood–Paley technology; in several variables it remains a driving engine of modern harmonic analysis.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá rozklad a rekonstrukci funkcí na reálné ose a v eukleidovském n-rozměrném prostoru pomocí trigonometrických systémů — Fourierových řad a Fourierovy transformace — spolu s operátory, jako jsou konjugované funkce, singulární integrály a maximální funkce, které řídí jejich konvergenci a sumabilitu. V jedné proměnné je teorie klasická a v podstatě uzavřená, opírající se o Calderónovu–Zygmundovu a Littlewoodovu–Paleyho techniku; ve více proměnných zůstává hnacím motorem moderní harmonické analýzy.
nodes.brief:
Studies the decomposition and reconstruction of functions on the real line and Euclidean n-space via trigonometric systems — Fourier series and the Fourier transform — together with the operators (conjugate function, singular integrals, maximal functions) that control their convergence and summability. In one variable this theory is classical and largely complete, resting on Calderón–Zygmund and Littlewood–Paley technology; in several variables it remains a driving engine of 

### Concepts
- **Harmonic analysis in one variable** (42Axx) — Studies Fourier series and the Fourier transform on the real line, including convergence, summability, and conjugate-function theory for one-dimensional trigonometric expansions.
- **Harmonic analysis in several variables** (42Bxx) — Extends Fourier analysis to Euclidean n-space, covering multiplier and singular-integral operators, maximal functions, and open problems such as the Bochner–Riesz and Fourier restriction conjectures.

nodes.name: Nontrigonometric Harmonic Analysis
nodes.en: Concerns function expansions in bases other than the trigonometric system — general orthogonal series, wavelets, frames, and related special-function expansions — together with their convergence, approximation, and stability properties. Generalizes classical Fourier methods to settings where trigonometric functions are not the natural building blocks, providing tools for multiresolution analysis and time-frequency localization used throughout signal processing and numerical analysis.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se rozvoji funkcí v jiných bázích než trigonometrickém systému — obecnými ortogonálními řadami, vlnkami, rámci a souvisejícími rozvoji speciálních funkcí — spolu s jejich konvergencí, aproximací a vlastnostmi stability. Zobecňuje klasické Fourierovy metody na situace, kde trigonometrické funkce nejsou přirozenými stavebními bloky, a poskytuje nástroje pro multirezoluční analýzu a časo-frekvenční lokalizaci využívané v zpracování signálu a numerické analýze.
nodes.brief:
Concerns function expansions in bases other than the trigonometric system — general orthogonal series, wavelets, frames, and related special-function expansions — together with their convergence, approximation, and stability properties. It generalizes classical Fourier methods to settings where trigonometric functions are not the natural building blocks, providing tools for multiresolution analysis and time-frequency localization used throughout signal processing and numerica

### Concepts
- **Nontrigonometric harmonic analysis** (42Cxx) — Studies expansions with respect to non-trigonometric orthogonal systems, including wavelets and frames, and their convergence and approximation properties.

nodes.name: Abstract harmonic analysis
nodes.en: Abstract harmonic analysis is the mathematical subject classification branch (MSC 43-XX) studying Fourier analysis, convolution, and duality on locally compact groups and homogeneous spaces, generalizing classical Fourier series and integrals via Haar measure and unitary representation theory. It excludes analysis specific to topological and Lie groups, covered separately under 22Exx.
nodes.cs: Abstraktní harmonická analýza je obor matematické klasifikace (MSC 43-XX) zabývající se Fourierovou analýzou, konvolucí a dualitou na lokálně kompaktních grupách a homogenních prostorech, který zobecňuje klasické Fourierovy řady a integrály pomocí Haarovy míry a teorie unitárních reprezentací. Nezahrnuje analýzu specifickou pro topologické a Lieovy grupy, kterou pokrývá samostatně kód 22Exx.
nodes.brief:
Fourier analysis survives the loss of Euclidean structure: on any locally compact group, Haar measure supports convolution, and unitary representation theory replaces the exponentials. Pontryagin duality (abelian case) and the Peter-Weyl theorem (compact case) are the twin classical summits; group algebras and almost periodic functions carry the theory into functional-analytic territory.

The pillar generalizes the Euclidean theory of [[02.01.04.30]] and rests on the structure theory of the topological groups in [[02.01.04.17]]; its group algebras and operator-algebraic methods are shared with [[02.01.04.34]] and [[02.01.04.35]], and its representation-theoretic core keeps it in conversation with [[02.01.04.16]].

nodes.name: Abstract Harmonic Analysis
nodes.en: Studies Fourier analysis, convolution, and duality on locally compact groups and homogeneous spaces, generalizing classical Fourier series and integrals via Haar measure and unitary representation theory. It underpins the Pontryagin and Peter-Weyl duality theorems, group algebras, and almost periodic functions, and provides the structural framework connecting representation theory to analysis on both abelian and non-abelian groups.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá Fourierovu analýzu, konvoluci a dualitu na lokálně kompaktních grupách a homogenních prostorech; klasické Fourierovy řady a integrály zobecňuje pomocí Haarovy míry a teorie unitárních reprezentací. Je oporou Pontrjaginovy duality a Peterovy–Weylovy věty, grupových algeber a skoroperiodických funkcí a poskytuje strukturní rámec spojující teorii reprezentací s analýzou na abelovských i neabelovských grupách.
nodes.brief:
Studies Fourier analysis, convolution, and duality on locally compact groups and homogeneous spaces, generalizing classical Fourier series and integrals via Haar measure and unitary representation theory. It underpins the Pontryagin and Peter-Weyl duality theorems, group algebras, and almost periodic functions, and provides the structural framework connecting representation theory to analysis on both abelian and non-abelian groups.

### Concepts
- **Abstract harmonic analysis** (43Axx) — The study of Haar measure, convolution algebras, Fourier and Fourier-Stieltjes transforms, and unitary representations on locally compact groups and homogeneous spaces.

nodes.name: Integral transforms, operational calculus
nodes.en: Integral transforms and operational calculus is the mathematical subject classification branch (MSC 44-XX) studying linear integral transforms — Laplace, Hankel, Mellin, Stieltjes, Hilbert, Z and their relatives — and the operational calculus built on them to convert differential and integral equations into algebraic problems on a transformed domain. Fractional derivatives, Fourier transforms, transforms in distribution spaces, and numerical transform methods are classified separately (26A33, 42A38/42B10, 46F12, 65R10).
nodes.cs: Integrální transformace a operátorový počet je obor matematické klasifikace (MSC 44-XX) zabývající se lineárními integrálními transformacemi — Laplaceovou, Hankelovou, Mellinovou, Stieltjesovou, Hilbertovou, Z-transformací a příbuznými — a operátorovým počtem na nich vystavěným, který převádí diferenciální a integrální rovnice na algebraické úlohy v transformované doméně. Zlomkové derivace, Fourierovy transformace, transformace v prostorech distribucí a numerické metody transformací jsou klasifikovány samostatně (26A33, 42A38/42B10, 46F12, 65R10).
nodes.brief:
Integral transforms convert analysis into algebra: Laplace, Mellin, Hankel, Stieltjes, Hilbert and Z transforms map differentiation to multiplication, so differential and integral equations become algebraic problems on the transformed side — the essence of operational calculus, made rigorous long after engineers had learned to trust it.

The Fourier transform proper is the property of [[02.01.04.30]]; the equations the transforms solve live in [[02.01.04.24]], [[02.01.04.25]] and [[02.01.04.33]]; distributional settings belong to [[02.01.04.34]]; numerical transform methods to [[02.01.04.46]]; and the special functions of [[02.01.04.23]] appear systematically as kernels and images.

nodes.name: Integral Transforms and Operational Calculus
nodes.en: Studies linear integral transforms — Laplace, Hankel, Mellin, Stieltjes, Hilbert, Z, and their many relatives — that convert differential and integral equations into algebraic or simpler analytic problems on a transformed domain, together with the operational calculus built on such transforms to solve linear ODEs, PDEs, and convolution equations symbolically. Encompasses inversion theory and operational rules such as shift, convolution, and differentiation-multiplication correspondences that let symbolic manipulation replace direct analytic solution.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se lineárními integrálními transformacemi — Laplaceovou, Hankelovou, Mellinovou, Stieltjesovou, Hilbertovou, Z-transformací a dalšími příbuznými — které převádějí diferenciální a integrální rovnice na algebraické či jednodušší analytické úlohy v transformované doméně, spolu s operátorovým počtem na nich vystavěným pro symbolické řešení lineárních ODR, PDR a konvolučních rovnic. Zahrnuje teorii inverze a operátorová pravidla, jako je posun, konvoluce a korespondence derivace-násobení, umožňující nahradit přímé analytické řešení symbolickou manipulací.
nodes.brief:
Studies linear integral transforms — Laplace, Hankel, Mellin, Stieltjes, Hilbert, Z, and their many relatives — that convert differential and integral equations into algebraic or simpler analytic problems on a transformed domain, together with the operational calculus built on such transforms to solve linear ODEs, PDEs, and convolution equations symbolically. Encompasses inversion theory, operational rules (shift, convolution, differentiation-multiplication correspondences), 

### Concepts
- **Integral transforms, operational calculus** (44Axx) — The study of linear integral transforms (Laplace, Hankel, Mellin, Stieltjes, Hilbert, and other kernel-based transforms) and the operational calculus built on them for solving differential, integral, and convolution equations.

nodes.name: Integral equations
nodes.en: Integral equations is the mathematical subject classification branch (MSC 45-XX) studying equations in which an unknown function appears under an integral sign combined with a kernel, spanning linear Fredholm and Volterra theory, spectral treatment of integral operators, singular and nonlinear kernels, integro-differential equations, and the qualitative, abstract and stochastic theory of solutions.
nodes.cs: Integrální rovnice je obor matematické klasifikace (MSC 45-XX) zabývající se rovnicemi, v nichž neznámá funkce vystupuje pod integrálem spolu s jádrem; zahrnuje lineární Fredholmovu a Volterrovu teorii, spektrální přístup k integrálním operátorům, singulární a nelineární jádra, integro-diferenciální rovnice a kvalitativní, abstraktní a stochastickou teorii řešení.
nodes.brief:
An unknown function under an integral sign, tied to a kernel: Fredholm and Volterra theory, spectral analysis of integral operators, singular and nonlinear kernels, and integro-differential equations. Fredholm's alternative and compact-operator spectral theory arose here and seeded functional analysis itself.

Boundary-value problems from the PDE theory of [[02.01.04.25]] convert into integral equations via potential-theoretic methods from [[02.01.04.21]]; the operator-theoretic viewpoint is that of [[02.01.04.35]] inside the spaces of [[02.01.04.34]]; the transforms of [[02.01.04.32]] diagonalize convolution kernels; and the numerical solution of integral equations belongs to [[02.01.04.46]].

nodes.name: Foundations of Linear Integral Equations
nodes.en: Studies equations in which an unknown function occurs linearly under an integral sign combined with a kernel — the bedrock formulation from which the rest of 45-XX branches. Fredholm theory classifies solvability via the Fredholm alternative and resolvent kernels for equations of the first and second kind, and this linear-operator machinery extends directly to coupled systems of several simultaneous equations. One of the most classical and thoroughly settled areas of twentieth-century analysis.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se rovnicemi, v nichž neznámá funkce vystupuje lineárně pod integrálem spolu s jádrem — základní formulací, z níž se odvíjí zbytek oboru 45-XX. Fredholmova teorie klasifikuje řešitelnost pomocí Fredholmovy alternativy a rezolventních jader pro rovnice prvního a druhého druhu a tento lineárně-operátorový aparát se přímo rozšiřuje na svázané soustavy více simultánních rovnic. Jde o jednu z nejklasičtějších a nejdůkladněji zavedených oblastí analýzy dvacátého století.
nodes.brief:
Studies equations in which an unknown function occurs linearly under an integral sign combined with a kernel — the bedrock formulation from which the rest of 45-XX branches. Fredholm theory classifies solvability via the Fredholm alternative and resolvent kernels for equations of the first and second kind, and this linear-operator machinery extends directly to coupled systems of several simultaneous equations. One of the most classical and thoroughly settled areas of 20th-cen

### Concepts
- **Linear integral equations** (45Axx) — Studies integral equations in which the unknown function occurs linearly under the integral sign, the general linear framework underlying 45-XX.
- **Fredholm integral equations** (45Bxx) — Treats equations with a fixed integration domain and kernel, governed by the Fredholm alternative and resolvent-kernel solvability theory.
- **Systems of linear integral equations** (45Fxx) — Extends linear and Fredholm theory to coupled systems of several simultaneous integral equations in several unknown functions.

nodes.name: Spectral Theory and Integral Operators
nodes.en: Reformulates integral equations as eigenvalue problems for the associated integral operator, connecting 45-XX to functional and operator theory. Compactness of the kernel yields a discrete spectrum via Riesz–Schauder theory, while the study of integral operators as bounded, compact, or singular maps between function spaces underlies spectral and numerical methods across analysis. This area bridges classical integral-equation theory with operator theory on Banach and Hilbert spaces.
nodes.cs: Přeformulovává integrální rovnice na úlohy o vlastních číslech příslušného integrálního operátoru, čímž propojuje obor 45-XX s funkcionální a operátorovou analýzou. Kompaktnost jádra dává diskrétní spektrum podle Rieszovy–Schauderovy teorie, zatímco studium integrálních operátorů jako omezených, kompaktních či singulárních zobrazení mezi funkčními prostory je základem spektrálních a numerických metod v analýze. Tato oblast spojuje klasickou teorii integrálních rovnic s operátorovou teorií na Banachových a Hilbertových prostorech.
nodes.brief:
Reformulates integral equations as eigenvalue problems for the associated integral operator, connecting 45-XX to functional and operator theory. Compactness of the kernel yields a discrete spectrum via Riesz–Schauder theory, while the study of integral operators as bounded, compact, or singular maps between function spaces underlies spectral and numerical methods across analysis. This area bridges classical integral-equation theory with operator theory on Banach and Hilbert s

### Concepts
- **Eigenvalue problems for integral equations** (45Cxx) — Studies eigenvalues and eigenfunctions of integral operators, the integral-equation analogue of Sturm–Liouville spectral theory.
- **Integral operators** (45Pxx) — Studies integral operators themselves — their boundedness, compactness, and spectral properties as maps between function spaces.

nodes.name: Special Classes: Volterra, Singular, and Nonlinear Equations
nodes.en: Groups integral-equation types distinguished by structural features of the kernel or the equation rather than by linearity alone. Volterra equations, with a variable upper limit of integration, encode causal memory-dependent evolution; singular equations carry non-integrable kernel singularities (Cauchy, Hilbert-type) requiring principal-value regularization; equations built around other named or structured kernels form a further catalogue; and nonlinear integral equations, in which the unknown function itself appears nonlinearly, are solved chiefly by fixed-point methods.
nodes.cs: Sdružuje typy integrálních rovnic rozlišené strukturálními rysy jádra nebo rovnice samotné, nikoli pouze linearitou. Volterrovy rovnice s proměnnou horní mezí integrace zachycují kauzální, na paměti závislý vývoj; singulární rovnice mají neintegrovatelné singularity jádra (Cauchyho, Hilbertova typu) vyžadující regularizaci hlavní hodnotou; rovnice vystavěné kolem dalších pojmenovaných či strukturovaných jader tvoří další katalog; a nelineární integrální rovnice, v nichž neznámá funkce sama vystupuje nelineárně, se řeší především metodami pevného bodu.
nodes.brief:
Groups integral-equation types distinguished by structural features of the kernel or the equation rather than by linearity alone. Volterra equations, with a variable upper limit of integration, encode causal memory-dependent evolution; singular equations carry non-integrable kernel singularities (Cauchy, Hilbert-type) requiring principal-value regularization; equations built around other named or structured kernels form a further catalogue; and nonlinear integral equations, i

### Concepts
- **Volterra integral equations** (45Dxx) — Studies equations with a variable, rather than fixed, upper limit of integration, modeling causal processes with memory.
- **Singular integral equations** (45Exx) — Studies equations whose kernel has a non-integrable singularity, solved via Cauchy principal values and Hilbert-transform techniques.
- **Nonlinear integral equations** (45Gxx) — Studies equations in which the unknown function appears nonlinearly, including Hammerstein- and Urysohn-type equations, via fixed-point methods.
- **Integral equations with miscellaneous special kernels** (45Hxx) — Catalogues integral equations distinguished by a specific named or structured kernel not covered by the other 45-XX subdivisions.

nodes.name: Integro-Differential Equations
nodes.en: Studies equations that combine differential operators with integral terms, modeling systems whose evolution depends on both an instantaneous rate of change and accumulated history. Integro-ordinary differential equations couple such memory terms with ODEs, as in viscoelasticity and structured population models; integro-partial differential equations do the same for PDEs, arising in nonlocal diffusion, kinetic theory, and delayed reaction-diffusion systems. Both remain active areas of applied analysis.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se rovnicemi, které kombinují diferenciální operátory s integrálními členy a modelují systémy, jejichž vývoj závisí jak na okamžité rychlosti změny, tak na nahromaděné historii. Integro-obyčejné diferenciální rovnice svazují takové paměťové členy s ODR, například ve viskoelasticitě a strukturovaných populačních modelech; integro-parciální diferenciální rovnice totéž dělají pro PDR a vystupují v nelokální difuzi, kinetické teorii a zpožděných reakčně-difuzních systémech. Obě oblasti zůstávají aktivními směry aplikované analýzy.
nodes.brief:
Studies equations that combine differential operators with integral terms, modeling systems whose evolution depends on both an instantaneous rate of change and accumulated history. Integro-ordinary differential equations couple such memory terms with ODEs, as in viscoelasticity and structured population models; integro-partial differential equations do the same for PDEs, arising in nonlocal diffusion, kinetic theory, and delayed reaction-diffusion systems. Both remain active 

### Concepts
- **Integro-ordinary differential equations** (45Jxx) — Studies ordinary differential equations coupled with an integral memory term acting on the unknown function.
- **Integro-partial differential equations** (45Kxx) — Studies partial differential equations coupled with an integral term, arising in nonlocal diffusion and kinetic models.

nodes.name: Qualitative, Abstract, and Stochastic Theory of Solutions
nodes.en: Gathers the meta-level questions asked about integral equations once existence of a solution is settled: how the solution behaves qualitatively (stability, boundedness, asymptotics), how it can be approximated by theoretical schemes, how the equation generalizes to abstract Banach- or Hilbert-space-valued settings, how unknown kernels or data can be recovered from observed solutions, and how randomness in the kernel or forcing term propagates to the solution process. These strands share a common meta-theoretic character rather than a single equation type.
nodes.cs: Shromažďuje metaúrovňové otázky kladené o integrálních rovnicích poté, co je existence řešení již zajištěna: jak se řešení kvalitativně chová (stabilita, omezenost, asymptotika), jak jej lze aproximovat teoretickými schématy, jak se rovnice zobecňuje do abstraktních prostorů s hodnotami v Banachových či Hilbertových prostorech, jak lze neznámá jádra či data rekonstruovat z pozorovaných řešení a jak se náhodnost v jádru či budicím členu přenáší na řešení. Tyto směry spojuje spíše společný metateoretický charakter než jediný typ rovnice.
nodes.brief:
Gathers the meta-level questions asked about integral equations once existence of a solution is settled: how the solution behaves qualitatively (stability, boundedness, asymptotics), how it can be approximated by theoretical schemes, how the equation generalizes to abstract Banach- or Hilbert-space-valued settings, how unknown kernels or data can be recovered from observed solutions, and how randomness in the kernel or forcing term propagates to the solution process. These st

### Concepts
- **Theoretical approximation of solutions to integral equations** (45Lxx) — Studies theoretical, as opposed to purely numerical, approximation schemes for solutions of integral equations.
- **Qualitative behavior of solutions to integral equations** (45Mxx) — Studies stability, boundedness, oscillation, and asymptotic behavior of solutions to integral equations.
- **Abstract integral equations, integral equations in abstract spaces** (45Nxx) — Extends integral-equation theory to unknowns and kernels valued in abstract Banach or Hilbert spaces.
- **Inverse problems for integral equations** (45Qxx) — Studies the recovery of an unknown kernel, domain, or forcing term of an integral equation from observed solution data.
- **Random integral equations** (45Rxx) — Studies integral equations with random kernels, coefficients, or forcing terms, and the resulting stochastic solution processes.

nodes.name: Functional analysis
nodes.en: Functional analysis is the mathematical subject classification branch (MSC 46-XX) studying vector spaces equipped with a topology, norm or inner product — from general topological linear spaces through Banach and Hilbert spaces to function spaces, distributions, and topological and operator algebras including C*-algebras — together with nonlinear and applied extensions of this theory. Manifolds modeled on topological linear spaces are classified separately under 57Nxx and 58Bxx.
nodes.cs: Funkcionální analýza je obor matematické klasifikace (MSC 46-XX) zabývající se vektorovými prostory opatřenými topologií, normou nebo skalárním součinem — od obecných topologických lineárních prostorů přes Banachovy a Hilbertovy prostory až po prostory funkcí, distribuce a topologické a operátorové algebry včetně C*-algeber — spolu s nelineárními a aplikovanými rozšířeními této teorie. Variety modelované na topologických lineárních prostorech jsou klasifikovány samostatně pod kódy 57Nxx a 58Bxx.
nodes.brief:
Functional analysis studies infinite-dimensional vector spaces with topology — Banach and Hilbert spaces, locally convex spaces, function spaces and distributions, and the topological and operator algebras (C*- and von Neumann) that grew out of the quantum mechanics of [[01.01]]. Duality, the closed-graph circle of theorems, and the geometry of Banach spaces form its structural core.

It is the ambient framework in which the operator theory of [[02.01.04.35]] lives; its L^p and Sobolev spaces, built on the measures of [[02.01.04.19]], are the natural habitat of the PDE theory of [[02.01.04.25]]; distributions generalize the functions of [[02.01.04.18]]; its group algebras serve [[02.01.04.31]]; and operator-algebra K-theory feeds [[02.01.04.15]].

nodes.name: Foundational Linear Spaces
nodes.en: The bedrock structures of functional analysis: vector spaces equipped with a topology, a norm, or an inner product, and the geometric and algebraic theory that follows from each level of extra structure. Banach and Hilbert space theory built here — completeness, duality, convexity, orthogonality — underlies essentially every other block in 46-XX and much of operator theory, PDE, and analysis at large. This area is classical and forms the standard graduate curriculum in functional analysis.
nodes.cs: Základní struktury funkcionální analýzy: vektorové prostory opatřené topologií, normou nebo skalárním součinem a geometrická a algebraická teorie, která z každé úrovně dodatečné struktury vyplývá. Zde vybudovaná teorie Banachových a Hilbertových prostorů — úplnost, dualita, konvexita, ortogonalita — je základem prakticky všech dalších bloků oboru 46-XX i velké části operátorové teorie, PDR a analýzy obecně. Tato oblast je klasická a tvoří standardní náplň postgraduálního kurzu funkcionální analýzy.
nodes.brief:
The bedrock structures of functional analysis: vector spaces equipped with a topology, a norm, or an inner product, and the geometric and algebraic theory that follows from each level of extra structure. Banach and Hilbert space theory built here — completeness, duality, convexity, orthogonality — underlies essentially every other block in 46-XX and much of operator theory, PDE, and analysis at large. This area is classical and forms the standard graduate curriculum in functi

### Concepts
- **Topological linear spaces and related structures** (46Axx) — Studies vector spaces endowed with a topology compatible with the linear operations, including locally convex, barrelled, and Fréchet spaces, generalizing normed spaces to settings without a single norm.
- **Normed linear spaces and Banach spaces; Banach lattices** (46Bxx) — Develops the geometry and structure theory of normed and complete (Banach) spaces, including basis theory, convexity, reflexivity, and the ordered-lattice refinements known as Banach lattices.
- **Inner product spaces and their generalizations, Hilbert spaces** (46Cxx) — Studies spaces equipped with an inner product and their completions, Hilbert spaces, exploiting orthogonality, projection, and self-duality to obtain the sharpest structural results in the normed hierarchy.

nodes.name: Function Spaces, Distributions, and Infinite-Dimensional Analysis
nodes.en: Concrete realizations of abstract linear-space theory as spaces of functions, together with their duals and the generalized-function (distribution) framework needed to differentiate and transform objects that are not classically smooth. Also covers measure, integration, differentiation, and holomorphy carried out directly in infinite-dimensional spaces, connecting functional analysis to harmonic analysis, PDE, and infinite-dimensional measure theory. This is mature core theory underlying much of modern analysis.
nodes.cs: Konkrétní realizace abstraktní teorie lineárních prostorů jako prostorů funkcí spolu s jejich duály a rámcem zobecněných funkcí (distribucí) potřebným k derivování a transformaci objektů, které nejsou klasicky hladké. Zahrnuje také míru, integraci, derivování a holomorfii prováděné přímo v nekonečněrozměrných prostorech, čímž propojuje funkcionální analýzu s harmonickou analýzou, PDR a nekonečněrozměrnou teorií míry. Jde o vyzrálou jádrovou teorii, na níž stojí velká část moderní analýzy.
nodes.brief:
Concrete realizations of abstract linear-space theory as spaces of functions, together with their duals and the generalized-function (distribution) framework needed to differentiate and transform objects that are not classically smooth. Also covers measure, integration, differentiation, and holomorphy carried out directly in infinite-dimensional spaces, connecting functional analysis to harmonic analysis, PDE, and infinite-dimensional measure theory. This is mature core theor

### Concepts
- **Linear function spaces and their duals** (46Exx) — Studies concrete Banach or locally convex spaces whose elements are functions (Lp, Sobolev, Orlicz, etc.) together with their dual spaces and duality pairings.
- **Distributions, generalized functions, distribution spaces** (46Fxx) — Develops the theory of distributions and other generalized functions as continuous linear functionals on test-function spaces, extending differentiation and Fourier analysis beyond classical functions.
- **Measures, integration, derivative, holomorphy (all involving infinite-dimensional spaces)** (46Gxx) — Extends measure theory, integration, differential calculus, and holomorphic function theory to functions and measures defined on infinite-dimensional vector spaces.

nodes.name: Topological and Operator Algebras
nodes.en: The theory of algebras carrying a compatible topology or norm, ranging from general topological and Banach algebras through commutative and involutive refinements to the self-adjoint operator algebras — C*-algebras and von Neumann algebras — that model observables in quantum physics and underlie noncommutative geometry. This progression from general to highly structured algebras is one of the deepest and most productive strands of twentieth- and twenty-first-century analysis.
nodes.cs: Teorie algeber nesoucích kompatibilní topologii nebo normu, od obecných topologických a Banachových algeber přes komutativní a involutivní zjemnění až po samoadjungované operátorové algebry — C*-algebry a von Neumannovy algebry — které modelují pozorovatelné veličiny v kvantové fyzice a jsou základem nekomutativní geometrie. Tento postup od obecných k vysoce strukturovaným algebrám patří k nejhlubším a nejplodnějším směrům analýzy dvacátého a jednadvacátého století.
nodes.brief:
The theory of algebras carrying a compatible topology or norm, ranging from general topological and Banach algebras through commutative and involutive refinements to the self-adjoint operator algebras — C*-algebras and von Neumann algebras — that model observables in quantum physics and underlie noncommutative geometry. This progression from general to highly structured algebras is one of the deepest and most productive strands of 20th- and 21st-century analysis, with C*- and

### Concepts
- **Topological algebras, normed rings and algebras, Banach algebras** (46Hxx) — Studies algebras equipped with a compatible topology or norm, in particular complete normed (Banach) algebras, developing spectral theory in an algebraic setting.
- **Commutative Banach algebras and commutative topological algebras** (46Jxx) — Specializes Banach and topological algebra theory to the commutative case, where Gelfand theory represents elements as functions on the algebra's maximal ideal space.
- **Topological (rings and) algebras with an involution** (46Kxx) — Studies topological algebras equipped with an involutive anti-automorphism (a *-operation), the structural prerequisite for C*-algebra and Banach *-algebra theory.
- **Selfadjoint operator algebras (C*-algebras, von Neumann (W*-) algebras, etc.)** (46Lxx) — Studies norm-closed or weakly-closed self-adjoint algebras of bounded operators on Hilbert space, the operator-algebraic framework underlying quantum theory and noncommutative geometry.

nodes.name: Generalized, Nonlinear, and Applied Methods
nodes.en: Cross-cutting extensions of functional analysis beyond the linear, classical core: categorical reformulations of the field's basic constructions, applications to other mathematical and physical domains, nonclassical variants adapted to non-Archimedean or other exotic settings, and genuinely nonlinear functional analysis needed for nonlinear PDE and fixed-point theory. These strands are comparatively young and less unified than the classical blocks, with nonlinear functional analysis the most actively developed of the four.
nodes.cs: Průřezová rozšíření funkcionální analýzy nad rámec lineárního, klasického jádra: kategoriální přeformulování základních konstrukcí oboru, aplikace v dalších matematických a fyzikálních oblastech, neklasické varianty přizpůsobené neArchimédovským či jiným exotickým prostředím a skutečně nelineární funkcionální analýza potřebná pro nelineární PDR a teorii pevného bodu. Tyto směry jsou poměrně mladé a méně sjednocené než klasické bloky, přičemž nejaktivněji rozvíjenou z nich je právě nelineární funkcionální analýza.
nodes.brief:
Cross-cutting extensions of functional analysis beyond the linear, classical core: categorical reformulations of the field's basic constructions, applications to other mathematical and physical domains, nonclassical variants adapted to non-Archimedean or other exotic settings, and genuinely nonlinear functional analysis needed for nonlinear PDE and fixed-point theory. These strands are comparatively young and less unified than the classical blocks, with nonlinear functional a

### Concepts
- **Methods of category theory in functional analysis** (46Mxx) — Applies categorical constructions — functors, limits, tensor products — to organize and unify functional-analytic structures and their morphisms.
- **Miscellaneous applications of functional analysis** (46Nxx) — Covers applications of functional-analytic methods to problems in other areas of mathematics, physics, and engineering not classified elsewhere.
- **Other (nonclassical) types of functional analysis** (46Sxx) — Encompasses nonclassical variants of functional analysis, such as analysis over non-Archimedean or non-standard fields, that fall outside the classical real/complex framework.
- **Nonlinear functional analysis** (46Txx) — Studies nonlinear maps and operators between infinite-dimensional spaces, including differential calculus in Banach spaces and nonlinear fixed-point and variational methods.

nodes.name: Operator theory
nodes.en: Operator theory studies linear operators on Banach and Hilbert spaces — their spectral, structural, and algebraic properties — spanning the axiomatic theory of bounded and unbounded operators, distinguished classes such as self-adjoint, normal, compact and Fredholm operators, differential, integral and semigroup operators governing evolution equations, and nonlinear operator theory with its associated equations and applications.
nodes.cs: Teorie operátorů zkoumá lineární operátory na Banachových a Hilbertových prostorech — jejich spektrální, strukturální a algebraické vlastnosti — a zahrnuje axiomatickou teorii omezených i neomezených operátorů, vyznačené třídy jako samoadjungované, normální, kompaktní a Fredholmovy operátory, diferenciální, integrální a pologrupové operátory popisující evoluční rovnice a nelineární teorii operátorů s jejími rovnicemi a aplikacemi.
nodes.brief:
Operator theory is the fine structure of linear maps on Banach and Hilbert spaces: spectral theory of self-adjoint, normal, compact and Fredholm operators, unbounded operators and their extensions, operator semigroups governing evolution equations, and nonlinear operator equations. The spectral theorem is its organizing miracle.
