The pillar spans foundations and decision theory, sampling and distribution theory, parametric and nonparametric inference, multivariate analysis and regression, experimental design and sequential methods, time-series and survival inference, and the young frontier of statistics on manifolds, networks and shapes.

Its probability substrate is [[02.01.04.44]]; its computational face — from numerical linear algebra to Monte Carlo — leans on [[02.01.04.46]] and [[02.01.04.11]]; the machine-learning boundary is shared with [[02.01.04.47]]; the design of experiments borrows the combinatorics of [[02.01.04.04]]; and applied statistical methodology radiates into every empirical science, including the measurement side of [[01.01]].

nodes.name: Foundations, Information, and Decision Theory
nodes.en: Establishes the conceptual and mathematical bedrock of statistical inference: the axiomatic and philosophical foundations of the subject, the information-theoretic concepts (sufficiency, ancillarity, Fisher information) that measure what data reveal about unknown parameters, and the decision-theoretic framework (risk, admissibility, minimaxity, Bayes rules) used to evaluate and choose among statistical procedures.
nodes.cs: Ustavuje pojmové a matematické podloží statistické inference: axiomatické a filozofické základy oboru, informačně-teoretické pojmy (postačitelnost, ancilarita, Fisherova informace) měřící, co data vypovídají o neznámých parametrech, a rozhodovací rámec (riziko, přípustnost, minimaxovost, bayesovská pravidla) pro hodnocení a volbu statistických postupů.
nodes.brief:
Establishes the conceptual and mathematical bedrock of statistical inference: the axiomatic and philosophical foundations of the subject, the information-theoretic concepts (sufficiency, ancillarity, Fisher information) that measure what data reveal about unknown parameters, and the decision-theoretic framework (risk, admissibility, minimaxity, Bayes rules) used to evaluate and choose among statistical procedures.

### Concepts
- **Foundational topics in statistics** (62Axx) — Examines the axiomatic, philosophical, and conceptual foundations underlying statistical reasoning and inference.
- **Sufficiency and information** (62Bxx) — Studies sufficient and ancillary statistics and information measures such as Fisher information that quantify what data reveal about a parameter.
- **Statistical decision theory** (62Cxx) — Develops the general theory of decision rules, loss and risk functions, admissibility, and minimax and Bayes procedures for statistical decision-making under uncertainty.

nodes.name: Sampling Theory and Distribution Theory
nodes.en: Covers the probabilistic and design-based machinery underlying statistical modeling: the exact and asymptotic distributions of statistics derived from probability models, and the theory of how observational units are selected from finite or infinite populations via survey sampling designs such as stratified and cluster sampling.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá pravděpodobnostní a návrhový aparát statistického modelování: přesná i asymptotická rozdělení statistik odvozených z pravděpodobnostních modelů a teorii výběru jednotek z konečných či nekonečných populací pomocí výběrových plánů šetření, jako jsou stratifikovaný a skupinkový výběr.
nodes.brief:
Covers the probabilistic and design-based machinery underlying statistical modeling: the exact and asymptotic distributions of statistics derived from probability models, and the theory of how observational units are selected from finite or infinite populations via survey sampling designs such as stratified and cluster sampling.

### Concepts
- **Statistical sampling theory and related topics** (62Dxx) — Develops the theory of survey sampling designs — simple random, stratified, cluster, and related schemes — for drawing representative samples from finite or infinite populations.
- **Statistical distribution theory** (62Exx) — Derives the exact and asymptotic probability distributions of statistics and estimators used as the basis for statistical models and inference procedures.

nodes.name: Parametric and Nonparametric Inference
nodes.en: Forms the core estimation-and-testing engine of statistics: parametric inference exploits a probability model known up to a finite-dimensional parameter via maximum likelihood, confidence intervals, and likelihood-ratio tests, while nonparametric inference relaxes distributional assumptions using rank-based, empirical-process, and kernel-smoothing methods.
nodes.cs: Tvoří jádro statistiky v odhadování a testování: parametrická inference těží z pravděpodobnostního modelu známého až na konečněrozměrný parametr pomocí maximální věrohodnosti, intervalů spolehlivosti a testů poměrem věrohodností, zatímco neparametrická inference uvolňuje předpoklady o rozdělení pomocí pořadových metod, empirických procesů a jádrového vyhlazování.
nodes.brief:
Forms the core estimation-and-testing engine of statistics: parametric inference exploits a probability model known up to a finite-dimensional parameter via maximum likelihood, confidence intervals, and likelihood-ratio tests, while nonparametric inference relaxes distributional assumptions using rank-based, empirical-process, and kernel-smoothing methods.

### Concepts
- **Parametric inference** (62Fxx) — Provides estimation and hypothesis-testing methods, including maximum likelihood and likelihood-ratio tests, for models specified up to a finite-dimensional parameter.
- **Nonparametric inference** (62Gxx) — Provides estimation and testing procedures, such as rank-based tests and kernel or empirical-process methods, that avoid strong parametric distributional assumptions.

nodes.name: Multivariate Analysis and Regression
nodes.en: Addresses statistical modeling of relationships among several variables simultaneously: multivariate analysis studies the joint covariance structure of vector-valued observations through tools like principal components, factor analysis, and discriminant analysis, while linear inference builds regression and analysis-of-variance models relating a response to explanatory variables.
nodes.cs: Věnuje se statistickému modelování vztahů mezi několika proměnnými zároveň: mnohorozměrná analýza zkoumá sdruženou kovarianční strukturu vektorových pozorování nástroji, jako jsou hlavní komponenty, faktorová analýza a diskriminační analýza, zatímco lineární inference buduje regresní modely a modely analýzy rozptylu spojující odezvu s vysvětlujícími proměnnými.
nodes.brief:
Addresses statistical modeling of relationships among several variables simultaneously: multivariate analysis studies the joint covariance structure of vector-valued observations through tools like principal components, factor analysis, and discriminant analysis, while linear inference builds regression and analysis-of-variance models relating a response to explanatory variables.

### Concepts
- **Multivariate analysis** (62Hxx) — Studies the joint distribution and covariance structure of vector-valued observations using techniques such as principal component, factor, discriminant, and cluster analysis.
- **Linear inference, regression** (62Jxx) — Develops the theory of linear models, least-squares regression, and analysis of variance for relating a response variable to one or more explanatory variables.

nodes.name: Experimental Design and Sequential Methods
nodes.en: Concerns how statistical data collection is planned and adaptively controlled: the combinatorial and statistical theory of experimental designs — factorial, block, and optimal designs — that allocate treatments efficiently, together with sequential methods in which the sample size or stopping rule adapts to data observed so far, such as sequential probability ratio tests and optimal stopping.
nodes.cs: Týká se plánování a adaptivního řízení sběru statistických dat: kombinatorické a statistické teorie experimentálních plánů — faktoriálních, blokových a optimálních — jež efektivně přidělují zásahy, spolu se sekvenčními metodami, v nichž se rozsah výběru či zastavovací pravidlo přizpůsobují dosud pozorovaným datům, jako jsou sekvenční test poměrem pravděpodobností a optimální zastavování.
nodes.brief:
Concerns how statistical data collection is planned and adaptively controlled: the combinatorial and statistical theory of experimental designs — factorial, block, and optimal designs — that allocate treatments efficiently, together with sequential methods in which the sample size or stopping rule adapts to data observed so far, such as sequential probability ratio tests and optimal stopping.

### Concepts
- **Design of statistical experiments** (62Kxx) — Develops the combinatorial and statistical theory of experimental designs, including factorial, block, and optimal designs, for allocating treatments to maximize information gain.
- **Sequential statistical methods** (62Lxx) — Studies statistical procedures, such as sequential probability ratio tests and optimal stopping rules, in which sampling continues adaptively based on data observed so far.

nodes.name: Stochastic-Process and Survival Inference
nodes.en: Extends inference to data with temporal dependence or incomplete observation: inference from stochastic processes covers estimation and testing for time series, Markov chains, and diffusions, while survival analysis handles time-to-event data subject to censoring or truncation via tools such as the Kaplan–Meier estimator and proportional-hazards regression.
nodes.cs: Rozšiřuje inferenci na data s časovou závislostí či neúplným pozorováním: inference ze stochastických procesů pokrývá odhadování a testování pro časové řady, Markovovy řetězce a difuze, zatímco analýza přežití zpracovává data o době do události podléhající cenzorování či trunkaci nástroji, jako jsou Kaplanův–Meierův odhad a regrese proporcionálních rizik.
nodes.brief:
Extends inference to data with temporal dependence or incomplete observation: inference from stochastic processes covers estimation and testing for time series, Markov chains, and diffusions, while survival analysis handles time-to-event data subject to censoring or truncation via tools such as the Kaplan–Meier estimator and proportional-hazards regression.

### Concepts
- **Inference from stochastic processes** (62Mxx) — Develops estimation and testing methods for data generated by stochastic processes, including time series, Markov chains, and diffusion models.
- **Survival analysis and censored data** (62Nxx) — Studies statistical methods for time-to-event data subject to censoring or truncation, including hazard-rate estimation, the Kaplan–Meier estimator, and proportional-hazards models.

nodes.name: Applications and Reference Tables
nodes.en: Gathers the applied and infrastructural periphery of the discipline: statistical methodology developed for and used within specific substantive fields such as biology, medicine, and engineering, together with the standardized numerical tables of critical values and distribution quantiles that practitioners historically consulted to apply statistical tests.
nodes.cs: Shromažďuje aplikovanou a infrastrukturní periferii oboru: statistickou metodologii vyvinutou pro konkrétní věcné oblasti, jako jsou biologie, medicína a inženýrství, a v nich užívanou, spolu se standardizovanými číselnými tabulkami kritických hodnot a kvantilů rozdělení, do nichž praktici historicky nahlíželi při aplikaci statistických testů.
nodes.brief:
Gathers the applied and infrastructural periphery of the discipline: statistical methodology developed for and used within specific substantive fields such as biology, medicine, and engineering, together with the standardized numerical tables of critical values and distribution quantiles that practitioners historically consulted to apply statistical tests.

### Concepts
- **Applications of statistics** (62Pxx) — Encompasses statistical methodology developed for and applied within specific substantive domains such as biology, medicine, engineering, and the social sciences.
- **Statistical tables** (62Qxx) — Comprises standardized numerical tables of critical values and distribution quantiles used to apply statistical tests and construct confidence intervals.

nodes.name: Statistics on Algebraic and Topological Structures
nodes.en: Extends statistical inference beyond Euclidean vector spaces to data valued on manifolds, shape spaces, trees, networks, and other algebraic or topological structures, encompassing directional statistics, shape analysis, and topological data analysis. This is among the youngest and most actively researched frontiers of the discipline, driven by modern non-Euclidean and structured data.
nodes.cs: Rozšiřuje statistickou inferenci za eukleidovské vektorové prostory na data s hodnotami na varietách, prostorech tvarů, stromech, sítích a dalších algebraických či topologických strukturách; zahrnuje směrovou statistiku, analýzu tvarů a topologickou analýzu dat. Patří k nejmladším a nejaktivněji zkoumaným frontám oboru, poháněným moderními neeukleidovskými a strukturovanými daty.
nodes.brief:
Extends statistical inference beyond Euclidean vector spaces to data valued on manifolds, shape spaces, trees, networks, and other algebraic or topological structures, encompassing directional statistics, shape analysis, and topological data analysis. This is among the youngest and most actively researched frontiers of the discipline, driven by modern non-Euclidean and structured data.

### Concepts
- **Statistics on algebraic and topological structures** (62Rxx) — Develops statistical inference for data valued in non-Euclidean spaces such as manifolds, shape spaces, trees, and networks, including directional statistics and topological data analysis.

nodes.name: Numerical analysis
nodes.en: Numerical analysis designs and analyzes algorithms that compute approximate solutions to continuous mathematical problems — solving equations, evaluating integrals and derivatives, and simulating dynamical systems — together with the error bounds and stability guarantees that certify those approximations. Its subfields range from foundational error and complexity theory through approximation, algebraic, and differential-equation solvers to stochastic methods and applications to the sciences.
nodes.cs: Numerická analýza navrhuje a analyzuje algoritmy, které počítají přibližná řešení spojitých matematických problémů — řešení rovnic, výpočet integrálů a derivací a simulaci dynamických systémů — spolu s odhady chyb a zárukami stability, které tyto aproximace certifikují. Její podobory sahají od základů teorie chyb a složitosti přes aproximační, algebraické a diferenciální řešiče až po stochastické metody a aplikace ve vědách.
nodes.brief:
Numerical analysis makes the continuous computable — and certifies the damage: discretization, iteration and approximation algorithms for equations, integrals and dynamical systems, always paired with error bounds, stability and complexity analysis. Floating-point arithmetic gives it its distinctive rigor-under-roundoff flavor.

Its approximation theory comes from [[02.01.04.29]] and its matrix computations from [[02.01.04.11]]; its largest customers are the differential equations of [[02.01.04.24]] and [[02.01.04.25]], the dynamical systems of [[02.01.04.26]] and the optimization of [[02.01.04.48]]; algorithmic and complexity questions are shared with [[02.01.04.47]]; and simulation for [[01.01]] and engineering is its reason for being.

nodes.name: Foundational Tools, Error Analysis & Computation
nodes.en: Covers the infrastructural and cross-cutting apparatus of numerical analysis: precomputed tables, convergence-acceleration techniques, rigorous error and interval bounds, graphical/nomographic solution methods, and the computational-complexity, parallel, and software aspects shared across all numerical algorithms. These subclasses do not target a single equation type but supply the scaffolding used throughout the discipline.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá infrastrukturní a průřezový aparát numerické analýzy: předpočítané tabulky, techniky urychlování konvergence, rigorózní chybové a intervalové meze, grafické a nomografické metody řešení a aspekty výpočetní složitosti, paralelismu a softwaru společné všem numerickým algoritmům. Tyto podtřídy nemíří na jediný typ rovnic, ale dodávají lešení užívané napříč celou disciplínou.
nodes.brief:
Covers the infrastructural and cross-cutting apparatus of numerical analysis: precomputed tables, convergence-acceleration techniques, rigorous error and interval bounds, graphical/nomographic solution methods, and the computational-complexity, parallel, and software aspects shared across all numerical algorithms. These subclasses do not target a single equation type but supply the scaffolding used throughout the discipline.

### Concepts
- **Tables in numerical analysis** (65Axx) — Covers the construction, organization, and use of numerical tables of function values and mathematical constants as computational aids predating widespread digital computation.
- **Acceleration of convergence in numerical analysis** (65Bxx) — Studies extrapolation and sequence-transformation techniques, such as Aitken's Delta-squared, Richardson extrapolation, and Pade approximants, that speed the convergence of slowly converging sequences and series.
- **Error analysis and interval analysis** (65Gxx) — Quantifies and rigorously bounds rounding, truncation, and propagated error in numerical computation, including interval-arithmetic methods that yield guaranteed enclosures of the true result.
- **Graphical methods in numerical analysis** (65Sxx) — Covers classical graphical and nomographic techniques historically used to obtain approximate numerical solutions without digital computation.
- **Computer aspects of numerical algorithms** (65Yxx) — Studies computational complexity, parallel and distributed implementation, and software/hardware performance aspects of numerical algorithms.

nodes.name: Approximation, Geometry, and Transform Methods
nodes.en: Covers the numerical treatment of continuous functions and geometric objects: interpolation, quadrature, spline and computational-geometric algorithms, numerical conformal mapping and potential theory, integral equations and transforms, and Fourier-analytic algorithms such as the fast Fourier transform. These subclasses share the goal of representing, approximating, or transforming continuous mathematical objects computationally.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá numerické zacházení se spojitými funkcemi a geometrickými objekty: interpolaci, kvadraturu, splajnové a výpočetně-geometrické algoritmy, numerické konformní zobrazování a potenciálovou teorii, integrální rovnice a transformace a fourierovské algoritmy, jako je rychlá Fourierova transformace. Společným cílem těchto podtříd je spojité matematické objekty výpočetně reprezentovat, aproximovat či transformovat.
nodes.brief:
Covers the numerical treatment of continuous functions and geometric objects: interpolation, quadrature, spline and computational-geometric algorithms, numerical conformal mapping and potential theory, integral equations and transforms, and Fourier-analytic algorithms such as the fast Fourier transform. These subclasses share the goal of representing, approximating, or transforming continuous mathematical objects computationally.

### Concepts
- **Numerical approximation and computational geometry (primarily algorithms)** (65Dxx) — Covers algorithms for interpolation, function approximation, numerical differentiation and quadrature, spline construction, and computational-geometric problems such as mesh generation and convex hulls.
- **Numerical methods in complex analysis (potential theory, etc.)** (65Exx) — Treats numerical conformal mapping, potential-theory computations, and other algorithmic techniques for functions of a complex variable.
- **Numerical methods for integral equations, integral transforms** (65Rxx) — Covers discretization and solution algorithms for integral equations, such as Fredholm and Volterra equations, and fast computation of integral transforms.
- **Numerical methods in Fourier analysis** (65Txx) — Covers fast Fourier transform algorithms and numerical trigonometric approximation and interpolation techniques.

nodes.name: Algebraic and Variational Solvers
nodes.en: Covers the numerical solution of algebraic, transcendental, and variational problems: linear systems and eigenproblems, nonlinear equation solving, optimization and mathematical programming, and the extension of these methods to operator equations posed in infinite-dimensional abstract spaces. Together these subclasses form the algorithmic core underlying almost every other numerical discipline.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá numerické řešení algebraických, transcendentních a variačních úloh: lineární systémy a problémy vlastních čísel, řešení nelineárních rovnic, optimalizaci a matematické programování a rozšíření těchto metod na operátorové rovnice v nekonečněrozměrných abstraktních prostorech. Tyto podtřídy dohromady tvoří algoritmické jádro, o něž se opírá téměř každá další numerická disciplína.
nodes.brief:
Covers the numerical solution of algebraic, transcendental, and variational problems: linear systems and eigenproblems, nonlinear equation solving, optimization and mathematical programming, and the extension of these methods to operator equations posed in infinite-dimensional abstract spaces. Together these subclasses form the algorithmic core underlying almost every other numerical discipline.

### Concepts
- **Numerical linear algebra** (65Fxx) — Covers direct and iterative algorithms for solving linear systems, eigenvalue problems, and matrix factorizations, together with their stability and complexity analysis.
- **Nonlinear algebraic or transcendental equations** (65Hxx) — Develops iterative algorithms, including Newton-type, fixed-point, and homotopy/continuation methods, for solving single and systems of nonlinear equations.
- **Numerical analysis in abstract spaces** (65Jxx) — Extends numerical methods and error analysis to operator equations posed in Banach, Hilbert, and other infinite-dimensional abstract spaces.
- **Numerical methods for mathematical programming, optimization and variational techniques** (65Kxx) — Covers algorithms for linear, nonlinear, and combinatorial optimization, variational inequalities, and related mathematical-programming problems.

nodes.name: Differential and Difference Equations
nodes.en: Covers discretization and solution methods for equations describing continuous and discrete evolution: ordinary differential equations, time-dependent partial differential equations, boundary-value partial differential equations, and difference, functional, and recurrence equations. This is the largest thematic cluster in numerical analysis, underpinning simulation across science and engineering.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá diskretizační a řešicí metody pro rovnice popisující spojitý i diskrétní vývoj: obyčejné diferenciální rovnice, časově závislé parciální diferenciální rovnice, okrajové úlohy pro parciální diferenciální rovnice a diferenční, funkcionální a rekurentní rovnice. Jde o největší tematický shluk numerické analýzy, nesoucí simulace napříč vědou a inženýrstvím.
nodes.brief:
Covers discretization and solution methods for equations describing continuous and discrete evolution: ordinary differential equations, time-dependent partial differential equations, boundary-value partial differential equations, and difference, functional, and recurrence equations. This is the largest thematic cluster in numerical analysis, underpinning simulation across science and engineering.

### Concepts
- **Numerical methods for ordinary differential equations** (65Lxx) — Develops and analyzes discretization schemes, including Runge-Kutta, multistep, and collocation methods, for initial- and boundary-value problems in ordinary differential equations.
- **Numerical methods for partial differential equations, initial value and time-dependent initial-boundary value problems** (65Mxx) — Covers finite-difference, finite-element, finite-volume, and spectral schemes for evolutionary, time-dependent partial differential equations.
- **Numerical methods for partial differential equations, boundary value problems** (65Nxx) — Covers discretization methods, including finite element, finite difference, and boundary element methods, for elliptic and other boundary-value partial differential equations.
- **Numerical methods for difference and functional equations, recurrence relations** (65Qxx) — Develops numerical algorithms for solving difference equations, functional equations, and recurrence relations.

nodes.name: Stochastic and Dynamical-Systems Methods
nodes.en: Covers numerical treatment of randomness and of long-term qualitative behavior: Monte Carlo and quasi-Monte Carlo methods, numerical schemes for stochastic differential equations, and computational techniques for invariant sets, bifurcations, Lyapunov exponents, and other dynamical-systems phenomena. Both areas trade classical deterministic error analysis for probabilistic or qualitative-behavior guarantees.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá numerické zacházení s náhodností a s dlouhodobým kvalitativním chováním: metody Monte Carlo a kvazi–Monte Carlo, numerická schémata pro stochastické diferenciální rovnice a výpočetní techniky pro invariantní množiny, bifurkace, Ljapunovovy exponenty a další jevy dynamických systémů. Obě oblasti vyměňují klasickou deterministickou analýzu chyb za pravděpodobnostní záruky či záruky kvalitativního chování.
nodes.brief:
Covers numerical treatment of randomness and of long-term qualitative behavior: Monte Carlo and quasi-Monte Carlo methods, numerical schemes for stochastic differential equations, and computational techniques for invariant sets, bifurcations, Lyapunov exponents, and other dynamical-systems phenomena. Both areas trade classical deterministic error analysis for probabilistic or qualitative-behavior guarantees.

### Concepts
- **Probabilistic methods, stochastic differential equations** (65Cxx) — Develops Monte Carlo, quasi-Monte Carlo, and numerical schemes for stochastic differential equations and other random processes.
- **Numerical problems in dynamical systems** (65Pxx) — Studies numerical computation of invariant sets, bifurcations, Lyapunov exponents, and the long-term behavior of discrete and continuous dynamical systems.

nodes.name: Applications to the Sciences
nodes.en: Covers numerical-analysis methods developed or adapted for specific scientific and engineering application domains, bridging general algorithmic theory with domain-specific computational practice. It serves as the discipline's designated interface to physics, engineering, and other applied fields.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá metody numerické analýzy vyvinuté či uzpůsobené pro konkrétní vědní a inženýrské aplikační domény a přemosťuje obecnou algoritmickou teorii s doménově specifickou výpočetní praxí. Slouží jako určené rozhraní disciplíny k fyzice, inženýrství a dalším aplikovaným oborům.
nodes.brief:
Covers numerical-analysis methods developed or adapted for specific scientific and engineering application domains, bridging general algorithmic theory with domain-specific computational practice. It serves as the discipline's designated interface to physics, engineering, and other applied fields.

### Concepts
- **Applications to the sciences** (65Zxx) — Covers numerical-analysis techniques tailored to specific application domains in physics, engineering, and other sciences.

nodes.name: Computer science
nodes.en: Computer science, in the MSC classification, covers the mathematical study of computation: the theory of algorithms and computational complexity, discrete structures underlying computing, the design of computing systems and data structures, and the mathematical foundations of artificial intelligence and machine learning. It groups the branches of mathematics that model what can be computed, how efficiently, and how intelligent behavior can be automated.
nodes.cs: Počítačová věda v klasifikaci MSC pokrývá matematické studium výpočtu: teorii algoritmů a výpočetní složitosti, diskrétní struktury, na nichž výpočet stojí, návrh výpočetních systémů a datových struktur a matematické základy umělé inteligence a strojového učení. Sdružuje obory matematiky, které modelují, co lze vypočítat, s jakou efektivitou a jak lze automatizovat inteligentní chování.
nodes.brief:
Computer science, as the MSC classifies it, is the mathematics of computation: algorithms and computational complexity, discrete structures, data structures and computing methodologies, and the mathematical foundations of artificial intelligence and machine learning. The P versus NP question is its emblematic open problem.

Its roots are the computability theory of [[02.01.04.03]]; its native mathematics is the combinatorics of [[02.01.04.04]]; algorithmic questions in continuous mathematics are shared with [[02.01.04.46]], in geometry with [[02.01.04.38]], and in coding and circuits with [[02.01.04.51]]; machine-learning theory converses with the statistics of [[02.01.04.45]]. The full discipline of computing extends beyond this mathematical core, and its computational-mathematics face within mathematics itself is [[02.01.03]].

nodes.name: Computational Theory and Algorithms
nodes.en: Covers the formal foundations of computation: models of what can be computed, the resource costs of computing it, the discrete combinatorial and graph-theoretic structures that computational problems are built from, and the design and rigorous analysis of algorithms that solve them. This area supplies the theoretical backbone shared by every other branch of computer science, from software verification to artificial intelligence, and includes open problems such as P versus NP that remain central to the field.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá formální základy výpočtu: modely toho, co lze vypočítat, náklady na zdroje potřebné k výpočtu, diskrétní kombinatorické a grafové struktury, na nichž jsou výpočetní problémy postaveny, a návrh a přísnou analýzu algoritmů, které je řeší. Tato oblast tvoří teoretickou páteř sdílenou všemi ostatními obory počítačové vědy, od verifikace softwaru po umělou inteligenci, a zahrnuje otevřené problémy jako P versus NP, které zůstávají pro obor klíčové.
nodes.brief:
Covers the formal foundations of computation: models of what can be computed, the resource costs of computing it, the discrete combinatorial and graph-theoretic structures that computational problems are built from, and the design and rigorous analysis of algorithms that solve them. This area supplies the theoretical backbone shared by every other branch of computer science, from software verification to artificial intelligence. Central open problems, notably P versus NP and 

### Concepts
- **Theory of software** (68Nxx) — Studies formal methods for specifying, semantically defining, and verifying the correctness of programs and programming languages.
- **Theory of computing** (68Qxx) — Studies abstract models of computation, computability, and computational complexity that bound what algorithms can achieve and at what cost.
- **Discrete mathematics in relation to computer science** (68Rxx) — Studies the combinatorial, graph-theoretic, and coding-theoretic structures that underpin computational problems and their algorithmic treatment.
- **Algorithms in computer science** (68Wxx) — Studies the design, correctness proofs, and complexity analysis of sequential, parallel, distributed, and randomized algorithms.

nodes.name: Systems, Data, and Computational Practice
nodes.en: Addresses the mathematical modeling of how computers are organized as physical and networked systems, how data is structured, stored, and retrieved, and how computational techniques are applied to concrete practical and scientific tasks. It also encompasses the software infrastructure — proof assistants, symbolic computation systems, computational libraries — that mathematics itself relies on to conduct and verify research; these subareas are mature, engineering-adjacent disciplines rather than open theoretical frontiers.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se matematickým modelováním toho, jak jsou počítače organizovány jako fyzické a síťové systémy, jak jsou data strukturována, ukládána a vyhledávána, a jak se výpočetní techniky aplikují na konkrétní praktické a vědecké úlohy. Zahrnuje také softwarovou infrastrukturu — asistenty pro dokazování, systémy symbolických výpočtů, výpočetní knihovny — na niž se matematika sama spoléhá při provádění a ověřování výzkumu; tyto podobory jsou vyzrálé, inženýrsky orientované disciplíny, nikoli otevřené teoretické fronty.
nodes.brief:
Addresses the mathematical modeling of how computers are organized as physical and networked systems, how data is structured, stored, and retrieved, and how computational techniques are applied to concrete practical and scientific tasks. It also encompasses the software infrastructure, proof assistants, symbolic computation systems, computational libraries, that mathematics itself relies on to conduct and verify research. These subareas are mature engineering-adjacent discipl

### Concepts
- **Computer system organization** (68Mxx) — Studies the architecture, networking, distribution, parallelism, security, and reliability of computer hardware and system-level software.
- **Theory of data** (68Pxx) — Studies the mathematical organization, storage, retrieval, and structural properties of data, including databases and abstract data types.
- **Computing methodologies and applications** (68Uxx) — Studies computational techniques applied to tasks such as graphics, image processing, and simulation across scientific and engineering domains.
- **Computer science support for mathematical research and practice** (68Vxx) — Studies computational tools, proof assistants, and symbolic-computation software built specifically to support mathematical discovery and verification.

nodes.name: Artificial Intelligence
nodes.en: Studies the mathematical foundations of automated reasoning, machine learning, knowledge representation, and intelligent agent behavior. It spans classical symbolic approaches such as logic-based planning and search, alongside statistical and neural-network learning theory, and underlies the current wave of large-scale generative models. The field is a fast-moving research frontier where foundational questions about generalization, learnability, and the theoretical limits of learning algorithms remain actively contested.
nodes.cs: Studuje matematické základy automatizovaného usuzování, strojového učení, reprezentace znalostí a chování inteligentních agentů. Zahrnuje klasické symbolické přístupy, jako je plánování a prohledávání založené na logice, spolu se statistickou teorií učení a teorií neuronových sítí, a stojí za současnou vlnou rozsáhlých generativních modelů. Obor je rychle se vyvíjející výzkumnou frontou, kde zůstávají aktivně diskutovány základní otázky zobecnění, naučitelnosti a teoretických limitů učicích algoritmů.
nodes.brief:
Studies the mathematical foundations of automated reasoning, machine learning, knowledge representation, and intelligent agent behavior. It spans classical symbolic approaches such as logic-based planning and search, alongside statistical and neural-network learning theory, and underlies the current wave of large-scale generative models. The field is a fast-moving research frontier where foundational questions about generalization, learnability, and the theoretical limits of 

### Concepts
- **Artificial intelligence** (68Txx) — Studies the mathematical theory of automated reasoning, learning, knowledge representation, and the design of intelligent agents.

nodes.name: Operations research, mathematical programming
nodes.en: Operations research and mathematical programming apply mathematical modeling and optimization to decision-making under constraints: queueing, inventory, scheduling, and game-theoretic models of operational systems, alongside the theory and algorithms of linear, nonlinear, integer, and combinatorial optimization. The field turns real-world resource-allocation and decision problems into solvable mathematical programs.
nodes.cs: Operační výzkum a matematické programování aplikují matematické modelování a optimalizaci na rozhodování za omezujících podmínek: teorii front, zásob, plánování a teoreticko-herní modely provozních systémů spolu s teorií a algoritmy lineární, nelineární, celočíselné a kombinatorické optimalizace. Obor převádí reálné problémy alokace zdrojů a rozhodování na řešitelné matematické programy.
nodes.brief:
Operations research turns decision problems into mathematics — queueing, inventory, scheduling, network flows, reliability — while mathematical programming supplies the optimization engine: linear, nonlinear, integer, convex and stochastic, with duality theory and algorithms from the simplex method to interior-point and first-order schemes.

Continuous optimal control is its variational sibling in [[02.01.04.36]]; polyhedral combinatorics ties integer programming to [[02.01.04.38]] and [[02.01.04.04]]; queueing and stochastic models rest on [[02.01.04.44]]; algorithmic complexity is shared with [[02.01.04.47]]; and its equilibrium and incentive questions border the game theory of [[02.01.04.49]]. It is where mathematics most directly prices, schedules and routes the industrial world.

nodes.name: Operations Research and Management Science
nodes.en: Operations research and management science builds quantitative models — queueing systems, inventory control, scheduling, network flows, reliability, and simulation — to guide decision-making in industrial, logistical, and organizational systems. It draws on probability, combinatorics, and applied optimization to analyze the performance and control of complex service and production processes. As a mature interface between mathematics and practice, it continues to expand into new application domains such as supply-chain logistics and service systems.
nodes.cs: Operační výzkum a manažerská věda budují kvantitativní modely — systémy hromadné obsluhy, řízení zásob, rozvrhování, síťové toky, spolehlivost a simulaci — jako oporu rozhodování v průmyslových, logistických a organizačních systémech. Čerpají z pravděpodobnosti, kombinatoriky a aplikované optimalizace k analýze výkonnosti a řízení složitých obslužných a výrobních procesů. Jako zralé rozhraní mezi matematikou a praxí se dále rozšiřují do nových aplikačních oblastí, jako jsou logistika dodavatelských řetězců a obslužné systémy.
nodes.brief:
Operations research and management science builds quantitative models — queueing systems, inventory control, scheduling, network flows, reliability, and simulation — to guide decision-making in industrial, logistical, and organizational systems. It draws on probability, combinatorics, and applied optimization to analyze the performance and control of complex service and production processes. As a mature interface between mathematics and practice, it continues to expand into n

### Concepts
- **Operations research and management science** (90Bxx) — Covers the mathematical modeling and analysis of operational systems — queueing, inventory, scheduling, reliability, and network flows — to support decisions in industry and management.

nodes.name: Mathematical Programming
nodes.en: Mathematical programming is the theory and algorithmic study of optimizing an objective function over a feasible region defined by equality, inequality, or combinatorial constraints, spanning linear, nonlinear, integer, convex, and stochastic programming. It furnishes the duality theory, complexity analysis, and solution methods — simplex, interior-point, branch-and-bound, cutting-plane, and first-order algorithms — that underlie computational optimization across engineering, economics, and the sciences.
nodes.cs: Matematické programování je teorie a algoritmické studium optimalizace účelové funkce nad přípustnou množinou zadanou rovnostmi, nerovnostmi či kombinatorickými omezeními; zahrnuje lineární, nelineární, celočíselné, konvexní a stochastické programování. Poskytuje teorii duality, analýzu složitosti a metody řešení — simplexovou metodu, metody vnitřního bodu, větvení a mezí, řezné nadroviny a algoritmy prvního řádu — na nichž stojí výpočetní optimalizace napříč inženýrstvím, ekonomií a vědami.
nodes.brief:
Mathematical programming is the theory and algorithmic study of optimizing an objective function over a feasible region defined by equality, inequality, or combinatorial constraints, spanning linear, nonlinear, integer, convex, and stochastic programming. It furnishes the duality theory, complexity analysis, and solution methods — simplex, interior-point, branch-and-bound, cutting-plane, and first-order algorithms — that underlie computational optimization across engineering,

### Concepts
- **Mathematical programming** (90Cxx) — Studies the theory, geometry, and algorithms for optimizing an objective function subject to constraints, including linear, nonlinear, integer, convex, and stochastic programming.

nodes.name: Game theory, economics, finance, and other social and behavioral sciences
nodes.en: Groups the mathematical treatment of strategic and economic decision-making with the formal study of social and behavioral phenomena (MSC 91-XX). Spans the axiomatic core of game theory and general-equilibrium economics, the probabilistic machinery of mathematical finance and actuarial risk, and models of social networks, cognition, and collective behavior built from probability, graph theory, and dynamical systems.
nodes.cs: Sdružuje matematické zpracování strategického a ekonomického rozhodování s formálním studiem sociálních a behaviorálních jevů (MSC 91-XX). Zahrnuje axiomatické jádro teorie her a ekonomie obecné rovnováhy, pravděpodobnostní aparát matematických financí a pojistně-matematických rizik a modely sociálních sítí, kognice a kolektivního chování budované na teorii pravděpodobnosti, teorii grafů a dynamických systémech.
nodes.brief:
Strategic interaction, markets and risk get their mathematics here: game theory's equilibria and mechanism design, general-equilibrium and welfare economics, the stochastic calculus of quantitative finance and actuarial science, and formal models of social networks, cognition and collective behavior.

Its optimization and duality tools come from [[02.01.04.48]], its stochastic machinery — martingales, diffusions, Itô calculus — from [[02.01.04.44]], and its empirical confrontation with data from [[02.01.04.45]]; evolutionary game dynamics borrows from [[02.01.04.26]], network models from [[02.01.04.04]], and algorithmic game theory from [[02.01.04.47]]. It is the largest MSC gateway from mathematics into the social and behavioral sciences.

nodes.name: Game Theory, Mathematical Economics, and Finance
nodes.en: Studies formal models of strategic and economic decision-making: how rational (and boundedly rational) agents compete, cooperate, trade, and price risk. Spans the axiomatic core of game theory, general-equilibrium and welfare theory in economics, and the stochastic-analytic machinery of modern quantitative finance and actuarial risk. These three areas share a common toolkit — optimization, equilibrium concepts, and probabilistic modeling — and constantly cross-fertilize, as when game-theoretic equilibrium ideas shape asset-pricing and market-design models.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá formální modely strategického a ekonomického rozhodování: jak racionální (i omezeně racionální) aktéři soutěží, spolupracují, obchodují a oceňují riziko. Sahá od axiomatického jádra teorie her přes teorii celkové rovnováhy a blahobytu v ekonomii až po stochasticko-analytický aparát moderních kvantitativních financí a pojistné matematiky. Tyto tři oblasti sdílejí společný instrumentář — optimalizaci, koncepty rovnováhy a pravděpodobnostní modelování — a neustále se vzájemně obohacují, například když pojmy rovnováhy z teorie her formují modely oceňování aktiv a návrhu trhů.
nodes.brief:
Studies formal models of strategic and economic decision-making: how rational (and boundedly rational) agents compete, cooperate, trade, and price risk. Spans the axiomatic core of game theory, general-equilibrium and welfare theory in economics, and the stochastic-analytic machinery of modern quantitative finance and actuarial risk. These three areas share a common toolkit — optimization, equilibrium concepts, and probabilistic modeling — and constantly cross-fertilize, e.g.

### Concepts
- **Game theory** (91Axx) — Studies mathematical models of strategic interaction among rational agents, covering cooperative and noncooperative games, equilibrium concepts, and extensive- and normal-form representations.
- **Mathematical economics** (91Bxx) — Develops formal mathematical models of economic behavior, market exchange, general equilibrium, welfare, and growth, distinct from the statistical methodology of econometrics.
- **Actuarial science and mathematical finance** (91Gxx) — Applies stochastic analysis and probability theory to derivative pricing, risk management, portfolio optimization, and insurance/actuarial modeling.

nodes.name: Mathematical Social and Behavioral Sciences
nodes.en: Encompasses the mathematical formalization of human collective and individual behavior outside the strictly economic sphere: social structure and networks, cognition and perception, cultural and group dynamics, and other social-science domains such as political science and linguistics. These subclasses borrow tools from probability, graph theory, dynamical systems, and measurement theory to build testable models of phenomena that are otherwise studied qualitatively. The field is younger and more heterogeneous than mathematical economics, and it grows with the availability of large-scale social data.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje matematickou formalizaci kolektivního i individuálního lidského chování mimo úzce ekonomickou sféru: sociální strukturu a sítě, kognici a vnímání, kulturní a skupinovou dynamiku a další společenskovědní oblasti, jako jsou politologie a lingvistika. Tyto podtřídy si vypůjčují nástroje z pravděpodobnosti, teorie grafů, dynamických systémů a teorie měření k budování testovatelných modelů jevů, které se jinak studují kvalitativně. Obor je mladší a různorodější než matematická ekonomie a roste s dostupností rozsáhlých sociálních dat.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses the mathematical formalization of human collective and individual behavior outside the strictly economic sphere: social structure and networks, cognition and perception, cultural and group dynamics, and other social-science domains such as political science and linguistics. These subclasses borrow tools from probability, graph theory, dynamical systems, and measurement theory to build testable models of phenomena that are otherwise studied qualitatively. The field

### Concepts
- **Social and behavioral sciences: general topics** (91Cxx) — Covers general-purpose mathematical and structural models — measurement, networks, and data representations — used across the social and behavioral sciences without being tied to one subfield.
- **Mathematical sociology (including anthropology)** (91Dxx) — Formalizes social structure, group dynamics, social networks, and cultural transmission using mathematical and computational models of collective behavior.
- **Mathematical psychology** (91Exx) — Models cognitive processes, perception, learning, and decision-making through mathematical frameworks, distinct from the statistical methodology of psychometrics.
- **Other social and behavioral sciences (mathematical treatment)** (91Fxx) — Provides mathematical treatments of social-science domains such as political science, geography, and linguistics that fall outside the more specific 91C–91E subclasses.

nodes.name: Systems theory; control
nodes.en: Groups the mathematical theory of dynamical and control systems (MSC 93-XX): the abstract formalization of states, inputs, and outputs together with structural properties such as controllability and observability, and the analysis of stability, robustness, and stochastic behavior under feedback and disturbance. Optimal control proper is classified separately under the calculus of variations (MSC 49-XX).
nodes.cs: Sdružuje matematickou teorii dynamických a řídicích systémů (MSC 93-XX): abstraktní formalizaci stavů, vstupů a výstupů spolu se strukturálními vlastnostmi, jako je řiditelnost a pozorovatelnost, a analýzu stability, robustnosti a stochastického chování při zpětné vazbě a poruchách. Optimální řízení je klasifikováno samostatně v rámci variačního počtu (MSC 49-XX).
nodes.brief:
Control theory studies systems with inputs and outputs that can be steered: controllability, observability, realization and canonical forms on the structural side; stability, robustness and feedback design — Lyapunov and input-output methods — on the behavioral side; and filtering, estimation and stochastic control where noise enters.

Its models are the differential and difference equations of [[02.01.04.24]] and [[02.01.04.27]], analyzed for distributed-parameter systems with the operator and semigroup theory of [[02.01.04.35]]; optimal control proper is delegated to [[02.01.04.36]], stochastic filtering rests on [[02.01.04.44]], the state-space calculus on [[02.01.04.11]], and signal and circuit questions border [[02.01.04.51]]. It is the mathematics by which engineering makes dynamics obey.

nodes.name: Foundations, System Structure, and Model Classes
nodes.en: Establishes the abstract mathematical framework of dynamical and control systems—states, inputs, outputs, and their algebraic or categorical formalization—together with the structural properties (controllability, observability, realization, canonical forms) that determine when a system can be steered or reconstructed from data. It further classifies control systems by model type, spanning linear, nonlinear, time-varying, discrete-time, hybrid, delay, and distributed-parameter systems.
nodes.cs: Ustavuje abstraktní matematický rámec dynamických a řídicích systémů — stavy, vstupy, výstupy a jejich algebraickou či kategoriální formalizaci — spolu se strukturálními vlastnostmi (řiditelnost, pozorovatelnost, realizace, kanonické tvary), jež určují, kdy lze systém řídit nebo rekonstruovat z dat. Dále třídí řídicí systémy podle typu modelu: lineární, nelineární, časově proměnné, diskrétní, hybridní, se zpožděním i systémy s rozloženými parametry.
nodes.brief:
Establishes the abstract mathematical framework of dynamical and control systems—states, inputs, outputs, and their algebraic or categorical formalization—together with the structural properties (controllability, observability, realization, canonical forms) that determine when a system can be steered or reconstructed from data. It further classifies control systems by model type, spanning linear, nonlinear, time-varying, discrete-time, hybrid, delay, and distributed-parameter

### Concepts
- **General systems theory** (93Axx) — Develops the abstract mathematical formalism of dynamical systems—states, inputs, outputs, and their transformations—independent of any particular model class.
- **Controllability, observability, and system structure** (93Bxx) — Studies structural properties of control systems such as controllability, observability, realization theory, canonical forms, and system equivalence or decomposition.
- **Model systems in control theory** (93Cxx) — Classifies and analyzes control systems according to model type, including linear, nonlinear, time-varying, discrete-time, hybrid, delay, and distributed-parameter systems.

nodes.name: Stability, Robustness, and Stochastic Control
nodes.en: Analyzes the qualitative behavior of feedback systems under perturbation, using Lyapunov-type and input-output methods to certify stability, robustness margins, and stabilizability by feedback design. It extends this analysis to systems driven by random disturbances, encompassing stochastic stability, filtering, estimation, and stochastic or adaptive optimal control. Together these topics govern whether a controlled system behaves reliably in the presence of uncertainty, whether the disturbance is deterministic model error or random noise.
nodes.cs: Analyzuje kvalitativní chování zpětnovazebních systémů při poruchách; ljapunovskými metodami a metodami vstup–výstup certifikuje stabilitu, meze robustnosti a stabilizovatelnost zpětnovazebním návrhem. Tuto analýzu rozšiřuje na systémy buzené náhodnými poruchami, včetně stochastické stability, filtrace, odhadování a stochastického či adaptivního optimálního řízení. Tato témata společně rozhodují o tom, zda se řízený systém chová spolehlivě za nejistoty, ať už je poruchou deterministická chyba modelu, nebo náhodný šum.
nodes.brief:
Analyzes the qualitative behavior of feedback systems under perturbation, using Lyapunov-type and input-output methods to certify stability, robustness margins, and stabilizability by feedback design. It extends this analysis to systems driven by random disturbances, encompassing stochastic stability, filtering, estimation, and stochastic or adaptive optimal control. Together these topics govern whether a controlled system behaves reliably in the presence of uncertainty, whet

### Concepts
- **Stability of control systems** (93Dxx) — Investigates Lyapunov and input-output stability, robustness margins, and feedback stabilization of control systems.
- **Stochastic systems and control** (93Exx) — Treats control, filtering, and estimation problems for systems subject to random disturbances, including stochastic stability and stochastic optimal control.

nodes.name: Information and communication theory, circuits
nodes.en: Groups the mathematical theory of information transmission with the graph- and logic-theoretic study of circuits (MSC 94-XX): entropy, channel capacity, and the algebraic construction of error-correcting codes on one side, and the connectivity, switching, and Boolean-complexity analysis of electrical and logical networks on the other, alongside residual topics such as fuzzy and possibility-theoretic generalizations of classical information measures.
nodes.cs: Sdružuje matematickou teorii přenosu informace se studiem obvodů pomocí teorie grafů a logiky (MSC 94-XX): na jedné straně entropii, kapacitu kanálu a algebraickou konstrukci samoopravných kódů, na druhé straně analýzu konektivity, přepínání a booleovské složitosti elektrických a logických sítí, spolu s doplňkovými tématy — zobecněními klasických informačních měr pomocí fuzzy množin a teorie možností (possibility theory).
nodes.brief:
Shannon's questions organize this pillar: how much information a source produces (entropy), how fast a channel can carry it (capacity), and how coding protects it — from algebraic error-correcting codes to modern network, polar and quantum codes — together with the graph- and logic-theoretic analysis of circuits and switching networks.

Its probabilistic core rests on [[02.01.04.44]]; code constructions draw on the finite fields of [[02.01.04.08]], the designs of [[02.01.04.04]] and the algebraic curves of [[02.01.04.10]]; circuit complexity and algorithmic questions are shared with [[02.01.04.47]]; signal representation with [[02.01.04.30]]; and quantum information keeps a channel open to [[01.01]].

nodes.name: Communication and Coding Theory
nodes.en: Covers the mathematical theory of transmitting, compressing, and protecting information: entropy and channel-capacity results in the Shannon tradition, source and channel coding, and the algebraic/combinatorial construction and decoding of error-correcting and error-detecting codes. These two subclasses form the mathematical core of digital communication engineering, underpinning everything from data compression to reliable storage and wireless transmission. Classical linear and algebraic coding theory is settled textbook material, while codes for modern settings — network, polar, and quantum codes — remain an active research frontier.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá matematickou teorii přenosu, komprese a ochrany informace: entropii a výsledky o kapacitě kanálu v shannonovské tradici, zdrojové a kanálové kódování a algebraicko-kombinatorickou konstrukci a dekódování samoopravných a detekčních kódů. Tyto dvě podtřídy tvoří matematické jádro digitální komunikační techniky a stojí za vším od komprese dat po spolehlivé ukládání a bezdrátový přenos. Klasická lineární a algebraická teorie kódování je ustálenou učebnicovou látkou, zatímco kódy pro moderní kontexty — síťové, polární a kvantové — zůstávají živou výzkumnou frontou.
nodes.brief:
Covers the mathematical theory of transmitting, compressing, and protecting information: entropy and channel-capacity results in the Shannon tradition, source and channel coding, and the algebraic/combinatorial construction and decoding of error-correcting and error-detecting codes. These two subclasses form the mathematical core of digital communication engineering, underpinning everything from data compression to reliable storage and wireless transmission. Classical linear 

### Concepts
- **Communication, information** (94Axx) — Studies the mathematical foundations of information transmission, storage, and processing, including entropy, source coding, and channel capacity in the Shannon-theoretic tradition.
- **Theory of error-correcting codes and error-detecting codes** (94Bxx) — Studies algebraic and combinatorial constructions of codes — block, cyclic, convolutional, and modern sparse-graph codes — together with their decoding algorithms, that protect transmitted or stored data against noise and errors.

nodes.name: Circuits, Networks, and General Information-Theoretic Topics
nodes.en: Groups the graph- and logic-theoretic study of electrical and switching circuits and networks with the residual miscellaneous topics of the discipline, chiefly fuzzy-set and possibility-theoretic generalizations of classical information measures. Circuit and network theory analyzes connectivity, switching, and Boolean-complexity properties of physical and logical networks, closely tied to combinatorics and complexity theory. The miscellaneous subclass captures uncertainty-based generalizations of information theory built on fuzzy sets and possibility measures.
nodes.cs: Sdružuje grafově a logicky pojaté studium elektrických a spínacích obvodů a sítí se zbytkovými tématy oboru, především fuzzy a posibilistickými zobecněními klasických informačních měr. Teorie obvodů a sítí analyzuje souvislost, spínání a booleovskou složitost fyzických i logických sítí v těsné vazbě na kombinatoriku a teorii složitosti. Zbytková podtřída zachycuje zobecnění teorie informace založená na neurčitosti, vystavěná na fuzzy množinách a mírách možnosti.
nodes.brief:
Groups the graph- and logic-theoretic study of electrical and switching circuits and networks with the residual miscellaneous topics of the discipline, chiefly fuzzy-set and possibility-theoretic generalizations of classical information measures. Circuit and network theory analyzes connectivity, switching, and Boolean-complexity properties of physical and logical networks, closely tied to combinatorics and complexity theory. The miscellaneous subclass captures uncertainty-bas

### Concepts
- **Circuits, networks** (94Cxx) — Studies mathematical models of electrical and logical circuits and networks, including graph-theoretic connectivity analysis and Boolean-circuit complexity of switching networks.
- **Miscellaneous topics in information and communication theory** (94Dxx) — Covers topics in information and communication theory not classified elsewhere in 94-XX, notably fuzzy sets, possibility theory, and other uncertainty-based generalizations of classical information measures.

nodes.name: Mathematics education
nodes.en: Groups the theory, research, and practice of teaching and learning mathematics (MSC 97-XX): the historical, institutional, and cognitive foundations of mathematics-education research, the didactics of core curricular content from arithmetic to analysis, the pedagogy of quantitative and computational topics such as statistics and modeling, and the educational materials, media, and technology that support instruction.
nodes.cs: Sdružuje teorii, výzkum a praxi výuky a učení se matematice (MSC 97-XX): historické, institucionální a kognitivní základy výzkumu matematického vzdělávání, didaktiku klíčového kurikulárního obsahu od aritmetiky po matematickou analýzu, pedagogiku kvantitativních a výpočetních témat, jako je statistika a modelování, a výukové materiály, média a technologie podporující výuku.
nodes.brief:
Mathematics education studies how mathematics is taught and learned: the cognitive, social and institutional foundations of the field, the didactics of each curricular pillar from arithmetic to analysis, the newer pedagogy of statistics, modeling and computational topics, and the materials and technology of instruction. It is the only MSC class whose subject is people engaging with mathematics rather than mathematics itself.

Its research methods are empirical as much as mathematical; its historical dimension is shared with [[02.01.04.02]] and its popularization side with [[02.01.04.01]]. The didactics of specific content areas mirror the pillars themselves — the teaching of probability and statistics tracks [[02.01.04.44]] and [[02.01.04.45]], and that of computational topics tracks [[02.01.04.47]] and [[02.01.04.46]].

nodes.name: Foundations, Policy, and Research in Mathematics Education
nodes.en: Encompasses the historical, social, institutional, and psychological dimensions of how mathematics is taught and learned, together with the general theory and practice of instruction. Includes the empirical and theoretical methodologies specific to mathematics-education research, the study of curricula and educational systems across cultures and eras, and the cognitive and affective processes underlying mathematical learning. This block forms the meta-disciplinary scaffolding on which content-specific didactics builds.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje historické, sociální, institucionální a psychologické dimenze výuky a učení matematiky spolu s obecnou teorií a praxí vyučování. Patří sem empirické i teoretické metodologie vlastní výzkumu matematického vzdělávání, studium kurikul a vzdělávacích systémů napříč kulturami a epochami a kognitivní i afektivní procesy, o něž se matematické učení opírá. Tento blok tvoří metadisciplinární lešení, na němž staví didaktiky jednotlivých obsahových oblastí.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses the historical, social, institutional, and psychological dimensions of how mathematics is taught and learned, together with the general theory and practice of instruction. Includes the empirical and theoretical methodologies specific to mathematics-education research, the study of curricula and educational systems across cultures and eras, and the cognitive and affective processes underlying mathematical learning. This block forms the meta-disciplinary scaffolding

### Concepts
- **History and society (aspects of mathematics education)** (97Axx) — Studies the historical development of mathematics teaching practices and the social, cultural, and political contexts that shape mathematics education.
- **Educational policy and systems** (97Bxx) — Examines the organization, governance, standards, and policy frameworks that structure mathematics education at institutional and national levels.
- **Psychology of mathematics education, research in mathematics education** (97Cxx) — Investigates the cognitive, affective, and social processes involved in learning and teaching mathematics, and develops the theoretical and empirical methodologies of mathematics education research.
- **Education and instruction in mathematics** (97Dxx) — Addresses general pedagogical theory, curriculum design, teaching methods, and instructional practice applicable across mathematical topics.

nodes.name: Education in Core Mathematical Content Areas
nodes.en: Covers the didactics of the classical pillars of the mathematics curriculum: logical and set-theoretic foundations, arithmetic and elementary number theory, geometry, algebra, and analysis. Each subclass addresses discipline-specific teaching methods, common student misconceptions, curricular sequencing, and assessment tailored to that branch of mathematics. This is the most institutionally stable and longest-studied slice of mathematics education, spanning primary-school arithmetic through university-level analysis.
nodes.cs: Pokrývá didaktiku klasických pilířů matematického kurikula: logických a množinových základů, aritmetiky a elementární teorie čísel, geometrie, algebry a analýzy. Každá podtřída se věnuje oborově specifickým vyučovacím metodám, běžným mylným představám žáků, řazení učiva a hodnocení šitému na míru dané větvi matematiky. Jde o institucionálně nejstabilnější a nejdéle studovanou výseč matematického vzdělávání, sahající od aritmetiky základní školy po vysokoškolskou analýzu.
nodes.brief:
Covers the didactics of the classical pillars of the mathematics curriculum: logical and set-theoretic foundations, arithmetic and elementary number theory, geometry, algebra, and analysis. Each subclass addresses discipline-specific teaching methods, common student misconceptions, curricular sequencing, and assessment tailored to that branch of mathematics. This is the most institutionally stable and longest-studied slice of mathematics education, spanning primary-school ari

### Concepts
- **Education of foundations of mathematics** (97Exx) — Concerns the teaching of logic, set theory, and the axiomatic foundations underlying mathematical reasoning.
- **Education of arithmetic and number theory** (97Fxx) — Covers pedagogy for numeration, arithmetic operations, and elementary number-theoretic concepts, primarily in primary and early-secondary curricula.
- **Geometry education** (97Gxx) — Studies the teaching and learning of Euclidean and other geometries, spatial reasoning, and geometric proof.
- **Algebra education** (97Hxx) — Addresses instruction in algebraic notation, equation solving, structures, and symbolic reasoning across educational levels.
- **Analysis education** (97Ixx) — Concerns pedagogy for calculus and mathematical analysis, including limits, continuity, differentiation, and integration.

nodes.name: Education in Quantitative, Computational, and Applied Mathematics
nodes.en: Concerns the teaching of combinatorics, graph theory, probability and statistics, mathematical modeling and applications, numerical methods, and computer science as they enter mathematics curricula. These areas have grown rapidly in curricular importance with the rise of data science, computational thinking, and STEM-integrated instruction. Pedagogical research here actively negotiates how to balance procedural computation with conceptual understanding, and instructional standards in these areas continue to evolve.
nodes.cs: Týká se výuky kombinatoriky, teorie grafů, pravděpodobnosti a statistiky, matematického modelování a aplikací, numerických metod a informatiky tam, kde vstupují do matematických kurikul. Kurikulární význam těchto oblastí rychle roste s nástupem datové vědy, informatického myšlení a integrované výuky STEM. Pedagogický výzkum zde aktivně hledá rovnováhu mezi procedurálním počítáním a konceptuálním porozuměním a výukové standardy v těchto oblastech se nadále vyvíjejí.
nodes.brief:
Concerns the teaching of combinatorics, graph theory, probability and statistics, mathematical modeling and applications, numerical methods, and computer science as they enter mathematics curricula. These areas have grown rapidly in curricular importance with the rise of data science, computational thinking, and STEM-integrated instruction. Pedagogical research here actively negotiates how to balance procedural computation with conceptual understanding, and instructional stan

### Concepts
- **Education of combinatorics, graph theory, probability theory, and statistics** (97Kxx) — Covers the teaching of discrete mathematics, counting, graph structures, probabilistic reasoning, and statistical literacy.
- **Education of mathematical modeling and applications of mathematics** (97Mxx) — Studies pedagogy for translating real-world problems into mathematical models and applying mathematics across disciplines.
- **Education of numerical mathematics** (97Nxx) — Addresses teaching of numerical methods, approximation, and computational techniques for solving mathematical problems.
- **Computer science (educational aspects)** (97Pxx) — Covers the pedagogy of computer science topics, programming, algorithms, and computational thinking, as taught within or alongside mathematics curricula.

nodes.name: Educational Materials, Media, and Technology in Mathematics Education
nodes.en: Covers the design, evaluation, and classroom use of instructional materials and technology for teaching mathematics — textbooks, manipulatives, educational software, and digital or online platforms, including intelligent tutoring systems and adaptive learning tools. Addresses efficacy, equity of access, and integration challenges as these tools evolve, distinct from the policy, research, and content-area concerns handled elsewhere in mathematics education.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se návrhem, hodnocením a využitím výukových materiálů a technologií pro výuku matematiky, jako jsou učebnice, manipulativní pomůcky, vzdělávací software a digitální či online platformy, včetně inteligentních výukových systémů a adaptivních výukových nástrojů. Řeší efektivitu, rovný přístup a začlenění do výuky s vývojem těchto nástrojů, na rozdíl od otázek politiky, výzkumu a obsahových oblastí řešených jinde v didaktice matematiky.
nodes.brief:
Studies the design, evaluation, and pedagogical use of textbooks, manipulatives, software, and digital or online platforms for teaching mathematics, including intelligent tutoring systems and computer-assisted instruction. As educational technology evolves rapidly, most recently through adaptive learning platforms and AI-based tutoring, this area sits at an active research frontier concerning efficacy, equity of access, and classroom integration. It intersects with but remain

### Concepts
- **Educational material and media and educational technology in mathematics education** (97Uxx) — Studies the design, evaluation, and classroom integration of textbooks, manipulatives, software, and digital technologies for teaching mathematics.
